observations.recommendation:
Management require the GSO to prepare PCs for every class of PPE; and 

the Municipal Accountant to prepare PPELC, 

and facilitate reconciliation with the balances in the books of accounts vis-à-vis the PCs and RPCPPEs. 

Henceforth, require the Inventory Committee to immediately conduct the physical inventory of all properties and

submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the auditor as required under existing regulations.
Management, in coordination with offices concerned, they will revisit and implement COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balance in the financial statements.

Management terminate the contract with the contractor and 

pursue the remedies provided in the IRR of RA 9184 and 

undertake the necessary steps to continue the remaining work on the project.
Management ensure that all designated collecting officers are adequately bonded.
Management fill up the casual plantilla positions at the Chocolate Hills Complex.
Management require the Municipal Treasurer to process the registration of Carmen Municipal College with the BIR.
To effect the necessary adjustments in order to present a reliable and fairly presented balance of the Due to LGUs account in the Financial Statements.

Management to implement and impose the billing and collection of the new water rates as stipulated by the 2014 Revenue Code of the Municipality of Carmen to provide additional source of income to the LGU for the operation of the Waterworks System and to prevent wastage of water supply.

Management submit the PIF to the GSIS as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, properties and interest of the government with the GIF and an explanation on why the insurable properties, assets and interests of the LGU were not yet insured with the GSIS. 

Henceforth, Management abide by the deadline of submission of the PIF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2018-02 to insure against any insurable risk the LGU’s properties, assets and interests.

Management submit the necessary supporting documents to establish the propriety of the distribution of the supplies such as inventory of the supplies at year-end and distribution list signed by the recipients. 

Henceforth, require the accounting department to follow the relevant rules and regulations in recording the procurement of supplies for distribution.

Management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.   Otherwise, abolish these items from the Annual Budget and replace them with positions/items necessary for the proper discharge of the Municipality of its functions, targets and programs.
We recommended that Management deduct the liquidated damages of ₱89,765.24 from the final payment to the contractor in order to effect the deficiencies noted during the inspection.

Management should take proper action on government properties that are for disposal.  The General Services Officer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.  

In the meantime, the Municipal Accountant reclassify other unserviceable properties to Other Assets account while awaiting the disposal of such assets amounting to ₱3,949,410.61.

Management submit an explanation for the much-delayed implementation of the projects resulting to the incurrence of interest expense of ₱2,396,553.52 and financial charges amounting to ₱305,225.90 to the disadvantage to the government.

Conduct a substantive study on how to maximize the loan usage and hasten the project implementation.

Management conduct an immediate investigation on the circumstances relating to the missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents and determine the persons responsible.

Send immediately letters to Municipal Treasurer, Fulgencio V. Paña to produce the disbursement vouchers covering these issued and negotiated checks.

If the previous Municipal Treasurer fails to produce the missing vouchers and based on the results of the investigation, the amount of the disbursement vouchers should be added to his accountability and file appropriate charges against him and the responsible officials and staff.

Determine if the check payments are valid and proper, if so, require the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to validate the transactions and projects by retrieving the corresponding supporting documents, ALOBS and Accountant’s Advice and reconstruct said transactions for proper recording in the books.

Management require the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation and/or demand letters to individuals or the business establishment concerned.


For receivables from officials and employees arising from overpayment of claims or under remittance of collections, the Municipal Treasurer shall institute deductions on their subsequent claims and salaries in satisfaction of their indebtedness pursuant to the provision of Section 37 of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445.

1. Inventory Committee, whose members shall be temporarily relieved from their regular duties, to prioritize compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.
2. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain PPE Property cards and PPE ledger cards, respectively, to facilitate periodic reconciliation of PPE accounts.
observations.reason: Property cards were not yet prepared

observations.recommendation: 3. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records and effect the necessary adjustments to present the correct Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current account balance.
observations.reason: Discrepancies between records were still noted in CY 2024

observations.recommendation: 4. Municipal Accountant prioritizes tracing or identifying the cause of the negative balances starting this report specifically with the significant abnormal balances of the Cash in Bank Local Currency Current Account and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the accurate balances of the affected accounts in order to fairly present the financial statements.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report
Restated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: 11. The Municipal Accountant to initiate the review of the supporting documents (official receipts, memorandum of agreement, contracts, resolutions, and the like) and subsequently reclassify the transactions based on the identified source and purpose in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: 14. The Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials consider the application of a software/computer system to integrate the records of the Municipal Assessor’s Office and the MTO so as to provide a seamless transmission of data for the real property taxes due and ultimately the generation of an accurate balance for establishing the RPT/SET receivable accounts at the beginning of each year.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this Report
Restated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: 18. The Municipal Accountant demands or follows up in writing the submission of liquidation reports from those barangays with unliquidated financial assistance, especially those that were granted in CY 2022 and prior years.
observations.reason: Pending submission by Management of the demand letters received by the barangays

observations.recommendation:
19. Revisit the project proposals through its implementing offices to check the implementation of projects of these funds pursuant to the law governing said grant and/or the Memorandum of Agreement/contract with the respective donors, and accordingly require the proper liquidation of the amount granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the donor; and
20. Review the source documents of each subsidiary ledger and subsequently reclassify the transactions based on the identified nature/purpose.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: 22. Provide the LDRRMO with the additional mandatory plantilla position and likewise be filled in by qualified personnel in accordance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1; hence, continuity of service may be secured and the quality of implementation on disaster risk management-related programs be ensured.
observations.reason: Three of four plantilla positions were filled in.

observations.recommendation: 23. Thoroughly review the deductions of personnel pertaining to GSIS premiums/loan amortization and ensure that these are fully remitted to the GSIS within the prescribed period;
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: 25. PPEs recorded under SEF shall also be subjected to One-time Cleansing procedures to clean up the old balances. PPEs inventoried, other than those acquired after CY 2010 which are already completely accounted after tracing, shall be computed with accumulated depreciation and shall be considered fully depreciated.
observations.reason: One-time cleansing not yet initiated by Management

observations.recommendation: 26. Process the derecognition of dormant unliquidated cash advance pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 by filing a request for write-off to be supported by certified relevant documents validating the reason for non-collectability, including the proof of exhaustion of all remedies to demand for liquidation of the cash advance.
observations.reason: For verification

observations.recommendation: 27. Submit explanation on the noted deficiencies and provide an approved payroll/s for the submitted unsupported claims totaling ₱96,000.00 if there are any, otherwise, the returned claims shall be duly receipted and returned to the DSWD.
observations.reason: No document gathering/verification

observations.recommendation: 29. Submit a valid explanation as to the deficiencies noted to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: No valid explanation submitted

observations.recommendation: 30. Ensure that the reconciling items be fully supported and appropriately/timely recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Revised in AOM 2025-011 (2024)

observations.recommendation: 31. Prepare the subsidiary ledgers of these accounts showing the details of receivables. Furthermore, follow the foregoing procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 to ensure that dormant receivables/fund transfers in the books are analyzed, verified, reconciled, provided with impairment loss and written-off accordingly for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: No request for write-off submitted

observations.recommendation: 32. Obtain a formal estimate of recoverable service amount for the unserviceable items, compare with the carrying amount and make as basis in providing an allowance for impairment. Disclosures shall be provided in the financial statement in accordance with this standard. Management shall also consider disposal of the above items.
observations.reason: For verification

observations.recommendation: 33. Immediately fill in the statutory positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) dated April 4, 2014, instead of designating existing employees to comply with NDRRMC–DILG)–DBM–CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.reason: Three of four plantilla positions were filled in.

observations.recommendation: 34. Submit the MPOC Resolution approving the Municipality’s POP and the release of the CF duly approved and signed by at least two-thirds (2/3) of its members in accordance with the applicable provision of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01, otherwise a notice of suspension will be issued if warranted.
observations.reason: No document submitted

observations.recommendation: 35. Submit the pertinent supporting documents to the transactions involving ₱19,480,960.09, otherwise, subject transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Supporting documents were not submitted

observations.recommendation: 36. Correct the noted deficiencies and submit the required documents immediately for validation. Thereafter, all the officers involved in the processing of claims should see to it that the supporting papers are complete and proper, before these are paid.
observations.reason: Supporting documents were not submitted

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management continue
exhausting all possible action and remedies to collect the dormant receivables. Otherwise, facilitate the request for write off, if warranted, in accordance with the proper disposition of dormant accounts outlined in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 and submit relevant documents to the audit team for validation on or before the third (3rd) quarter of CY 2025.
observations.managementAction:
Perform the set out guidelines in the Accounting Office and sending of demand request to Sangguninag Bayan
Mayor's Office February 1, 2025 March 1, 2025
Accounting Office 2025 2025
Sangguninang Bayan Office 2025 2025
observations.reason: Expedite the procedure and sending of demand letter to accountable officers is ongoing. This is due to some officers having retired or resigned, and the current addresses of others being unknown. As of date of this report, 2 out of the 16 AOs were given demand letters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall require the BAC to fasten the creation of another account of the LGU to comply with the posting requirements on all its procurement. Notices of Award and to Proceed, and the approved contract be posted on the PhilGEPS website and website of the municipality, if any, within the reglementary period as required by 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat had already made the request for the creation of a new account to the PHILGEPS given that the current account had difficulties on posting the NTP and NOA to the PHILGEPS website. The BAC Secretariat is still waiting for the response of the PHILGEPS for the creation of a new account.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat still suffers the lack of proper posting in PHILGEPS thus resulting to non-compliance and non-submission of pertinent reports to GPPB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall require the BAC to fasten the creation of another account of the LGU to comply with the posting requirements on all its procurement. Notices of Award and to Proceed, and the approved contract be posted on the PhilGEPS website and website of the municipality, if any, within the reglementary period as required by 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC secretariat in charge of the PHILGEPS had already made the request for the change of account to the PHILGEPS given that the current account had difficulties on posting NTP and NOA to the PHILGEPS website.
observations.reason: No improvement had been received by the BAC Secretariat from the GPPB for the said request.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor set a timeline for securing copies of the corresponding certificate of title (OCT/TCT) and the Municipal Accountant to trace the acquisition cost of the unrecorded real properties for recognition in the books; otherwise, request an appraisal for determination of the fair market value as basis of recording and disclose the same in the notes to financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Already reconciled with MASSO
observations.reason: Securing OCT/TCT is still ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare a separate ICF for each PPE sub-major account group and clearly indicate the condition of each item, and the Property Unit prepare Property Cards and undertake collaborative procedures with the Accounting Unit to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records.
observations.reason: PC is still not prepared due to the retirement of the employee originally assigned to the task.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the Municipal Accountant, revert to the general fund, the unexpended Counterparts/Equity, for projects that had already been completed with a total amount of ₱439,188.68 to be used for priority PPAs of the Municipal Government and return to the DSWD the amount of ₱130,000.00 for the unimplemented project since it has been long outstanding and instruct the organization concerned to submit their new carefully planned project proposal once their federation has been re-established.
observations.managementAction: DSWD- ₱130,000 on-going implementation

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the GSO and the RHU to summarize all RIS/ issuances of inventory and prepare the SSMI/ RSPI and forward it to the Accounting Office for recording in the books. Henceforth, ensure the weekly preparation of the said reports in compliance with Section 121, Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs and COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reclassify the tangible items below the capitalization threshold to the Prior Period Adjustment account and close the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to record the donated lot following the standards provided in IPSAS 17 on the Measurement and Recognition of PPE and paragraph 55 of IPSAS 23 on the recognition of Transferred Assets with Condition. Below is the proposed accounting entry:
Land					xxx
Trust Liabilities				xxx
To record 20,000 square meters lot for the construction of water treatment and related facilities.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees informing them of the existence of the checks and facilitate replacement thereof.  We further recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the asset account and the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the GSO to prepare PCs for every class of PPE; and the Municipal Accountant to prepare PPELC, and facilitate reconciliation with the balances in the books of accounts vis-à-vis the PCs and RPCPPEs. Henceforth, require the Inventory Committee to immediately conduct the physical inventory of all properties and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the auditor as required under existing regulations.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the reversion of the unspent balance for CY 2018 special trust fund for LDRRMF to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommend and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to submit a Schedule of Dormant Accounts and conduct a thorough verification to confirm the existence and accuracy of these balances. Request for authority to write off these dormant accounts should be submitted to the COA in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed to :
discontinue the use of open dumpsite;
strengthen campaigns for recycling and proper garbage segregation; and
devise strategies for the efficient disposal of waste through existing disposal facilities.

We recommended that Management fill the vacant positions at the Chocolate Hills Complex to enhance its operations and ensure accountability. Additionally, develop internal control systems to improve inventory management, which will help prevent inventory losses, missed sales opportunities, and improve financial reporting.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
require the Municipal Engineer to explain in writing the discrepancies noted in the completion date per COC and Quarterly Report on the Government PPAs; and
ensure that for succeeding claims, photos attached to the disbursement vouchers showing the progression of the project from start to completion have time stamps.

We recommended and Management agreed to carefully evaluate requests for overtime services and ensure that only priority activities are authorized for cash remuneration. If so warranted, consider compensatory time-off, as long as it does not adversely impact the operations of the agency.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Disbursing Officer to refrain from paying salary differentials or expenses categorized as Personnel Services in the Petty Cash Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Treasurer,
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor, to initiate steps to expedite the
integration and build-up of the necessary database required for the system on
the real property taxes due to determine the correct amount of receivables and
to ultimately generate an accurate balance for the RPT/SET receivable
accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
1. Prepare separate subsidiary ledgers for each road component and LRN
Ledger Cards for each local road;
2. Present in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosures
pertaining to the road network system;

We also reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Treasurer agreed
to:
3. In coordination with the other members of the Inventory Committee,
prepare and maintain a complete Report on the Physical Count of the Road
Network System and the Road Map;
4. Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components; and

We further reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Engineer agreed
to:
5. Provide assistance to the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer in
determining the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
obtain and examine the source documents (official receipts, memorandum of
agreement, contracts, resolutions, and the like) in order to properly identify
each transaction and then reclassify it according to the identified source and
purpose in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
identify the cause of the negative balances and effect the necessary adjustments
to reflect the accurate balances of the affected accounts in order to fairly present
the financial statements.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to accomplish all the
required procedures for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts under COA
Circular 2020-006, particularly by conducting a complete physical count of all
items categorized as PPE and reconciliation of records between the Accounting
and Treasurer’s Office.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Assessor agreed to coordinate to reconcile the Certificates of Title
against the recorded lots and effect the necessary adjustments, if any, to present
the correct Land account balance.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the concerned
personnel to submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents of
the 41 checks debited from the current account of the Municipality to the
Accounting Office for recording.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately
adjust/correct the reconciling items once these are identified upon preparation
of BRS, and prepare the necessary journal entries for the cancellation of stale
checks.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert efforts to
account the composition and specification of balances carried over from the
previous years, as well as the item balances that need to be adjusted in the
books, and take appropriate actions thereon.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile its records
with that of the GSIS as soon as possible to determine and remit any unpaid
balance/s.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant and other
concerned officials agreed to:
1. Revisit the project proposals through its implementing offices to check the
implementation of projects charged to these funds pursuant to the law
governing said grant and/or the Memorandum of Agreement/contract with
the respective donors, and accordingly require the proper liquidation of the
amount granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the donor;
and
2. Review the source documents of each subsidiary ledger and subsequently
reclassify the transactions based on the identified nature/purpose.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management, through the Municipal
Accountant and other concerned personnel agreed to:
1. Reconcile the records and retrieve relevant documents to identify the source
agencies and purpose of the grant of financial assistance to finally process
for its liquidation;
2. Process for the return of the excess funds from completed projects for those
agencies requiring the return of the same in the Memorandum of
Agreement; and
3. Secure authority from the Cebu Provincial Government and other source
agencies to realign grants to other priority projects of the Municipality and
recognize income for the unexpended balances of the financial assistance.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
1. Establish a Disposal Committee to handle the disposal of the identified
unserviceable properties to prevent potential loss of income in accordance
with Section 79 of PD No. 1445; and
2. Ensure that the Municipal Treasurer prepares the IIRUP to facilitate the
derecognition after disposal of the unserviceable properties in the accounting
records.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the
concerned requesting offices, agreed to conduct periodic physical inventory
count of supplies and materials and consequently submit the consolidated
RPCIs, in prescribed form, not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year
for the first and second semester, respectively.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer, or requesting offices, agreed to maintain supplies ledger cards and
stock cards, respectively, to record the movement of all inventory items and as
basis for the reconciliation of inventories along with the monitoring of the RIS
and preparation of the SSMI.

Further, we recommended and Management agreed to ensure proper storage
for drugs and medicines and appropriate documentation for all inventory
movements, and initiate steps to comply and implement the policies and
procedures on the recording, monitoring and issuance of inventories as
embodied in the NGAS Manual for LGUs, especially on the recognition of
inventory upon purchase, and of expense for supplies issued.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Engineering Office
and the MPDC, as well as other concerned offices, agreed to provide a detailed
explanation for the non-implementation of the aforementioned projects, and
thereafter conduct detailed engineering investigations and pre-planning prior to
execution in order to achieve full implementation of the projects and timely
delivery of the desired services and benefits to the beneficiaries.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to issue an Executive Order
creating a GAD M and E Team in compliance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1 Instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to immediately submit the necessary construction-related documents to the Municipal Accounting Office upon project completion and record the completion of projects under the Engineering Offic (MEO) to the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) upon project completion for on-time recording.
2 Require the Municipal Accounting Office to draw a journal entry voucher on or before the second quarter of CY 2025 to correct the understated accumulated depreciation of all identified PPEs, thereby ensuring accurate balances for the affected accounts in the financial statements as of December 31, 2024.

We recommended that the Management continue exhausting all possible action and remedies to collect the dormant receivables. Otherwise, facilitate the request for write off, if warranted, in accordance with the proper disposition of dormant accounts outlined in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 and submit relevant documents to the audit team for validation on or before the third (3rd) quarter of CY 2025.

We recommended the Management to:
1. Require the Petty Cash Custodian to replenish the petty cash fund when disbursements reach 75% of the authorized amount, or as needed as stated in Section 1.2 of COA Circularr No. 2012-001.
2. Re-evaluate petty cash fund policy and request for an increase of established Petty Cash Fund to cover unforeseen petty expenses, if necessary.

We recommended the Management to:
1. Immediately assign dedicated personnel to manage and maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs).
2. Evaluate and streamline the existing accounting system to simplify the process of encoding individual inventory items. Explore options for batch processing or data import to reduce manual effort.
3. Provide comprehensive training to accounting staff on the proper use and importance of SLCs.
4. Conduct a workload assessment to determine if additional personnel are needed to effectively manage inventory and property responsibilities.
5. Develop and implement standardized inventory management procedures, including clear guidelines for record- keeping, monitoring, and reporting in compliance with The Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units – Volume 1, which outlines the Accounting Policies for Inventory Account.

We recommended that the Management take immediate action to:
1. Provide a suitable warehouse or secure storage facility to properly store and protect medical supplies in line with good storage practices.
2. Implement regular monitoring and inventory inspections to ensure proper storage conditions are maintained, mitigating the risk of supply spoilage and waste.
3. Conduct a workload assessment to determine if additional personnel are needed to effectively manage inventory, specifically assigned for the warehousing of drugs and medicines.
4. Ensure compliance with the DOH Supply Chain Management Service Warehouse Operations Manual to prevent financial loss and disrupt healthcare services within the community.

We recommended that Management the following:
1. Conduct comprehensive training for the Municipal Agriculture Office (MAGRO) staff on the proper preparation and maintenance of the Biological Assets Property Card (BAPC), in accordance with the Local Government Accounting Manual (LGAM) Volume I and Volume II;
2. Instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit promptly to the Accounting Office all necessary documents, such as Inventory Transfer Report or equivalent documents from donor agency for initial measurement of biological assets and regularly furnish the report on the changes in the fair value of the biological assets to ensure up-to-date fair value measurement of the biological assets for its subsequent recording.
3. Direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant for timely recording of all receipts of donations in kind and its subsequent utilization or disposal in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Management take immediate action to:
A. Direct the focal person in-charge of the Medical Assistance - Kabahagi Program to immediately return to the approved medical assistance procedure as stipulated in the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and conduct regular reviews of its medical assistance procedures to ensure continued compliance and effectiveness.
B. Conduct a thorough review all procurement of medical assistance, as they provide purely cash assistance, to correct any deviations, strengthen internal control and implement appropriate mode of procurement based on the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management the following:
1. Require the concerned personnel to submit duly corrected DTRs with complete and authenticated entries, or an official certification from their immediate supervisor or facility head, along with any supporting documents, to justify the previously disbursed HEA; and complete documentation as outlined in DBM-DOH Joint Administrative Order No. 2023-0001.
2. Direct the department head to thoroughly review and validate the submitted records to ensure compliance with documentation requirements and prevent potential audit disallowances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MAO to draw journal entry
voucher to record the RPT and SET Receivables amounting to ₱12,503,870.95, make
necessary adjustments as warranted and observe the prescribed accounting
procedures in recording RPT and SET receivable and deferred income accounts.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:
c. Through the Disposal and Appraisal Committee, observe the necessary
protocols in the disposal of unserviceable properties as stipulated in Joint
Circular No. 2024-1 of COA and DBM; and
d. For the Inventory Committee to come up with a PIP for a complete and thorough
physical inventory of the agency’s assets and ensure full implementation of one-time
cleansing of PPE.

In line of the foregoing, we recommended the following courses of actions:
a) To create/assign a Responsibility Center (RC) for their GAD Focal Point System
(GFPS) as mandated in COA Circular 2021-008; and
b) Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through full implementation of GAD-
related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds
were provided.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:
a) Thoroughly review the required attachments embodied in the procurement act
and COA Circulars mentioned above. Enhance the internal control system in
preparing the contract documents to ensure completeness and accuracy
thereof, facilitating effective audit and technical inspection by COA’s TAG; and
b) Ensure required documents are submitted, as stipulated in the procurement act
and relevant COA circulars.

In view of the foregoing observation, we recommended the following to the
Management:
a) Efficiently utilize the fund for the purpose designed, and assess whether the
funded PPAs are feasible for implementation; otherwise, consider
reprogramming to more suitable PPAs to encompass the four (4) thematic areas
of DRRM and integrate them into the AIP for approval by the local Sanggunian to
serve as a roadmap in LDRRMF utilization and improve the capacity of the
Municipality to respond in emergencies or disasters; and
b) Ensure that the planned PPAs under the LDRRMP are implemented effectively
and within the intended duration.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare
journal entry vouchers to recognize the transfer of the completed
infrastructure projects from the Construction in Progress account to its
appropriate PPE accounts as follows:
Flood Control System ₱7,511,383.40
Other Infrastructure Assets 6,999,257.00
Buildings 1,496,203.96
Road Networks 1,998,632.00
Other Structures 499,999.28
Const in Progress-Infrastructure Assets ₱17,005,477.32
Const in Progress-Bldgs & Other Strucures 1,499,998.32
To recognize completed projects
under the Construction in Progress
accounts.

4.21 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Revisit the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units
and ensure that appropriate accounts are used in the recording of
transactions in the books of the Municipality, as prescribed in
Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015;
b. Trace all entries made to Due from Other Funds and Due to Other
Funds from General Fund to Other Funds and vice versa and prepare
adjusting journal entries in June 2025 to recognize the reciprocal
accounts to the affected Fund in addition to the following adjusting
journal entries:
Advances for Payroll ₱ 726,247.55
Due from Other Funds ₱ 726,247.55
To correct the double-entry for payment of expenditures by the GF
due from other funds
Government Equity ₱7,802,074.20
Due to Other Funds ₱7,802,074.20
To correct the misclassification of accounts
c. Reconcile the ending balances of the reciprocal accounts monthly to
ensure the accuracy and fairness of records and presentation to the
Statement of Financial Position.

5.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit in CY 2025
the necessary documents required by the Provincial Government of
Agusan del Norte to collect the over remittance of ₱247,282.81; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to examine the Abstract of Real
Property Tax to verify the accuracy of the shares to be remitted by
the Municipality to the provincial government.

7.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the AAO or the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement
the deduction of the required monthly loan amortization from the
payroll of the employees with delinquent accounts;
b. Direct the AAO and the Municipal Accountant to fast track the
reconciliation of service records from the GSIS by June 2025, for all
employees still connected with the LGU and to pay immediately the
unremitted amounts, if any; and
c. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit on time the withheld
premiums and loan amortizations of employees to the GSIS.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
adjusting entries to correct the misclassified asset accounts with their
corresponding accumulated depreciation.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
adjusting entries to record the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated
balance of the LDRRMF for CYs 2023 and 2024, amounting to
₱1,231,708.50 and ₱2,398,004.73, respectively, and record the
unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in future years in the pertinent
calendar year of incurrence.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
adjusting journal entry to reverse the derecognition of the financial
assistance amounting to ₱300,000.00 that was inadvertently closed and
require the liquidation report from Brgy. Halian duly received by COA
before the same can be closed.

4.9 We recommended that the:
4.9.1 Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the
recognition of prior period income in the current year, and
request information/data/report from the MT for year-end
undeposited collections which will only be forwarded the
following year; and
4.9.2 Municipal Treasurer report all collections for the year even
including those due for deposit in the ensuing year.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to undertake
necessary procedures to revert to the unappropriated surplus of the
General Fund, the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable in the amount of
₱107,652.55, which has been outstanding for more than seven years and
have no claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not
covered by perfected contracts.

6.6 We recommended that the:
6.6.1 GSIS AAO and Remitting Officer investigate
changes/discrepancies in the GSIS online system and
communicate promptly with the GSIS personnel for easy
troubleshooting if problems exist; and
6.6.2 Municipal Accountant and Payroll In-charge update the rate of
mandatory contributions for Pag-IBIG.

7.7 We recommended that the:
7.7.1 Municipal Budget Officer prepare the SAAOB of continuing
appropriations of the 20 percent DF, trace and establish the
actual unaccounted balance in coordination with the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, and submit a copy to the
Municipal Mayor, copy furnished COA; and
7.7.2 MPDO propose the reversion of these balances after viable
developmental projects have been determined with the
approval of the Municipal Development Council (MDC).

We recommended that the HRMO spearhead the review and assessment
of the Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern taking into
consideration the absence of recruitment policy, existing number of
manpower complement, and compliance with the daily minimum wage
rate for Region XIII and submit the same to the Municipal Mayor so that
appropriate action can be taken to maximize the potential of the human
resources of the LGU.

We recommended and the MDRRM Officer agreed to refrain from using
the LDRRMF for payment of administrative costs to ensure that only
disaster risk reduction and management related activities are charged
against the said fund.

10.14 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
10.14.1 Local Chief Executive (1) issue a directive to all concerned units,
esspecially the LDRRM Officer, to refrain from using the LDRRM
Fund for anything other than its intended purpose; (2) LDRRM
Officer release stockpiled/prepositioned items to eligible
beneficiaries in the event of a disaster or are already stored for a
long time but before their expiration dates, supported with proper
documentation; and (3) GSO submit the lacking distribution list of the
recipients with the corresponding number of kilos or bags received.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) refrain from
utilizing the GAD Fund for expenses not allowed to be charged, unless
justified as clearly addressing a specific gender issue, to maximize the
utilization of the GAD budget to achieve the objective of the government
regarding the institutionalization of GAD in the community.

12.3 We recommended that the Local School Board thru the Focal Person:
a. Document the service of copies of the approved SEF Budget to the
Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal
Treasurer within seven (7) days of its approval;
b. Submit to the DepEd Central Office through the appropriate
channels, the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization or the
SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1;
c. Furnish the Local Sanggunian of Del Carmen and the Regional
Offices of the DBM and DILG the same reports in No. 2 within the
prescribed period; and
d. Post on the LGU's website and/or in at least three (3) designated
conspicuous public places consistent with the deadline of the DILG
Full Disclosure Policy.

We recommended and the Management agreed to act on the pending
requests of the Lessee and pursue the collection of unpaid rentals by
exhausting all administrative and legal remedies.

14.7 We recommended that the:
14.7.1 Municipal Treasurer monitor the status of delivery of the
Notices of Delinquency so that further action can be made to
effect the available remedies for collection of RPT and SET; and
14.7.2 Municipal Assessor to update the data on their system in order
to provide reliable information.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a committee to
review the adherence of the operation of the Numancia Mall and Public
Terminal with the Municipal Revenue Code of 2020, recommend
appropriate corrective actions to ensure its propriety and retrieval of
Contract/MOA for submission to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Liquidating Officer immediately turn-over all
collections received to the Municipal Treasurer for review and validation
of the official receipts to ascertain the accuracy and completeness of
the amounts received for deposit in compliance with the above
guidelines.

We recommended and the Disposal Committee agreed to fast track the
disposal of the unserviceable properties and submit all the documents
to the Municipal Accountant after the disposal, for proper recording in the
books.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat submit immediately the
aforementioned contracts and, henceforth, comply with the prescribed
period of submission, which is five working days from the execution of
contracts.

19.18 We recommended that the:
19.18.1 Municipal Engineer:
a. prepare a catch-up plan to hasten the completion of the
projects;
b. refrain from recommending approval of suspensions and
time extensions with reasons not enumerated in Items 10 and
11 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of Republic Act (RA)
No. 9184;
c. recommend imposition of liquidated damages;
d. conduct a cost-benefit analysis of whether to terminate the
contract and recommend the same, if warranted; and
19.18.2 Local Chief Executive act on the recommendation of the
Municipal Engineer to impose liquidated damages and/or
terminate the contract.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a. Designate a personnel/committee to craft internal rules on the limits
of reimbursements; and
b. Issue a directive to all Implementing Units to include in the Annual
Procurement Plan, their respective quarterly requirements for
equipment and common-use supplies including activities that
require the provision for food such as seminars and conferences so
that these can be procured.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that the funds
received for DRRM-related operations and activities, whether from other
LGUs or National Government agencies, be recorded as Trust Liability-DRRM.
Also agreed to prepare an Adjusting Journal Entry (AJE) to recognize the
unspent balance of assistance received from the City of Makati as Trust
Liability-DRRM with the following suggested AJE:
Due to LGUs ₱66,170.00
Trust Liability-DRRM ₱66,170.00

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed, that accounts
payable be recorded only when the goods and services covered by the ORS
have already been delivered/rendered and supported with valid
documentation. For future transactions, strictly adhere to Section 46 of
PD No. 1177 and Section 141, Chapter 3, NGAS for LGUs, Volume III.

Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following accounting entries to close the Due to Other NGAs account:

Trust Fund Books

	Construction In Progress		xxx
	Advances to Contractors		xxx

	Government Equity xxx

Municipal Accountant record all subsequent procurements of construction materials for distribution as “Construction Materials for distribution” and credit the same only upon issuance thereof following the submission of the General Services Office of the consolidated monthly RIS and SSMI.

Necessary adjusting journal entries be prepared to correct the balances of affected accounts.

Management require the Municipal Accountant to recompute the depreciation of the components of road network system without providing 5% residual value and 

make the necessary entries to correct the balances of affected accounts. Henceforth, discontinue the practice of providing for 5% residual value in the computation of depreciation expense of Road Networks account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
Management return the unutilized PDAF to the Bureau of the Treasury pursuant to Supreme Court Decision dated November 19, 2013.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the disbursement voucher to return SEF funds previously transferred to TF- LDRRMF; and

Discontinue the practice of providing for appropriations of LDRRM in the SEF funds.
Management ensure that all the complete set of bidding documents are prepared and issued to the bidders in the conduct of procurement thru competitive bidding.

Management require the various Department Heads to refrain from collecting overtime pay in accordance with CSC-DBM JC No. 2 s. 2015, dated November 25, 2015 on the payment of authorized overtime services of government personnel.

Management require the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the necessary procedures in order to facilitate the withdrawal of said funds for transfer to other Authorized Government Depository Banks of the Municipality and ensure that the funds are restored and readily available for use.

Management provide immediate attention and action to the monitoring and reconciliation of these accounts and 

evaluate the need and the capacity of the Municipality to hire additional manpower complement skilled in accounting preferably Certified Public Accountants.

Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of ₱100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.

5. The Municipal Accountant records the adjustments to correct the inclusion of the General Fund LDRRMF-CO for the CO balance in the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account as follows:
Trust Fund Books

Cr. Cash in Bank ₱326,789.37

Cr. Prior Period Adjustment ₱326,789.37
To revert the CYs 2022-2023 unutilized LDRRMF-CO balance to General Fund

12. Obtain revised/renewed performance and/or warranty surety bonds from the contractor, with the appropriate validity period; and
13. Issue/Cause the issuance of Certificates of Final Acceptance only after the defects liability period or one year from the completion date.

21. The MDRRM Officer submits the revised/supplemental LDRRMF IP and AIP for 2023 which includes the PPAs charged to the unexpended balances of prior years’ LDRRMF to substantiate the expenditures charged thereof pursuant to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

24. Reconcile its records pertaining to the Due to PhilHealth account and verify if remittances had been previously made for the above outstanding balance. Also, remit the same if such dues were found to have not been paid to the PHIC.

28. Require the ineligible beneficiaries to refund the cash aid they received and ensure that the total amount of ₱624,000.00 are collected and returned to DSWD. We enjoined the responsible officials to initiate the collection efforts for the refund by sending demand letters.
observations.reason: Pending submission by the barangays was addressed; collection efforts initiated

1. Require the Petty Cash Custodian to replenish the petty cash fund when disbursements reach 75% of the authorized amount, or as needed as stated in Section 1.2 of COA Circularr No. 2012-001.
2. Re-evaluate petty cash fund policy and request for an increase of established Petty Cash Fund to cover unforeseen petty expenses, if necessary.

2. Re-evaluate petty cash fund policy and request for an increase of established Petty Cash Fund to cover unforeseen petty expenses, if necessary.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:
For Municipal Accounting Office:
1. Immediately assign dedicated personnel to manage and maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs).
2. Evaluate and streamline the existing accounting system to simplify the process of encoding individual inventory items. Explore options for batch processing or data import to reduce manual effort.
3. Provide comprehensive training to accounting staff on the proper use and importance of SLCs.
For General Services Office:
4. Conduct a workload assessment to determine if additional personnel are needed to effectively manage inventory and property responsibilities.
5. Develop and implement standardized inventory management procedures, including clear guidelines for record- keeping, monitoring, and reporting in compliance with The Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units – Volume 1, which outlines the Accounting Policies for Inventory Account.
6. Provide thorough training to all GSO personnel on inventory management practices and procedures.
7. GSO and the Municipal Accounting Office should work together that their system and records are reconciled and up to date.
observations.managementAction:
Conduct review and assessment of the existing inventory system and determine the need to augment personnel to the GSO.
Seek assistance from IT Department, the LGU IT and Accounting Department for the possibility to create an electronic inventory control system.
Provide training to GSO and Accounting Office personnel.
Assessment of the warehouse/stockroom, as supported by their Contracts of Service.
Improve the inventory system of the LGU and inform each personnel involved.
Provide GSO comprehensive training to GSO personnel for inventory handling procedures.
Conduct regular reconciliation of both offices.
observations.reason:
A personnel is already assigned to manage and maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards. Order No. 01 series of 2025 was issued to assign personnel to manage and maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs), effective March 17, 2025.
Due to complexity of the system, existing IT provider has no technical knowledge to create such system. The Municipality opted to seek a third-party system provider for an inventory system.
Training was provided to focal persons in-charge with inventory management. In-house training on Inventory Supply Management for LGU Carmen was conducted by the Municipality on October 21, 2025, and was attended by focal persons from the GSO and Accounting Office.
Two personnel were assigned to manage the inventory and property of the Municipality in the warehouse/stockroom, as supported by their Contracts of Service.
The GSO submits the RSMI to the Accounting Office monthly, leading to the creation of JEVs for necessary adjustments.
Interview with the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer confirmed that they are still reconciling records due to the variety of low-value supplies and materials, which are voluminous in number.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management take immediate action to:
1. Provide a suitable warehouse or secure storage facility to properly store and protect medical supplies in line with good storage practices.
2. Implement regular monitoring and inventory inspections to ensure proper storage conditions are maintained, mitigating the risk of supply spoilage and waste.
3. Conduct a workload assessment to determine if additional personnel are needed to effectively manage inventory, specifically assigned for the warehousing of drugs and medicines.
4. Ensure compliance with the DOH Supply Chain Management Service Warehouse Operations Manual to prevent financial loss and disrupt healthcare services within the community.
observations.managementAction:
Provide suitable warehouse for drugs and medicines.
Conduct regular monitoring of drugs and medicines and inventory monitoring of drugs and medicines and inventory or when needs arise.
Conduct workload assessment in the GSO for effective management of inventories.
Ensure the GSO compliance of DOH Supply Chain Management Service Warehouse Operations Manual.
observations.reason:
Dedicated storage area within the warehouse has been allocated for drugs and medicines, in accordance with the DOH Operations Manual.
Daily recording of in and out inflows and outflows are maintained using supplies ledger cards, and corresponding RSMIs are submitted to the Accounting Office to record necessary adjustments. Regular inspection by the MHO to check storage condition.
Two additional personnel were assigned to the warehouse for proper monitring of inventory.
Regular inspection by the MHO to check storage condition.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management the following:
1. Conduct comprehensive training for the Municipal Agriculture Office (MAGRO) staff on the proper preparation and maintenance of the Biological Assets Property Card (BAPC), in accordance with the Local Government Accounting Manual (LGAM) Volume I and Volume II;
2. Instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit promptly to the Accounting Office all necessary documents, such as Inventory Transfer Report or equivalent documents from donor agency for initial measurement of biological assets and regularly furnish the report on the changes in the fair value of the biological assets to ensure up-to-date fair value measurement of the biological assets for its subsequent recording.
3. Direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant for timely recording of all receipts of donations in kind and its subsequent utilization or disposal in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Conduct consultative dialogue with MAGRO and Biological Asset Accounting Office.
Submit on a regular basis any status report of all biological assets.
Submit all necessary documents for all donated Biological Assets for recording.
observations.reason:
Training was provided to focal persons in-charge with Biological Asset inventory management. In-house training on Inventory of Biological Asset for LGU Carmen was conducted by the Municipality on October 21, 2025, and was attended by focal persons from the MAGRO, GSO and Accounting Office.
Quartely submission of reports by the MAGRO to the Accounting office for regular recording of the biological assets.
Regular recording and updating of account upon receipt of report from MAGRO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management take immediate action to:
A. Direct the focal person in-charge of the Medical Assistance - Kabahagi Program to immediately return to the approved medical assistance procedure as stipulated in the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and conduct regular reviews of its medical assistance procedures to ensure continued compliance and effectiveness.
B. Conduct a thorough review all procurement of drugs and medicines to correct any deviations, strengthen internal control and implement appropriate mode of procurement based on the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction:
Review the existing guidelines and comply what are stipulated as mandated by the Sangguniang Bayan extending assistance.
Strictly implement the Kabahagi cash basis of assistance and follow the procedure in the existing guidelines of Donations.
observations.reason:
Reinstate the approved medical assistance procedure as stipulated in Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 294, dated November 16, 2022.
Interview with the focal person in charge confirmed that the Kabahagi program reverted to its approved medical assistance procedure, as stipulated in Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 294, dated November 16, 2022.
Review of sample transactions confirmed the receipt of cash assistance as originally stipulated in the SB Resolution (JEV No. 5463 dated 8/29/2025).
Kabahagi program's medical assistance provides purely cash assistance to recipients, no drugs or medicines were procured for its implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management the following:
1. Require the concerned personnel to submit duly corrected DTRs with complete and authenticated entries, or an official certification from their immediate supervisor or facility head, along with any supporting documents, to justify the previously disbursed HEA; and complete documentation as outlined in DBM-DOH Joint Administrative Order No. 2023-0001.
2. Directed the department head to thoroughly review and validate the submitted records to ensure compliance with documentation requirements and prevent potential audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction:
Submit certification from previous MHO and HR Office.
Review submitted documents for claim and ensure related documentation for all claims related to HEA.
observations.reason:
Sudmitted certification of work attendance by the former Municipal Health Officer was submitted, evidencing the work attendance of the personnel who were recipients of HEA.
Review of sample transactions confirmed complete documentation as outlined in DBM-DOH Joint Administrative Order No. 2023-0001 (JEV No. 5769 dated 9/12/2025 and JEV No. 0518 dated 11/18/2025).
