We recommended Drew adjusting entries to recognize the corresponding depreciation of the PPE from the date of completion and properly adjust the previous ones to reflect the correct carrying balances.
Depreciation shall be for one month if the PPE is available for use on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is available for use after the 15th of the month.
observations.managementAction: the Municipal Accountant immediately to record the corresponding understated depreciation expenses and adjust the previous ones to reflect the correct carrying balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant consider the prescribed limitation of two percent (2%) of allocation for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses to the Office of the Municipal Mayor only. Also, obliges the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the obligated amount for the EME account is within the approved Budget. Moreover, requires the Municipal Accountant to properly discern the usage of account title, especially in recording the EME disbursements, and make a necessary adjustment to the affected accounts to fairly present the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Drew adjusting journal entry and made internal controls to prevent the recurrence of classifying the transactions as Municipal EME.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Drew adjusting entries to reclassify the Construction in Progress account of ₱899,500.00 to Buildings account to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: the Engineering and Accounting Office reconcile their respective records on ongoing construction projects and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts by reclassifying the Construction in Progress account of ₱899,500.00 to Buildings account to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the direct personnel in charge of the preparation and submission of the required supporting documents for the cash advance for Confidential Funds to submit within seven (7) days after release of check, and to support it with complete documentations.
observations.managementAction: The liaison personnel in charge had already made good and submission of the required documents for the cash advance for Confidential Funds to submit within seven (7) days after release of check, and to support it with complete documentations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and accountable officers exhaust all possible actions to prioritize the liquidation of fund transfers received from NGAs, GOCCs, and private institutions. Return any unused balance to the Source Agencies.
observations.managementAction: Drew adjusting entries and the management had already assigned personnel under the Municipal Administrator’s Office to properly monitor fund transfers received from NGAs, other LGUS, GOCCs, and private institutions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that realignment/reprogramming is always made supported by an appropriation ordinance and strictly adhere to Section 336 of RA 7160 to ensure the validity and legality of the newly reprogrammed PPAs and their implementation.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and Municipal Management had already acknowledged the right of the MPDO to control the disbursements of the 20% Development Fund for him to have a better understanding of how the fund is being spent and the monitoring of the funds was returned in the hands of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator thus giving more controls in the implementation and monitoring on the utilization of the funds and completion of projects in accordance with the contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management acknowledge the right of the MPDO to control the disbursements of the 20% Development Fund for him to have a better understanding of how the fund is being spent and that the MPDO take the necessary actions to ensure that the PPAs are being fully utilized and monitored to address issues that will be encountered in the project implementation and completion.
observations.managementAction: The management had already communicated and the MPDO had been assigned personnel under the Municipal Administrator’s Office to properly monitor the disbursements of the 20% Development Fund and the monitoring of the funds was returned in the hands of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator thus giving more controls in the implementation and monitoring on the utilization of the funds and completion of projects in accordance with the contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management allow to use of one of the vehicles of the Municipality as a common service for the day-to-day operations or disaster response and rescue operations in conformity with Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01-2020 dated November 4, 2020 and if warranted reclassify the recording of the said vehicle from Motor Vehicles to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment. Otherwise, refund or replenish the 20% DF for the cost of the subject vehicle, the appropriations for which shall be taken from the General Fund. Moreover, the LGU officials strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1-2020 of the DBM, DOF, and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO and Municipal Accounting Office already stopped using the modified 4x4 pick-up vehicle for purposes other than disaster-related activities. Moreover, the Accounting Office had already made adjustment in his books by reclassifying the modified pick-up from Motor Vehicles to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Budget Officer not to charge expenses without proper monitoring and strictly utilize the 5% LDRRMF only for disaster risk management activities as provided in the above guidelines to ensure that the objectives outlined in the allocation of the fund are achieved through proper usage thereof.
observations.managementAction: Both offices had already applied major adjustments to prevent the recurrence of the findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer not to release stockpiling/prepositioning items to ineligible beneficiaries and not to release such items except during emergency cases, ensure that distribution be properly documented and provide the complete distribution list of recipients; also Local Chief Executive issue directives and ensure internal controls.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO already stopped using the stockpile for non-disaster purposes. The Municipal Accounting Office made adjustments to comply with the recommendations. The LDRRMO coordinated with GSO and MSWDO to prepare protocols in the distribution of goods nearing their expiration dates as to not waste the stockpiles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management not to provide Representation and Transportation Allowance (RATA), and provide caution in the disbursement of funds by adhering to rules and regulations to avoid this kind of transaction.
observations.managementAction: The matter is already discussed to the Sangguniang Bayan and the Vice Mayor Renato L. Dulguime had promised to stop the provision of meals and snacks during sessions. The accounting office also to stop allowing this kind of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer limit the allocation for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses to the Office of the Municipal Mayor and comply with the two percent (2%) statutory limitation for EME based on the actual collections of RPT in the next preceding calendar year in the preparation of annual budget. Moreover, requires the Municipal Accountant to strengthen the control on the EME. Furthermore, the accounting office had already made adjustment to prevent the recurrence of the audit findings.
observations.managementAction: Both the budget and accounting office had already made adjustments to prevent the recurrence of the audit findings. Moreover, the accounting office refrained from using the 2% statutory limitation for EME. The disbursements of funds by office further disapproving payments charged against the EME in excess of the two percent (2%) limitation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the committee vigorously pursue the collection of all unpaid rentals by pursuing the delinquent account through arranging challenging installment payments, imposing penalties, and persistent demand letters.
observations.managementAction: The general services office along with the Municipal Administrator’s Office had already applied actions to prioritize the liquidation of fund transfers received from NGAs and other institutions and the Local Chief Executive is already looking into other remedies such cancelling the remaining contract through PPP with the delinquent lessee; suggestion to pursue through legal remedies have already in place.
observations.reason: Despite the constant reminder of the treasury Office, there’s still no response coming from the delinquent lessee. On top of that, no legal action had been taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO to retrieve the records of the properties and Tag/Stickers on all properties for easier cross-referencing with the AREs and proper identification of the properties; and attach Property Tags to the old and newly acquired PPEs before these are distributed to the intended end-users to establish control on the use of the government property and equipment.
observations.managementAction: The general services office along with the Property Committee conducted a second round of inspection to determine those properties which still not have tag/ sticker and renew the tag/ sticker for those that have already faded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer require the contractor to submit monthly Statement of Work Accomplished and monitor that implementation of the project is in accordance with the timelines set in the contract; impose appropriate sanctions and consider termination if necessary; and the Municipal Administrator to coordinate actions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal management already conducted meetings and the contractor promised to finish the projects and submitted catch-up plans. The Municipal Engineer is to monitor and require the contractor to submit a Statement of Work Accomplished monthly and ensure that implementation of the project is in accordance with the timelines set in the contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Disposal Committee prepare the pertinent documents to facilitate an appraisal review of the unserviceable properties by the COA, including an Appraisal Report of unserviceable properties for disposal, and other related documents such as photographs of unserviceable properties for disposal; And file the application for its disposal with the COA, pursuant to COA Circular 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The disposal committee along with the Inventory and Property Committee conducted a second round of inspection to determine those properties which still not have tag/ sticker and renew the tag/ sticker for those that have already faded. They planned to dispose said unserviceable properties in August or September of the same year. A meeting had been conducted and plans to dispose the properties were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended JEV for that the Municipal Accountant adjust the misstatement of Depreciation Expense-Road Networks, Accumulated Depreciation-Road Networks and Government Equity by account and be compliant with the latest provisions regarding the computation of depreciation for this account.
observations.managementAction: adjustment drawn and booked

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare journal entries to correct the balances of each affected liability account as follows: Debit: Trust Liabilities ₱1,152,977.87 ₱135,568.81; Credit: Due to National Government Agencies ₱1,152,977.87; Due to LGUs ₱135,568.81.
observations.managementAction: adjustment drawn and booked

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant stop charging prior period expenses to the current appropriation and that the Municipal Accountant accrue December water, internet subscription, electricity, other professional services, cable, satellite, and radio expenses at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist implement the project and thereafter submit the liquidation report to the source agency so that the Municipal Government can request additional funding from the DILEEP.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMO stop charging ineligible expenses to the LDRRMF and strictly adhere to the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer fully implement, or the very least commence the implementation of these five projects and the Local Development Coordinator make a written representation to the Local Development Council that the municipality's priority development projects should be procurement and implementation-ready.
observations.managementAction: Continuing appropriations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board ensure that the SEF Supplemental Budget is signed by the appropriate signatories to adhere to the provisions of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: The SEF annual budget 2024 & Supplemental were signed by the appropriate signatories

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit documents that would substantiate or support the gender issues identified for these subject activities to ensure that the expenditures are eligible to be charged to GAD.
observations.managementAction: PRAISE appropriations are no longer included in GAD appropriations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC require the winning bidders to secure the Certification from the Insurance Commission for every Performance Bond in the form of a Surety Bond posted for the awarded projects.
observations.managementAction: Certification secured and attached

observations.recommendation: We recommended JMC No. 2014 - 01 to provide complete budgetary support to the MDRRMO under the General Fund and refrain from using the LDRRMF for payment of administrative costs of the office.
observations.managementAction: Observed

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Management agreed to review the provisions of every Memorandum of Agreement they enter into with any government agency and strictly adhere to the prescribed period provided. Moreover, fast-track the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented. Project near completion

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare the IIRUP and initiate proper disposal to avoid further deterioration of the properties. Proper segregation must be made of these properties that are still saleable and which can be subjected to destruction.
observations.managementAction: Assets already disposed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for the LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the two (2) NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Circular (JMC) 2014-1 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO position is not mandatory for 5th income class municipality, hence, the creation of the two (2) positions. Positions of LDRRMO II and LDRRMO I were created and filled up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensures strict compliance with the minimum standards and by operating means requirements of a controlled dump in accordance with Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: Negotiations with LBDA are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management coordinate with offices concerned, revisit COA Circular No. 2020-006 and comply with all the guidelines and procedures provided therein.

We recommended that Management implement and impose the billing and collection of the new water rates as stipulated by the 2014 Revenue Code of the Municipality of Carmen to provide additional source of income to the LGU for the operation of the Waterworks System and to prevent wastage of water supply.

We recommended that Management submit the PIF to the GSIS as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, properties and interest of the government with the GIF and an explanation on why the insurable properties, assets and interests of the LGU were not yet insured with the GSIS. 

Henceforth, it is recommended that Management abide by the deadline of submission of the PIF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2018-02 to insure against any insurable risk the LGU’s properties, assets and interests.

We recommended that Management submit the necessary supporting documents to establish the propriety of the distribution of the supplies such as inventory of the supplies at year-end and distribution list signed by the recipients. 

Henceforth, require the accounting department to follow the relevant rules and regulations in recording the procurement of supplies for distribution.

We recommended that Management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.   Otherwise, abolish these items from the Annual Budget and replace them with positions/items necessary for the proper discharge of the Municipality of its functions, targets and programs.

We recommend that management provide immediate attention and action to the monitoring and reconciliation of these accounts and at the same time evaluate the need and the capacity of the Municipality to hire additional manpower complement skilled in accounting preferably Certified Public Accountants.
We recommended Management to return the unexpended balances of PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury and secure official receipt thereof.

We recommended that Management deduct the liquidated damages of ₱89,765.24 from the final payment to the contractor in order to effect the deficiencies noted during the inspection. 

Likewise, we recommended that henceforth, Management ensure the proper implementation and timely execution of the terms of the approved project contract so that the target completion date will be attained which leads to the opportune availment of the constituents of the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of ₱100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.

Henceforth, fund transfers to NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts of the LGU in accordance with the same COA Circular in order to afford proper review and evaluation of the utilization of the fund release.

We recommended that Management should take proper action on government properties that are for disposal.  The General Services Officer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.  

In the meantime, the Municipal Accountant should reclassify other unserviceable properties to Other Assets account while awaiting the disposal of such assets amounting to ₱3,949,410.61.

We recommended that  Management:

Submit an explanation for the much-delayed implementation of the projects resulting to the incurrence of interest expense of ₱2,396,553.52 and financial charges amounting to ₱305,225.90 to the disadvantage to the government.

Conduct a substantive study on how to maximize the loan usage and hasten the project implementation.

We recommended that management henceforth:
Conduct an immediate investigation on the circumstances relating to the missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents and determine the persons responsible.

Send immediately letters to Municipal Treasurer, Fulgencio V. Paña to produce the disbursement vouchers covering these issued and negotiated checks.

If the previous Municipal Treasurer fails to produce the missing vouchers and based on the results of the investigation, the amount of the disbursement vouchers should be added to his accountability and file appropriate charges against him and the responsible officials and staff.

Determine if the check payments are valid and proper, if so, require the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to validate the transactions and projects by retrieving the corresponding supporting documents, ALOBS and Accountant’s Advice and reconstruct said transactions for proper recording in the books.

We recommended that management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation and/or demand letters to individuals or the business establishment concerned.

For receivables from officials and employees arising from overpayment of claims or under remittance of collections, the Municipal Treasurer shall institute deductions on their subsequent claims and salaries in satisfaction of their indebtedness pursuant to the provision of Section 37 of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445.

Consider the application of a software/computer system to integrate the records of the Municipal Assessor’s Office and the MTO so as to provide a seamless transmission of data for the real property taxes due and ultimately the generation of an accurate balance for establishing the RPT/SET receivable accounts at the beginning of each year.

Provide the LDRRMO with the additional mandatory plantilla position and likewise be filled in by qualified personnel in accordance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to ensure continuity of service along with the efficient and effective implementation of disaster risk management programs.

Thoroughly review the deductions of personnel pertaining to GSIS premiums/loan amortization and ensure that these are fully remitted to the GSIS within the prescribed period;


Review/analyze carefully the journal entries made before posting the same in the SLs to ensure accurate recording of transactions and reliable balances of the Due to GSIS account.

Reconcile its records pertaining to the Due to PhilHealth account and verify if remittances had been previously made for the above outstanding balance. Also, remit the same if such dues were found to have not been paid to the PHIC.

Process the derecognition of dormant unliquidated cash advance pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 by filing a request for write-off to be supported by certified relevant documents validating the reason for non-collectability, including the proof of exhaustion of all remedies to demand for liquidation of the cash advance.

Submit explanation on the noted deficiencies and provide an approved payroll/s for the unsupported claims totaling ₱96,000.00 if there are any, otherwise, the returned claims shall be duly receipted and returned to the DSWD.

Require the ineligible beneficiaries to refund the cash aid they received and ensure that the total amount of ₱624,000.00 are collected and returned to DSWD. We enjoined the responsible officials to initiate the collection efforts for the refund by sending demand letters.
Submit a valid explanation as to the deficiencies noted to avoid disallowance in audit.
Ensure that the reconciling items be fully supported and appropriately/timely recorded in the books of accounts.

Prepare the subsidiary ledgers of these accounts showing the details of receivables. Furthermore, follow the foregoing procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 to ensure that dormant receivables/fund transfers in the books are analyzed, verified, reconciled, provided with impairment loss and written-off accordingly for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

Obtain a formal estimate of recoverable service amount for the unserviceable items, compare with the carrying amount and make as basis in providing an allowance for impairment. Disclosures shall be provided in the financial statement in accordance with this standard. Management shall also consider disposal of the above items.

Immediately fill in the statutory positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) dated April 4, 2014, instead of designating existing employees to comply with NDRRMC–DILG)–DBM–CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

Submit the MPOC Resolution approving the Municipality’s POP and the release of the CF duly approved and signed by at least two-thirds (2/3) of its members in accordance with the applicable provision of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01, otherwise a notice of suspension will be issued if warranted.

Correct the noted deficiencies and submit the required documents immediately for validation. Thereafter, all the officers involved in the processing of claims should see to it that the supporting papers are complete and proper, before these are paid.

We recommended the LDRRM Officer not to release stockpiling/prepositioning items to ineligible beneficiaries and remind them that such items will only be released during emergency cases or already stored for a long time but before their expiration dates, supported with proper documentation. Also, refrain from using the LDRRM Fund from not its intended purpose. Moreover, submit a justification letter as to the distribution of the goods as well as the complete list of the recipients with the corresponding number of kilos or bags received.
observations.managementAction: Refrain from distributing welfare goods (stockpiled goods) to purposes other than those allowed in Section 2 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 and Section 5.2 of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.reason: Non-submission of the lacking Distribution Sheets

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management vigorously pursue the collection of all unpaid rentals by exhausting administrative and legal remedies with the delinquent account through arranging installment payments, imposing penalties, and persistent demand letters.
observations.managementAction: To collect overdue accounts from the lease agreement and the treasury office.
observations.reason: Despite the constant reminder of the treasury office, there’s still no response coming from the delinquent lessee. However, it becoming an increasing challenging to collect unpaid accounts given that the previous lessee always insisted that they don’t have any liability given the pandemic and typhoon Odette destruction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer justify with supporting documents showing valid and compelling reasons for not implementing calibrated actions in response to the delays incurred by the contractor, and not initiating the termination of contracts as well as the imposition of other appropriate sanctions against the contractor due to default. Also, submit the lacking documents to determine the actual amount of liquidated damages to be imposed by the Municipality to the erring contractors. Moreover, the Project Engineer has to monitor and require the contractor to submit a Statement of Work Accomplished monthly and ensure that implementation of the project is in accordance with the timelines set in the contract.
observations.managementAction: The management particularly the Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Accounting are already looking to possibilities of either cancelling the existing contract or imposing liquidating damages to the contractor.
observations.reason: A meeting had already conducted and it was concluded that the contractor is given an extension at the end of the year. The president explained that their company had experienced unexpected financial difficulties which resulted to the delay on the procurement and delivery of construction materials needed to finish the projects. As reserve alternatives, the management was already considering as other option to cancel the contract and blacklist the erring contractor if they did not make good of their promise and among else, the finance committee also planned to withhold any payments and temporarily suspended on requesting for the downloading of the rest of the loan from the DBP to prevent the premature commencement of the amortization of the rest of the loan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Disposal Committee to prepare the pertinent documents to facilitate an appraisal review of the unserviceable properties by the COA, including a properly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report for unserviceable properties, an Appraisal Report of unserviceable properties for disposal, and other related documents such as photographs of unserviceable properties for disposal; And file the application for disposal with the COA, pursuant to COA Circular 92-386.
observations.managementAction: To dispose in due time the reported unserviceable motor vehicles and office and other unserviceable properties in accordance to the provisions of Section 79 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Section 379 of Republic Act (RA) 7160.
observations.reason: The disposal of said unserviceable properties is planned by August or September of the same year. Moreover, to comply with the rules and regulations pertaining to the disposal of public properties, the disposal committee is planning to attend related seminars or to conduct thorough review of the disposal regulations to avoid violations and to smoothly conduct the disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall require the BAC to fasten the creation of another account of the LGU to comply with the posting requirements on all its procurement. Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed, and the approved contract be posted on the PhilGEPS website and website of the municipality, if any, within the reglementary period as required by 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: To request new account to the PHILGEPS/ DBM that will allow NOA and NTP to the PHILGEPS sites.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariats are still waiting for the response of the PHILGEPS for the creation of a new account. The concerns were already addressed and coordinated with the appropriate agency. Until now, the LGU still suffers the lack of proper posting in PHILGEPS thus resulting to non-compliance and non-submission of pertinent reports to GPPB.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following accounting entries to close the Due to Other NGAs account:
Trust Fund Books
Due to Other NGAs    			xxx
Construction In Progress				xxx
Advances to Contractors				xxx

Management’s Comment:
Management informed that they have already drawn the necessary accounting entries to close the Due to Other NGAs account per JEV Nos. 300-2024-03-03931 and 100-20024-03-72722 and JEV Nos. 300-2024-03-03932 and 100-2024-03-72723 dated March 8, 2024.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant record all subsequent procurements of construction materials for distribution as “Construction Materials for distribution” and credit the same only upon issuance thereof following the submission of the General Services Office of the consolidated monthly RIS and SSMI.
We further recommended and Management agreed that necessary adjusting journal entries be prepared to correct the balances of affected accounts.
Management’s Comment:
Management prepared JEV No. 100-2024-03-74701 to correct the balances and take up the necessary adjustment to the Construction Materials for Distribution account.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the GSO to prepare PCs for every class of PPE; and the Municipal Accountant to prepare PPELC, and facilitate reconciliation with the balances in the books of accounts vis-à-vis the PCs and RPCPPEs. Henceforth, require the Inventory Committee to immediately conduct the physical inventory of all properties and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the auditor as required under existing regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to recompute the depreciation of the components of road network system without providing 5% residual value and make the necessary entries to correct the balances of affected accounts. Henceforth, discontinue the practice of providing for 5% residual value in the computation of depreciation expense of Road Networks account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
Management’s Comment:
Management informed that the Municipal Accountant prepared the revised Depreciation Schedule-Public Infrastructure for the period ended December 31, 2023, showing the depreciation of the components of road network system without the 5% residual value. Moreover, adjusting journal entries with JEV Nos. 100-2024-03-74116 and JEV No. 100-2024-03-774701 dated March 22, 2024.

We recommended and  Management agreed that, in coordination with offices concerned, they will revisit and implement COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to return the unutilized PDAF to the Bureau of the Treasury pursuant to Supreme Court Decision dated November 19, 2013.
Management’s Comment:
Management informed that they have prepared DV No. GF- 2024-04-2307 dated April 17, 2024 to return the unutilized funds for PDAF to the Bureau of the Treasury.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the disbursement voucher to return SEF funds previously transferred to TF- LDRRMF; and
Discontinue the practice of providing for appropriations of LDRRM in the SEF funds.
Management’s Comment:
Management prepared disbursement voucher no. TF2024-04-099 dated April 26, 2024 to return to the SEF funds that were previously transferred to TF-LDRRMF.
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all the complete set of bidding documents are prepared and issued to the bidders in the conduct of procurement thru competitive bidding.

We recommended and Management agreed to terminate the contract with the contractor and pursue the remedies provided in the IRR of RA 9184 and undertake the necessary steps to continue the remaining work on the project.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the various Department Heads to refrain from collecting overtime pay in accordance with CSC-DBM JC No. 2 s. 2015, dated November 25, 2015 on the payment of authorized overtime services of government personnel.
Management’s Comment:
Management informed that they issued a memorandum addressed to all Department Heads dated December 29, 2023 to observe strictly the provisions of CSC-DBM Circular No. 1, series of 2015 dated November 24, 2015, for implementation beginning January 2024.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the necessary procedures in order to facilitate the withdrawal of said funds for transfer to other Authorized Government Depository Banks of the Municipality and ensure that the funds are restored and readily available for use.
Management’s Comment:
Management informed that the Municipal Treasurer has attended the Sangguniang Bayan Session to explain the request for reactivation, closure and transfer of the Municipality’s bank accounts at Philippine Postal Savings Bank to Land Bank of the Philippines. For this, SB Resolution No. 76, series of 2024 was adopted.
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all designated collecting officers are adequately bonded.

We recommended and Management agreed to fill up the casual plantilla positions at the Chocolate Hills Complex.
Management’s Comment:
Management informed that for the supervisory position, the plantilla remain unfilled because no one qualified for the position. Management also acknowledged concerns over the inventory management in the Chocolate Hills Complex. Further they expressed that they are amenable with the recommendations.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to process the registration of Carmen Municipal College with the BIR.
Management’s Comment:
Management informed that the processing of the requirements for registration to the BIR of the Carmen Municipal College is already on going.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that the:
Inventory Committee, whose members shall be temporarily relieved from their regular duties, prioritize compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts; and
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, to facilitate periodic reconciliation of PPE accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records and effect the necessary adjustments to present the correct Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current account balance.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prioritize tracing or identifying the cause of the negative balances starting specifically with the significant abnormal balances of the Cash in Bank Local Currency Current Account and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the accurate balances of the affected accounts in order to fairly present the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the adjustments to correct the inclusion of the General Fund LDRRMF-CO balance in the Trust Liabilities-DRRM, as follows:

Transfer of LDRRMF-CO to General Fund Books
General Fund Books

Cr. Prior Period Adjustment			₱326,789.37
To revert the CYs 2022-2023 unutilized LDRRMF-CO balance to General Fund

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to initiate the review of the supporting documents (official receipts, memorandum of agreement, contracts, resolutions, and the like) and subsequently reclassify the transactions based on the identified source and purpose in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that the Municipal Engineer:
Obtain revised/renewed performance and/or warranty surety bonds from the contractor, with the appropriate validity period; and
Issue/Cause the issuance of Certificates of Final Acceptance only after the defects liability period or one year from the completion date.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials agreed to consider the application of a software/computer system to integrate the records of the Municipal Assessor’s Office and the MTO so as to provide a seamless transmission of data for the real property taxes due and ultimately the generation of an accurate balance for establishing the RPT/SET receivable accounts at the beginning of each year.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that:
The Municipal Accountant reconcile the records and retrieve relevant documents to identify the source agencies and purpose of the grant of financial assistance to finally process for its utilization;
The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the return of the excess funds from completed projects for those agencies requiring the return of the same in the Memorandum of Agreement; and
The concerned officials secure authority from the Cebu Provincial Government and other source agencies to realign grants to other priority projects of the Municipality and recognize income for the unexpended balances of the financial assistance.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to demand or follow-up in writing the submission of liquidation reports from those barangays with unliquidated financial assistance, especially those that were granted in CY 2022 and prior years.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant with the other concerned officials agreed to:
Revisit the project proposals through its implementing offices to check the implementation of projects of these funds pursuant to the law governing said grant and/or the Memorandum of Agreement/contract with the respective donors, and accordingly require the proper liquidation of the amount granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the donor; and
Review the source documents of each subsidiary ledger and subsequently reclassify the transactions based on the identified nature/purpose.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRM Officer, agreed to submit the revised/supplemental LDRRMFIP and AIP for 2023 which includes the PPAs charged to the unexpended balances of prior years’ LDRRMF to substantiate the expenditures charged thereof pursuant to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

For the Municipal Accountant to improve practices in reporting the accounts by:
a) drawing a journal entry voucher to effect the necessary adjustments to correct
the misclassified PPE accounts;
b) Disclosing the total Road Network System account in the Notes to Financial
Statements; and
c) conducting reconciliation of the General Ledger and RPCPPE to facilitate review
of noted variances;
d.) Revisit the policy in establishing the estimated useful life of an infrastructure
asset considering significant factors; and
e.) Facilitate immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties.

We recommended the following to the management:
a) Comply with the requirements on the conduct of monthly reconciliation to
lessen the accumulation of unreconciled items;
b) Prepare the adjusting journal entry for the minor discrepancies mentioned in the
first observation above; and
c) Perform the appropriate actions to transfer the balance of the aforesaid
accounts to an active account.

We recommended the following to the Management:
a) Efficiently utilize the fund for the purpose designed and assess whether the
originally funded PPAs are feasible for implementation; otherwise, consider
reprogramming to more suitable PPAs to encompass the four (4) thematic areas
of DRRM and integrate them into the AIP for approval by the local Sanggunian
to serve as a roadmap in LDRRM Fund utilization and improve the capacity of
the Municipality to respond in emergencies or disasters; and
b) Prepare a complete and comprehensive annual LDRRMF Investment Plan
which shall include the PPAs of current year appropriations and shall
incorporate under a separate caption the list of PPAs to be funded from the
unexpended balances of previous years’ LDRRMF and monitor the unexpended
balances to be recognized under the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account including
its utilization and balances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor agreed to reconcile their records pertaining to the unrecorded parcels of land and the inconsistences on the land descriptions presented in the Tax Declaration of Properties. Additionally, after reconciliation, the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to recognize the unrecorded parcels of land to the books of accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the timeline in transferring the unexpended balances of the LDRRM Fund to the Trust Liabilities – DRRM at year end under the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor facilitate and supervise the proper proceedings for the land titling and to cause the immediate transfer to the Municipality to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the LGU pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular 92-386; and
Allocate funds intended for the processing of documents/requirements for Certificates of Land Title.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly observe the appropriate entries for the recognition of fines and penalties and advance payments pertaining to real property taxes. In addition, prepare adjusting journal entries for the reconciliation of the RPT Receivable and/or SET Receivable accounts to its corresponding Deferred RPT and/or Deferred SET accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Consider the provision of additional manpower in the Municipal Accounting Office with technical expertise on bookkeeping or accounting; and
Request to the Commission on Audit for the installation of an electronic accounting system such as the eNGAs and/or eBudget to facilitate timely and systematic generation of financial reports.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to recognize the corresponding depreciation of the PPE from the date of completion and adjust the previous ones to reflect the correct carrying balances. Depreciation shall be for one month if the PPE is available for use on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is available for use after the 15th of the month.

We recommended that the Management consider the prescribed limitation of two percent (2%) of allocation for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Budget to the Office of the Municipal Mayor only. Also, obliges the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the obligated and disbursed amount for the EME account is within the approved Budget. Moreover, requires the Municipal Accountant to properly discern the usage of account title, especially in recording the EME disbursements, and make a necessary adjustment to the affected accounts to fairly present the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering and Accounting Office reconcile their respective records on ongoing construction projects and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of the accounts by reclassifying the Construction in Progress account of ₱899,500.00 to Buildings account to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended the direct the personnel in charge of the preparation and submission of the required supporting documents for the cash advance for Confidential Funds to submit within seven (7) days after release of check, and to support it with complete documentations.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant and accountable officers concerned to utilize the fund transfers according to the agreement and exhaust all possible actions to prioritize the liquidation of fund transfers for every project or return any unused balance to the Source Agencies.

We recommended that Management ensure that realignment/reprogramming is always supported by an appropriation ordinance and strictly adhere to Section 336 of RA 7160 to ensure the validity and legality of the newly programmed PPAs and their implementation.

We recommended that the Management acknowledge the right of the MPDO to control the disbursements of the 20% Development Fund for him to have a better understanding of how the fund is being spent and take the necessary actions to ensure that the PPAs are being fully utilized and monitored to immediately address issues that will be encountered in the project implementation and achieve the optimum desirable socio-economic and environmental development of the LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed to allow to use of one of the vehicles of the Municipality as a common service for the day-to-day operations or errands of the LGU and ensure that the procured modified 4x4 pick-up vehicle be exclusively used for disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief operations in conformity with Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01-2020 dated November 4, 2020, and if warranted reclassify the recording of the said vehicle. Otherwise, refund or replenish the 20% DF for the cost of the subject vehicle, the appropriations for which shall be taken from the General Fund. Moreover, the LGU officials strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1-2020 of the DBM, DOF, and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended and the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Budget Officer agreed not to charge expenses without appropriations and over the allotted budget, and regularly monitor the spending against a specific appropriation to avoid the incurrence of expenses above the budget. Also, ensure that the augmentations and realignment of appropriation are fully supported with proper documentation.

We recommended and Management agree to refrain from using the LDRRMF for payment of administrative costs and strictly utilize the 5% LDRRMF only for disaster risk management activities as provided in the above guidelines to ensure that the objectives outlined in the allocation of the fund are achieved through proper usage thereof.

We recommended the LDRRM Officer agreed to properly execute their PPAs to avoid splitting contracts and instruct the General Services Officer or BAC Secretariat, as the case may be, to consolidate the procurement of similar and related items into one purchase request and subject the same to competitive bidding pursuant to Rule IV, Section 10 of the revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure competitiveness and obtain the lowest and most advantageous price.

We recommended and the LDRRM Officer agreed not to release stockpiling/prepositioning items to ineligible beneficiaries and remind them that such items will only be released during emergency cases or already stored for a long time but before their expiration dates, supported with proper documentation. Also, refrain from using the LDRRM Fund from not its intended purpose. Moreover, submit a justification letter as to the distribution of the goods as well as the complete list of the recipients with the corresponding number of kilos or bags received.

We recommended and Management agreed to vigorously pursue the collection of all unpaid rentals by exhausting administrative and legal remedies with the delinquent account through arranging installment payments, imposing penalties, and persistent demand letters.

We recommended and the GSO agreed to retrieve the records of the properties of such Property Tags/Stickers on all properties for easier cross-referencing with the AREs and proper identification of the properties; and attach Property Tags to the old and newly acquired PPEs before these are distributed to the intended end-users to establish control on the use of the government property and equipment.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to justify with supporting documents showing valid and compelling reasons for not implementing calibrated actions in response to the delays incurred by the contractor, and not initiating the termination of contracts as well as the imposition of other appropriate sanctions against the contractor due to default. Also, submit the lacking documents to determine the actual amount of liquidated damages to be imposed by the Municipality to the erring contractors. Moreover, the Project Engineer has to monitor and require the contractor to submit a Statement of Work Accomplished monthly and ensure that implementation of the project is in accordance with the timelines set in the contract.

We recommended and the Disposal Committee agreed to prepare the pertinent documents to facilitate an appraisal review of the unserviceable properties by the COA, including a properly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report for unserviceable properties, an Appraisal Report of unserviceable properties for disposal, and other related documents such as photographs of unserviceable properties for disposal; And file the application for disposal with the COA, pursuant to COA Circular 92-386.

We recommended and Management agreed not to provide representation expenses to any LGU officials who are also receiving Representation and Transportation Allowance (RATA), and exercise caution in the disbursement of funds by adhering to rules and regulations to avoid Irregular, Unnecessary, Excessive, Extravagant, or Unconscionable (IUEEU) expenditures.

We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to limit the allocation for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses to the Office of the Municipal Mayor and comply with the 2% statutory limitation for EME based on the actual collections of RPT in the next preceding calendar year in compliance with Section 325(h) of RA 7160 in the preparation of annual budget. Moreover, requires the Municipal Accountant to strengthen the control on the disbursements of funds by disapproving payments charged against the EME in excess of the 2% limitation.

We recommended that Management shall require the BAC to fasten the creation of another account of the LGU to comply with the posting requirements on all its procurement. Notices of Award and NTP to Proceed, and the approved contract be posted on the PhilGEPS website and website of the municipality, if any, within the reglementary period as required by 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference of ₱4,454,046.88 between the RPT and SET Receivables, submit the Abstract of Real Property Tax as required by Section 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I; and

Make necessary adjusting entries to reconcile the receivable account balances at year-end pursuant to Sections 233 and 235 of Book II of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to review the validity vis-à-vis the possibility of claim of the outstanding payables and cause the reversion of those without valid claim to the unappropriated surplus. The management is also reminded to be stringent in assessing expenses to be obligated at year end.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the remaining long outstanding balance of ₱6,810,124.51 for errors and misclassifications; and

To effect the necessary adjustments in order to present a reliable and fairly presented balance of the Due to LGUs account in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEVs to acknowledge the receipt of reimbursement of claims and submit these to PhilHealth within the reglementary period to facilitate the subsequent release and to avoid the withholding of claims.

Henceforth, investigate the reasons and submit an explanation/justification on the 21 months delay in the preparation and submission of the JEV/JVs on the past releases.

We recommended submission of the soft copies of the above reports as soon as possible.

Further, require the Municipal Accountant to submit a written explanation on the delay and non-submission of soft copies of these reports despite follow-ups and discussion during the Entrance Conference.  If the observation is left uncorrected, propriety and validity of the financial data cannot be consolidated; its significance and usefulness to the users of the data is lost if submission is delayed.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the department heads to see to it that accomplishments on a daily basis of Job Order workers are not redundant with the job description of the LGU's existing regular employees, otherwise wages paid are subject to audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that Management refrain from resorting to procurement and disbursement through reimbursement.  Acquisition of goods and services is recommended to   pass through the regular mode of procurement pursuant to R.A. 9184 and disbursement procedures in accordance with the NGAS.  Payments shall be made directly to supplier, legitimate payee or concerned persons.

Henceforth, comply with the requirement of adequate procurement planning in   accordance with Section 7 of R.A.9184.

It is recommended that the Management, with the assistance of the MPDC projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the constituents and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices in materials and feasibility of the projects in order to address the needs of the Municipality.  

Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects attune with the present needs and constraints.

Revisit the applicable procedural rules and guidelines on property and supply management, such as IPSAS 12 and COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, to ensure proper recording of donated properties and supplies.
Continue with the reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts, record the necessary adjustments in the books and submit a copy of the journal entries to the Audit Team.
