Prepare the CLUP to guide in the selection, prioritization and screening of projects, programs and activities in the CDP. 

Convene with the Local Development Council to identify the bottlenecks that might have contributed to the delay of the approval of the CDP and LDIP and to provide viable solutions in resolving any identified constraints.

Coordinate with the concerned departments, such as the MSWD, Office of the Municipal Agriculturist, Municipal Health Officer, among others, to aid in the database formulation for expeditious approval of the plans/programs.

Formulate a holistic/ comprehensive yet realistic plan to expedite the timely procurement and implementation of the identified priority development projects.

Conduct detailed engineering investigation and pre-planning prior to execution in order to achieve full implementation of the projects for a timely delivery of the desired services and benefits to its beneficiaries. 

Work on the reversion of funds for proposed projects that duplicated or overlapped with those of the other LGUs to make such funds available for other meaningful use.

Ensure that all accountable officers whose duties permit or require the possession or custody of government funds are properly bonded. 

Reevaluate its existing cash requirements and henceforth ensure compliance with the provisions of the fidelity bond requirement either by increasing the amount of bond of its accountable officers or by designating additional collectors for the excess accountability under a separate fidelity bonding requirement as deemed appropriate.
Assign personnel responsible for the monitoring of the renewal of the LGU-owned motor vehicles to ensure timely registration with the LTO.

Submit a valid justification for the incurred delays on the abovementioned activity. 

Identify the cause of the delays and implement measures to efficiently execute the procurement activities within the prescribed period as required in the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

For acquired PPEs after CY 2010, trace completely the PPE items from the books of accounts down to transaction documents/journals and compute for the required depreciation based on information gathered; and

PPEs recorded under SEF shall also be subjected to One-time Cleansing procedures to clean up the old balances. PPEs inventoried, other than those acquired after CY 2010 which are already completely accounted after tracing, shall be computed with accumulated depreciation and shall be considered fully depreciated.
Perform reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts and record the necessary adjustments in the books. A copy of the journal entries shall be provided to the Audit Team.

Instruct the MDRRMO to submit a supplemental LDRRMF investment plan for CY 2021 with MDRRMC recommendation and approval of Sanggunian and Local Chief Executive to support the disbursements for projects charged to the unexpended balance. Henceforth, the MDRRMC shall ensure that the annual investment plan shall cover all projects and activities to be funded from the current year’s budget for LDRRMF and all unexpended balance from prior years.

Ensure that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources in compliance with the provisions set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2020-1.

Require the aforementioned supporting documents from all departments that facilitated the receipt and distribution of donations in-kind, prepare the required summary/list and submit to the audit team. The one-time separate reports for cash and in-kind donations shall also be prepared and submitted to the NDRRMC through the OCD, copy furnish the Audit Team.

Check the completeness of the documents supporting a particular transaction before signing Certificate A of the voucher.

Submit the pertinent supporting documents to the transactions involving ₱19,480,960.09, otherwise, subject transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit.

See to it that all claims against the Municipal funds are covered with duly supported complete documentation to evidence actual disbursements and delivery of items allegedly procured. A repetition of the same will be dealt with in accordance with prescribed laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to derecognize PPE items costing below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 and reclassify against the Government Equity account those PPEs whose expenditures are classified as “repairs and maintenance”. Further, derecognize and transfer the PPE items whose future economic benefit or service potential flows to other government agencies or barangay government rather than to the Municipality. Finally, to recognize adjustment to the affected PPE accounts including the accumulated depreciation and the Government Equity.
observations.managementAction:
PPE items below ₱50,000 were derecognized.

Adjustments were made to the affected PPE accounts including the accumulated depreciation and the Government Equity.
observations.reason: PPE items whose future economic benefit or service potential flows to other government agencies or barangay government rather than to the Municipality were still recorded as PPE.

observations.managementAction: The GSO has recently occupied their own office.
observations.reason: The newly designated General Services Officer has yet to familiarize herself with the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant should observe the journal entries prescribed under the NGAS Manual for the setting-up of the RPT/SET Receivables and the Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts at the beginning of the calendar year. In addition, conduct a reconciliation of the accounts, draw an adjusting entry to correct the deficiencies, and regularly maintain Subsidiary Ledgers to keep track with the movements to the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant observed the journal entries prescribed under the NGAS Manual for the setting-up of the RPT/SET Receivables and the Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts at the beginning of the calendar year.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has yet to conduct a reconciliation of the RPT/SET Receivables and the Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that:

The Municipal Treasurer to remit to the Provincial Government the under remittance of ₱35,728.92 and to ensure compliance with Section 271 of the Local Government Code of the Philippines in computing for the distribution of proceeds of the basic Real Property Tax; and

The Municipal Accountant regularly prepares the monthly Abstract of Real Property Tax so as to facilitate the distribution of real property tax collection and submit the same to the Provincial Accountant to ensure adherence to the provisions of Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction:
Remitted the under remittance to the Provincial Government.


Prepares the monthly Abstract of Real Property Tax.
observations.reason: The monthly Abstract of Real Property Tax to be regularly submitted to the Provincial Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant to regularly submit the weekly summary of JEVs showing the breakdowns of the amounts Due to LGUs and the monthly Abstract of Real Property Tax to the Provincial Accountant to ensure compliance with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs and fairly present the accounts Due to LGUs and Cash in Bank account in the financial statements as of December 31, 2022.
observations.reason: The weekly summary of JEVs and the monthly Abstract of Real Property Tax to be regularly submitted to the Provincial Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that:

The barangay officials to coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office in the preparation of the program of works on the projects allotted to them;

The Municipal Engineering Office to address issues and concerns in the implementation of PPAs to prevent various modifications to the program of works and to conduct the procurement process as planned; and

The Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned offices, to closely monitor the implementation of planned development projects of the 20% Development Fund and to adopt methods to ensure timely conduct of the procurement process.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:

The Municipal Accountant to issue written notices/demand letters to the concerned accountable officers with outstanding cash advances for immediate liquidation.


The Municipal Accountant to disclose the probability of collection in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

Retrieve the supporting documents for cash advances for Confidential Fund and forward immediately to COA ICFAU.

Document the actions taken on the collection of outstanding cash advance to personnel whose location could not be located, and file a request for write-off, if necessary.

Comply and retrieve all documents in compliance to COA Circular No. 2016-005 to write-off the dormant account of the deceased and former Municipal Mayor and refile the same to the Commission on Audit Regional Office No. XIII.
observations.managementAction:
The supporting documents for cash advances for Confidential Fund were forwarded to COA ICFAU.


Requested for authority to write-off dormant accounts.
observations.reason:
Written notices/demand letters were not issued.



The probability of collection was not disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements.





The request for authority to write-off dormant accounts was denied by COA due to failure to comply with the documentary requirements provided under Item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that:

The AAO or the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the deduction of the required monthly loan amortization from the payroll of the employees with delinquent accounts.

The AAO and the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the concerned employees to obtain records from the GSIS for reconciliation and to pay immediately the unremitted amounts, if any.

The Municipal Accountant to remit on time the withheld premiums and loan amortizations of employees to the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Deducted the required monthly loan amortization from the payroll of the employees with delinquent accounts.
observations.reason: Records from the GSIS and the LGU is not yet reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to prepare and submit the required report to the oversight agencies stated in the DBM LBC No. 141. In addition, strictly observed the specific guidelines, for future reference, on the financial assistance received from various national government agencies for monitoring and transparency.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:

Each department/end-user unit to carefully plan and monitor the procurement of common-use supplies through proper forecasting, projections, and estimations of inventories to be used and stocked and to accomplish the Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMP) template to be submitted to the Budget Officer for Budget approval.

The Budget Officer to forward the individual PPMPs included in the budget proposal to the BAC Secretariat, in coordination with the Property Custodian-Designate, for consolidation and scheduling of procurement activities in order to facilitate bulk purchases instead of per office requirements, in accordance with Section 7.3.4 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184.

The Property Custodian-designate or designate a General Services Office to facilitate and store bulk purchases of supplies and materials. In this way, the Municipality can reduce the number of checks issued for the purchase of common-use office supplies and avail of trade discounts.
observations.managementAction:
PPMP were submitted the Budget Officer and BAC.


















The GSO has recently occupied their own office for storage of supplies.
observations.reason:
Bulk purchases were still done instead of per office requirements.




Bulk purchases were still done instead of per office requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less. The members of the said committee shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed;

Instruct the Inventory Committee to coordinate with the General Services Office in the preparation of a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP); and

Strictly observe the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking, recognition of those found at station and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Management:

Create an Inventory Committee with adequate number of members to complete the conduct of physical inventory within the prescribed timeframe, who will coordinate with the General Services Office in the preparation of the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for every semester of a given year, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002;

The GSO shall coordinate with the Accounting Office for the periodic reconciliation of both records, as prescribed in the said COA Circular; and

Require the Supply Unit to strictly observe the preparation and weekly submission of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Accounting Unit for appropriate recording of the supplies and materials issuances.

We recommended that the Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the non-submitted paid disbursement vouchers, and observe the prescribed period for the submission of accounts.

We recommended that the Management:

Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection Reports, As-Built Plans and pictures for every infrastructure project implemented By Administration as this will be the basis for the issuance of the related Certificate of Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all progress billings pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and such other applicable laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management provide specific timeline for the implementation of the planned projects in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan, Project Procurement Management Plan, and Annual Procurement Plan. In addition, communicate with the DSWD for the planned projects to be funded out with LGU counterpart otherwise, realign said funds for other priority programs or projects.

We recommended that the:

Management ensure strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the liquidation of cash advances;

The Municipal Accountant issue a demand letter to the accountable officers who are no longer connected to the Municipality, requiring him/her to liquidate all long outstanding advances; and

File the appropriate request for authority to write-off dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Management requested for authority to write-off dormant accounts but was denied by COA due to failure to comply with the documentary requirements provided under Item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer:

Observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting of inventories;

Prepare and maintain the required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports, such as SLC, SC, RIS and RSMI, as prescribed in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs; and

Conduct periodic physical count of inventories to determine any necessary adjustments in the books and inventory records.
observations.managementAction: The Management observed the perpetual inventory method in accounting of inventories; however, SSMI were not prepared and not submitted by the Property Custodian- designate to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO-designate ensure the submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, and completely accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: The Management still did not submit within the required period the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to recognize the corresponding depreciation of the PPE from the date of completion and adjust the previous ones to reflect the correct carrying balances. Depreciation shall be for one month if the PPE is available for use on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is available for use after the 15th of the month.
observations.managementAction: To draw adjusting entries immediately based on the recommendations given by the COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management consider the prescribed limitation of two percent (2%) of allocation for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Budget to the Office of the Municipal Mayor only. Also, obliges the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the obligated and disbursed amount for the EME account is within the approved Budget. Moreover, requires the Municipal Accountant to properly discern the usage of account title, especially in recording the EME disbursements, and make a necessary adjustment to the affected accounts to fairly present the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: To draw adjusting entries based on the recommendations given by the COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineering and Accounting Office reconcile their respective records on ongoing construction projects and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of the accounts by reclassifying the Construction in Progress account of ₱899,500.00 to Buildings account to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: To draw adjusting entries immediately to properly classify the transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the direct the personnel in charge of the preparation and submission of the required supporting documents for the cash advance for Confidential Funds to submit within seven (7) days after release of check, and to support it with complete documentations.
observations.managementAction: Follow the recommendations by submitting within seven days the required supported documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant and accountable officers concerned to utilize the fund transfers according to the agreement and exhaust all possible actions to prioritize the liquidation of fund transfers for every project or return any unused balance to the Source Agencies.
observations.managementAction: To properly monitor the funds based in the Memorandum of Agreement or existing circular or law governing the funds received from NGAs, other LGUS, GOCCs, and private institutions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that realignment/reprogramming is always supported by an appropriation ordinance and strictly adhere to Section 336 of RA 7160 to ensure the validity and legality of the newly programmed PPAs and their implementation.
observations.managementAction: Follow the recommendations by ensuring that realignment/reprogramming is always supported by an appropriation ordinance and strictly adhere to Section 336 of RA 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management acknowledge the right of the MPDO to control the disbursements of the 20% Development Fund for him to have a better understanding of how the fund is being spent and take the necessary actions to ensure that the PPAs are being fully utilized and monitored to immediately address issues that will be encountered in the project implementation and achieve the optimum desirable socio-economic and environmental development of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Follow the recommendations by making adjustments to the monitoring and controlling of transactions and projects under the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management allow to use of one of the vehicles of the Municipality as a common service for the day-to-day operations or errands of the LGU and ensure that the procured modified 4x4 pick-up vehicle be exclusively used for disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief operations in conformity with Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01-2020 dated November 4, 2020, and if warranted reclassify the recording of the said vehicle. Otherwise, refund or replenish the 20% DF for the cost of the subject vehicle, the appropriations for which shall be taken from the General Fund. Moreover, the LGU officials strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1-2020 of the DBM, DOF, and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: Refrain the use of the modified 4x4 rescue pick-up to purposes other than disaster-related activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Budget Officer not to charge expenses without appropriations and over the allotted budget, and regularly monitor the spending against a specific appropriation to avoid the incurrence of expenses above the budget. Also, ensure that the augmentations and realignment of appropriation are fully supported with proper documentation.
observations.managementAction: To avoid incurring expenses without appropriation and recurrence of the findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from using the LDRRMF for payment of administrative costs and strictly utilize the 5% LDRRMF only for disaster risk management activities as provided in the above guidelines to ensure that the objectives outlined in the allocation of the fund are achieved through proper usage thereof.
observations.managementAction: To refrain from making expenditures not related to disaster risk reduction management activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LDRRM Officer to properly execute their PPAs to avoid splitting contracts and instruct the General Services Officer or BAC Secretariat, as the case may be, to consolidate the procurement of similar and related items into one purchase request and subject the same to competitive bidding pursuant to Rule IV, Section 10 of the revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure competitiveness and obtain the lowest and most advantageous price.
observations.managementAction: To avoid incurring expenses without appropriation and to consolidate procurement planning pursuant to the Annual Procurement Plan/Work and Financial Plan of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (LDRRMP).

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO to retrieve the records of the properties of such Property Tags/Stickers on all properties for easier cross-referencing with the AREs and proper identification of the properties; and attach Property Tags to the old and newly acquired PPEs before these are distributed to the intended end-users to establish control on the use of the government property and equipment.
observations.managementAction: To continue putting tag/sticker to procured/acquired government properties to establish ownership and control properties and determines those properties which still not have tag/sticker and renew the tag/ sticker for those that have already faded away.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management not to provide representation expenses to any LGU officials who are also receiving Representation and Transportation Allowance (RATA), and exercise caution in the disbursement of funds by adhering to rules and regulations to avoid Irregular, Unnecessary, Excessive, Extravagant, or Unconscionable (IUEEU) expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Stop providing meals and snacks during session.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Budget Officer to limit the allocation for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses to the Office of the Municipal Mayor and comply with the two percent (2%) statutory limitation for EME based on the actual collections of RPT in the next preceding calendar year in compliance with Section 325(h) of RA 7160 in the preparation of annual budget. Moreover, requires the Municipal Accountant to strengthen the control on the disbursements of funds by disapproving payments charged against the EME in excess of the two percent (2%) limitation.
observations.managementAction: To limit the allocation for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses to the Office of the Municipal Mayor and comply with the 2% statutory limitation for EME based on the actual collections of RPT in the next preceding calendar year in compliance with Section 325(h) of RA 7160 in the preparation of annual budget.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the regulation on the one-time cleansing of PPE, we recommended
that management:
a.) Create an Inventory Committee which shall have, but not limited to, at
least one representative each from the Accounting and GSO;
b.) Instruct the Inventory Committee to come up with a Physical Inventory
Plan for a complete and thorough physical inventory of the agency’s
assets.
c.) After which, require the immediate reconciliation with the accounting
records to facilitate review of noted variances, effect necessary
adjustments, provision of necessary disclosure in the notes to financial
statements, and the necessary disposition for those non-
existing/missing and disposal of unserviceable items.
For the disposal of the unserviceable properties, we also recommended that the
management:
d.) Create a Disposal and Appraisal Committee (DAC) which shall have, but
not limited to, at least one representative each from the Accounting and
GSO; and
e.) The DAC appraise the value of the unserviceable PPE and recommend the
mode of disposal.
For the other observations, we reiterated the following recommendations:
f.) Allocate in the annual budget the estimated cost for land titling to
expedite the titling of lands acquired;
g.) Adopt a uniform property identification system assigning property number
indicated in tags for each PPE item; and
h.) Insure all insurable properties to indemnify or compensate for any damage to,
or loss of all its properties in case of destruction.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:
a.) Regularly transfer the amounts deposited from the bank accounts in ONB
to LBP in order to avoid the accumulation of funds in the former and to
ensure sufficiency of funds in the latter;
b.) Convene and formalize the closure/discontinuance of maintaining the
bank account in the PNB and expedite the transfer of funds to the proper
account in LBP; and,
c.) Regularly monitor the cash in bank accounts recorded in the ledgers and
cashbooks to avoid the incurrence of abnormal negative balances.

We recommended the following to the Management:
To the LDRRMO, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and
Municipal Budget Officer (MBO):
a.) Set a specific implementation target period for each PPA of the LDRRMF
in the LDRRMP and AIP;
b.) Collaborate to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF, specifically the
70% Prevention and Mitigation Fund, Continuing Appropriations and
Special Trust Fund through proper planning, periodic monitoring of the
availability of the appropriations, and prompt execution and
implementation of the planned PPAs during the year;
To the Municipal Accountant:
c.) Ensure that the monthly LDRRMF Utilization Reports and Notes to
Financial Statements provide accurate, specific, and sufficient details of
17 Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012
the utilized and unutilized balances of the current appropriations,
continuing appropriations, and special trust fund; and
To the LDRRMO:
d.) Regularly submit the monthly and annual Reports on Sources and
Utilization of the LDRRMF to the audit team and to the respective
government oversight offices such as OCD, DILG, RDRRMC and DBM.

We recommended that the management expedite the update of the Local
Revenue Code in compliance with RA 7160 to maximize its taxing power and to
provide more funds for its operations.

a.) Require the Municipal Accountant reclassify all completed projects in the CIP account to the appropriate fixed-asset account and record the accumulated depreciation from prior year and depreciate these assets accordingly; b.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer, designated as Property and Supply Officer to prepare the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) as basis in recording the de-recognition from the books of the unserviceable properties and facilitate immediate disposal of the said unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. 1445; c.) Allocate in the annual budget the estimated cost for land titling to expedite the titling of lands acquired; d.) Adopt a uniform property identification system assigning property number indicated in tags for each PPE item; e.) Insure all insurable properties to indemnify or compensate for any damage to, or loss of all its properties in case of destruction.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted the IIRUP containing the list of the unserviceable items to audit team.
observations.reason: The unserviceable properties are still carried in the books. The unserviceable PPE were not yet disposed.

observations.recommendation: a.) Direct the Accounting Office and Treasurer’s Office in the regular retrieval of the supporting documents necessary for the timely validation of the book reconciling items, particularly the CMs related to PhilHealth; and b.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw JEVs to record the validated book reconciling items that were noted in the Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2021 in order to correct the balances of the Cash in Bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer coordinated and the Municipal Accountant drew JEVs to record the validated book reconciling items.
observations.reason: There are still CY 2022 book reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2021 yet to be recorded.

observations.recommendation: a.) Assign/designate personnel to function solely in recording the daily transactions in the Cashbooks for Cash in Bank, ruling and footing the cashbooks at the end of each month, and conducting at least monthly reconciliation between the Cash in Bank balances per cashbook and bank statement, and capacitate the personnel with the pertinent guidelines on the proper handling, custody and judicious preparation of the said cashbooks; b.) Ensure that the cashbooks are updated daily and conduct regular reconciliation between the cashbook balances and bank balances for all of the accounts (even in instances that the unadjusted balances are the same) to monitor the reconciling items that need to be adjusted/recorded in the Cashbooks for Cash in Bank; and c.) Record/Adjust the valid and long outstanding reconciling items in the Cashbooks for Cash in Bank to correct the cashbook balances of the respective Cash in Bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer assigned personnel and coordinated with the Municipal Accountant. Some cashbooks were updated and reconciliations were performed
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: a.) Prepare schedule of all receivables (as well as the schedules of the unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers) on a quarterly basis and conduct periodic verification, analysis and validation of their existence, in accordance with the Sections 6.1 and 7.1 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; b.) Prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to recognize any impairment in accordance with the IPSAS, any subsequent authorized write-off, and/or any recovery of the write-off vis-à-vis receivable accounts; c.) Assist the Accounting Office in the preparation of the schedules of all receivables and aging of dormant receivable accounts (as well as of the unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers) on a quarterly basis; d.) Direct the Management to exercise or exhaust all possible remedies for the collection of overdue accounts. If these efforts prove futile, document all action undertaken to recover the accounts; and e.) File a written request for authority to write-off the dormant accounts to the Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA), in accordance with Section 8.2 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and the request be supported by: (1) a list of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts; (2) the supporting documents listed in Section 8.3 of the same circular; and (3) the aging of the dormant accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management identified the status of receivable accounts. The Accounting Office prepared schedules for identified receivables and coordinated for collection efforts.
observations.reason: Some receivable accounts were identified and scheduled

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management direct the Municipal Accountant comply with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS by adequately providing a concise narrative on significant changes and breakdown of material balances to ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the disclosure requirements and to include the necessary reconciliations and narratives in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA versus actual amount in the Financial Statements was still not disclosed.

observations.recommendation: The Management: (1) resolve the discrepancies in the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF reflected in the Monthly Utilization Reports for CY 2020; and (2) ensure that the amount and details of the unutilized balances of the previous years’ appropriations of the LDRRMF are accurately, properly, and adequately disclosed in the LDRRMF Utilization Reports and Notes to Financial Statements, in consonance with the provisions of Sections 5.1.5 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMC, through its LDRRM Officer, prepared a separate plan summarizing the PPAs to be charged from the STF. The Management conducted periodic monitoring and started to itemize PPAs.
observations.reason: The utilization of LDRRMF for CY 2022 was still not maximized.

observations.recommendation: a.) Strategically plan and implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized
observations.managementAction: b.) Specify the implementation target period of the projects in the Annual Investment Program to guide the Municipality in scheduling the procurement activities and evaluation of actual performance against standards on a periodic basis
observations.reason: The MPDC conducted planning and monitoring

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the GAD Focal Point System-Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) to: a.) update and institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered age- and sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; b.) establish clear baselines and realistic estimates in budget preparation for PPAs translated from the demands/commitments identified in the GAD agenda.
observations.managementAction: Updating of GAD database is still ongoing.
observations.reason: GAD database updates are still ongoing and not yet fully institutionalized.

observations.recommendation: a.) The Municipal Accountant shall regularly conduct monthly reconciliation of the Cash in Bank records for all of its bank accounts (whether local or foreign) with the depository bank balances within ten days from receipt of the bank statements and at least quarterly reconciliation with the Municipal Treasurer’s cashbooks (including the Cash in Bank accounts), in compliance with Section 74 of PD 1445 and Section 181(c) of GAAM Volume 1. We recommended that the Management submit the BRS on time with complete supporting documents to the concerned COA auditor for verification; b.) The Municipal Treasurer shall submit and furnish to the Accountant all paid DVs summarized in the Report of Checks Issued on a daily basis and copies of validated Deposit Slips summarized in Report on Collections and Deposits to ensure timely recording of disbursements and deposits in the books; and c.) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall analyze the dormant bank account and communicate with the source agency to determine the nature and terms of the account. Reversion to the General Fund may be applied if proven that the unexpended amount is the balance of their counterpart funding to the SEDIP. They shall cause the closure of the dormant account and transfer the balance to an active account in order to protect the amount from possible risk of loss of rights and ownership.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted BRSs though not always on time
observations.reason: analysis of dormant accounts was conducted.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the unrecorded parcels of lands and allocate resources to expedite titling and secure complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Titling of land still in process.
observations.reason: Titling of land still in process.

observations.recommendation: a. Resolve the discrepancies in the unexpended balances of the Current Year CY 2020 unexpended Appropriations (MF-MOOE, MF-CO for completed projects and MF-CO for unimplemented/partially implemented projects) reflected in the accounting and other financial records of various departments of the Municipality in order to arrive at the correct balances of STF and DRMMF-Continuing Appropriations (from CY 2020) as of December 31, 2020; b. Direct the MDRRMO and MPDC to convene in the proper classification of the specific PPAs (either CO or MOOE, but not both) to be funded from the LDRRMF of the LGU and to ensure that disbursements are only made within the nature and budget appropriations of the PPAs for LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended allotments were reconciled and some PPAs in the LDRRMP were transferred to STF
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Resolve the discrepancies in the LDRRMF balances that were reflected in the Monthly Utilization Reports for CY 2020; and ensure that the amount and details of the unutilized balances of the previous years’ appropriations of the LDRRMF are accurately, properly, and adequately disclosed in the LDRRMF Utilization Reports and Notes to Financial Statements, in consonance with the provisions of Sections 5.1.5 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO and Accounting Office coordinated to reconcile balances
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: The LDRRMC, through its LDRRM Officer, prepare a separate Plan (summarizing the PPAs to be charged from the STF 2017 to 2021) which shall be incorporated in the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP), and shall include the PPAs of current year appropriations. The LDRRMO shall conduct periodic review of the LDRRM and prepare a revised and updated Plan whenever new expense item is introduced and integrated in the Revised/Supplemental AIP for approval of the LCE.
observations.managementAction: A separate plan (summarizing the PPAs to be charged from the STF 2017 to 2021) was prepared and transferred to STF where applicable
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: Disclose the nature, purpose and other relevant information about the fund in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Disclosure was made in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: The management intensify its information campaign and enforce implementation of all existing regulations on the segregation of solid waste at the barangay level
observations.managementAction: and The Municipal Government strictly monitor and provide assistance to barangays in the proper establishment and operation of their Materials Recovery Facility.
observations.reason: Ongoing

observations.recommendation: Return to the respective source agencies the unutilized balance of funds for projects which are already fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Management returned the unutilized balances of completed projects to the respective source agencies.
observations.reason: The unutilized balance of funds for projects which are already completed were returned to the source agencies.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality should exert extra efforts in the Information and Education Campaign for Solid Waste Management. Also, continue allocating in the annual budget a fund for the programs/projects/ activities until such time that the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 are fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: The management shall consider coordination with Panabo City for possible MOA for dumping of residual wastes of the municipality.
observations.reason: There is a difficulty in finding suitable disposal site as 98% of the area of the municipality is flat and prone to flooding. The safe closure and rehabilitation of the former dumpsite has been fully implemented on September 30, 2020 and 10% of the area are now planted with trees while 90% is now proposed to be converted into Sanitary Landfill subject to the Geographical Assessment of the Mines and Bureau of Sciences. During the year, the LGU has purchased 1 shredder for plastic wastes and 1 shredder for glass wastes in order to reduce the volume of wastes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO-designate ensure the submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, and completely accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: The Management still did not submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dump in accordance with Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: The LGU ensures the proper disposal of Solid Waste by alternative means
observations.reason: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the BAC, submit a written explanation on why the regulations for the procurement of consultancy contract involving individual consultants were not observed.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require the ineligible beneficiaries to refund the cash aid they received and ensure that the total amount of ₱624,000.00 are collected and returned to DSWD. We enjoined the responsible officials to initiate the collection efforts for the refund by sending demand letters.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted a written justification to the audit team.
observations.reason: Recommendation was not acted by Management due its pending request for reconsideration.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Inventory Team to ensure that a reliable RPCPPE containing complete and detailed information of items counted is prepared after conduct of annual physical count of all PPE items. A copy of the report shall be submitted to the auditor in compliance with the timing requirement in Volume 1, Section 266 of the GAM for LGUs.

We also reiterated that Management designate personnel to assist the accounting department in the preparation of the PPE ledger cards.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of some of the PPE Ledger Cards had been started but these were not updated regularly due to the lack of manpower.
observations.reason: A related audit observation was issued during CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the subsidiary ledgers of these accounts showing the details of receivables. Furthermore, follow the foregoing procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 to ensure that dormant receivables/fund transfers in the books are analyzed, verified, reconciled, provided with impairment loss and written-off accordingly for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant work back on tracing entries to CIP accounts and ascertain the status of the infrastructure projects recorded. Completed projects shall be immediately classified to proper PPE account and its corresponding accumulated depreciation computed and recorded. A copy of the JEV, supporting documents and schedules shall be provided to the auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: On-going tracing of Construction in Progress accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant obtain a formal estimate of recoverable service amount for the unserviceable items, compare with the carrying amount and make as basis in providing an allowance for impairment. Disclosures shall be provided in the financial statement in accordance with this standard. Management shall also consider disposal of the above items.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to submit the duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names of the taxpayers and the tax due at the start of each year to the Municipal Accountant as basis for recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables in order to present reliable and accurate receivable and deferred income accounts.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Instruct the Engineering Department to immediately submit to Accounting Office the
necessary construction-related documents upon project completion so that CIP account
could be timely transferred and recorded to its specific PPE accounts.
2. Ensure that the two important documents “Certificate of Completion” and Certificate
of Acceptance” be signed/approved by the Local Chief Executive ahead of the other
related construction payment documents so that recording of appropriate PPEs for the
completed projects during the year be timely done.
3. Require the Accounting Office to draw Journal Entry Voucher to record the
understatement of Accumulated Depreciation of all identified PPEs in order to present
the accurate balance of the affected accounts in the financial statements as at
December 31, 2021.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to
prioritize the adjustment in the books of all cash accounts affected in the bank
reconciliation statements. Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up adjusting
entries corresponding to the reconciling items.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct those concerned to facilitate immediate processing to officially transfer to the
above PPE to the respective users/beneficiaries, secure an authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan by way of resolution to authorize the Mayor to execute a Deed
of Donation (DOD) or a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA);
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)
derecognizing the assets from the books of the Municipality and furnish the
respective beneficiaries a copy of the JEV and MOA/DOD to facilitate the recording
in the latter’s respective books; and
c) Henceforth, strictly enforce the guidelines and procedures in One Time Cleansing of
PPE Account Balances.
d) We also recommended that the management sees to it that other properties not
mentioned in our AOM but have similar conditions to our observation may also be
derecognized in the books of accounts of the municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended the management the following:
1. Institute follow up and action against the defaulting NGOs/POs pursuant to the
provision of the Memorandum of Agreement and COA Circular no. 2007-001;
2. The Municipal Accountant should send formal demand letters to NGOs/POs
requesting them to liquidate and submit the required documents as stated in the
Memorandum of Agreement and COA Circular no. 2007-001; and
3. Strictly observe and implement the provisions of guidelines and controls under COA
Circular Nos. 2007-001 to ensure that objectives and proper implementations of
programs are realized to their intended purpose.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor for the following:
1. Direct the focal person to submit a written explanation in identification and selection
(126) beneficiaries for the implementation of the TUPAD program specifically on the
barangay workers who avail of such.
2. Direct the focal person to submit lacking documents such as Certification from the
LGU/Barangay or DSWD, Daily Time Records, and Accomplishment Reports
3. If result of the evaluation on the submitted explanation for the (126) beneficiaries
cannot be fully justified, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized fund totaling P1,105,970.00
to the Source Agency through Bureau of Treasury. Moreover, furnished the Audit
Team with a copy of the deposit slip of such transactions and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to close
any Due to NGAs account for the amount of fund returned to the SA.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. Direct the responsible official(s) of the Municipality to submit the (6) MOAs entered
by Municipality with the National Government Agencies; and
b. Henceforth, all MOAs covering fund transfer from NGAs should be submitted to the
Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to submit a certification specifically
indicating the names of frontliners who served at the quarantine facilities of the LGU
including their Daily Time Record on their actual involvement/work in the said
facilities;
b. We recommended that the MHO submits the pertinent reports, duly received by the
DOH, as enumerated under Section 6.4 of the DBM-DOH JMC No.2, s2020 (items b
to d), for further audit verification; and
Section 6.4 of the DBM-DOH JMC No. 2 S.2020, enumerated the roles and
responsibilities of the LGUs and Other Health Facilities concerned, stated as follows:
a. Facilitate the payment of the COVID-19 SRA to eligible public and private HWs;
b. Submit to the DOH-CHD reports on the number of the COVID-19 patients
attended to, and list of eligible health workers indicating their positions,
compensation/s entitled to, and days physically present;
c. Submit to the DOH-CHD a certification attesting to the truthfulness of the
contents of the report and adequate consent for sharing of data has been obtained
from the personnel involved. The certification must be signed by the head of
human resource or personnel division (if such division is in the structure) and the
head of health facility, clearly indicating his/her designation or position; and
d. Submit to the DOH-CHD the financial and accomplishment reports for
monitoring, in accordance with pertinent accounting and auditing rules and
regulations.
c. We further recommended that the municipality conscientiously follow the relevant
guidelines and conditions on the granting of allowances or any other pay to its
personnel as enumerated in the law, rules and regulations allowing such grant.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the management:
1. Direct the Municipality Development Council Planning and Development Officer to
monitor and evaluate the implementation of all 20% DF programs/projects/activities.
2. If the result of evaluation will determine that these projects could not be implemented
during the year, direct the preparation of request for re-alignment to the Municipality
Development Council.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the following:
1. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking documents
within (3) days upon receipt of this AOM, to support the validity and correctness of
such payment, to wit:
a. Daily Time Records
b. Accomplishment Reports
c. Program of Works
d. Original Delivery Receipts with full acknowledgment from the implementing
department the receipt of the goods.
2. Submit a written explanation from the implementing department of the project for
the lapses in the documentation and implementation of the Improvement and
Resettlement Site Project at New Camiling and New Maligaya, Carmen, Davao del
Norte.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the MDRRMO and MPDC to
properly plan and monitor the full implementation of the LDRMM plan towards the
achievement of the objectives of RA No. 1012 in ensuring disaster preparedness for the
safety of the Municipality’s constituents.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents within five (5) days upon the
receipt of this AOM for subsequent audit decision.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Direct the Accounting Department thoroughly analyze abnormal/negative balance of
the Inter-Agency Payable Due to BIR to ensure the correctness of their balances as
presented in the financial statements as at year-end; and
b. Require the responsible official/employees to remit the employer/employee
contributions and loan payments immediately to GSIS, Philhealth, Pag-ibig amounting
to P210,063.78 to avoid interest, penalties and surcharges.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive the following:
a) Direct those concerned to facilitate immediate processing to revert to the
Unappropriated Surplus all undocumented payables, secure an authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan by way of resolution to authorize the Mayor to execute the
transfer of funds to General Fund;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
reverse previous entry made recognizing payable without supported with complete
documentation; and
c) Directs the GSO to install control measures that can monitor the recording and
timely payment of payables.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of action:
1. Direct the Local Finance Committee together with Internal Audit Division to create a
comprehensive written policy on the grant of financial assistance, incorporating
among others the control measures to ensure efficient and effective disbursement of
government funds.
2. Direct the Sangguniang Bayan to revisit the approved municipal ordinances and
resolutions regarding the financial assistance that will serve as a guide in crafting the
internal policy.
3. Direct the Budget and Accounting Office to process only financial assistance
transactions that are in adherence with the appropriation ordinance.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive:
1. Instruct the concerned officials to compute the appropriate liquidated damages
incurred by the project contractor in the amount equal to (1/10) of one percent (1%) of
the cost of delay in the implementation of water source development project.
2. Henceforth, direct prompt implementation of all projects based on the timelines as
stipulated in the contract.

We recommended that the management:
a.) Require the Municipal Accountant reclassify all completed project in the
CIP account to the appropriate asset account and record the
accumulated depreciation from prior year and depreciate these assets
accordingly;
b.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer, designated as Property and Supply
Officer to prepare the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable
Properties (IIRUP) as basis in recording the de-recognition from the
books of the unserviceable properties carried in the PPE accounts and
facilitate immediate disposal of the said unserviceable properties
pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. 1445;
c.) Allocate in the annual budget the estimated cost for land titling to
expedite titling of lands acquired;
d.) Adopt a uniform property identification system assigning property
number indicated in tags for each PPE item; and
e.) Insure all insurable properties to indemnify or compensate for any
damage to, or loss of all its properties in case of destruction.

We recommended the following to the Management:
a.) Direct the coordination between Accounting Office and Treasurer’s Office in
the regular retrieval of the supporting documents necessary for the timely
validation of the book reconciling items, particularly the CMs related to
PhilHealth; and
b.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw JEVs to record the
validated book reconciling items that were noted in the Bank
Reconciliation Statements for CY 2021 in order to correct the balances of the
Cash in Bank accounts.

We recommended that the Management institute measures to improve the
internal control on cash management, recording and monitoring by requiring
the Treasurer’s Office to:
a.) Assign/designate personnel to function solely in recording the daily
transactions in the Cashbooks for Cash in Bank, ruling and footing the
cashbooks at the end of each month, and conducting at least monthly
reconciliation between the Cash in Bank balances per cashbook and
bank statement, and capacitate the personnel with the pertinent
guidelines on the proper handling, custody and judicious preparation of
the said cashbooks;
b.) Ensure that the cashbooks are updated daily and conduct regular
reconciliation between cashbook balances and bank balances for all of
the accounts (even in instances that the unadjusted balances are the
same) to monitor the reconciling items that need to be
adjusted/recorded in the Cashbooks for Cash in Bank; and
c.) Record/Adjust the valid and long outstanding reconciling items in the
Cashbooks for Cash in Bank to correct the cashbook balances of the
respective Cash in Bank accounts.

In view of the aforementioned observations, we recommended the following:
To the Municipal Accountant:
a.) Prepare schedule of all receivables (as well as the schedules of the
unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers) on a quarterly basis
and conduct periodic verification, analysis and validation of their
existence, in accordance with the Sections 6.1 and 7.1 of COA Circular
2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;
b.) Determine the status (if dormant or not) of the receivable accounts that
are not yet identified and prepare the aging of dormant receivable
accounts (as well as the dormant cash advances and inter-agency fund
transfers) on a quarterly basis as prescribed by Section 7.4 of the same
circular (Annexes 1 to 3) to support any request for write-off of the
accounts;
c.) Prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to recognize any impairment
in accordance with the IPSAS, any subsequent authorized write-off,
and/or any recovery of the write-off vis-à-vis receivable accounts;
To the Municipal Treasurer or Treasurer’s Office:
d.) Coordinate with the Accounting Office in the determination of the status
of the receivable accounts (that are not yet identified) as dormant or not
by specifying the date of the last collection (date of last transaction or
movement) for each account and in the conduct of regular monitoring
and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected
when these become due and demandable;
e.) Assist the Accounting Office in the preparation of the schedules of all
receivables and aging of dormant receivable accounts (as well as of the
unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers) on a quarterly basis;
To the Local Chief Executive:
f.) Direct the Management to exercise or exhaust all possible remedies for
the collection of overdue accounts. If these efforts prove futile,
document all action undertaken to recover the accounts; and
g.) File a written request for authority to write-off the dormant accounts to
the Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA), in
accordance with Section 8.2 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and the
request be supported by: (1) a list of available records and extent of
validation made on the accounts; (2) the supporting documents listed in
Section 8.3 of the same circular; and (3) the aging of the dormant
accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that management direct the Municipal
Accountant comply with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS by adequately
providing a concise narrative on significant changes and breakdown of
material balances to ensure that relevant information is available to the
intended users of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Council (LDRRMC) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator
(MPDC) Office prudently plan and materialize timely and relevant Programs,
Projects and Activities (PPAs) of the LDRRMF. Furthermore, coordination
among the said offices and the Municipal Budget Office is recommended to
regularly monitor the availability of the appropriations and the implementation
of each PPA in order to optimally utilize the available funds to fulfill the
purpose of the fund and attain the objectives of the aforementioned
provisions.

We recommended that Management:
a.) Strategically plan and implement its developmental projects within the
target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is
optimally utilized;
b.) Specify the implementation target period of the projects in the Annual
Investment Program to guide the Municipality in scheduling the
procurement activities and evaluation of actual performance against
standards on a periodic basis; and
c.) Require the MPDC to conduct periodic monitoring and evaluation of the
municipality's PPA implementation with appropriate reports submitted
to the Mayor for perusal and appropriate action and furnish the audit
team of the required reports for timely review and validation of
accomplishments.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the GAD Focal Point
System-Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) to:
a.) establish clear baselines and realistic estimates in budget preparation
for PPAs translated from the demands/commitments identified in the
GAD agenda; and
b.) update and institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and
systematically gathered age- and sex-disaggregated data which will
provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and
policy formulation.

We recommended that the Management:
Create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less. The members of the said committee shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed;
Instruct the Inventory Committee to coordinate with the General Services Office in the preparation of a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP); and
Strictly observe the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking, recognition of those found at station and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Management:
Create an Inventory Committee with adequate number of members to complete the conduct of physical inventory within the prescribed timeframe, who will coordinate with the General Services Office in the preparation of the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for every semester of a given year, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002;
The GSO shall coordinate with the Accounting Office for the periodic reconciliation of both records, as prescribed in the said COA Circular; and
Require the Supply Unit to strictly observe the preparation and weekly submission of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Accounting Unit for appropriate recording of the supplies and materials issuances.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the rules and regulations of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 for the utilization of the BGCM Fund and other issuances in mitigating the effects of COVID-19 disease.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Chairperson of the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with the posting requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 to promote accountability and transparency in the conduct of Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to have contact information of the payees to communicate within a reasonable period of time that their checks are ready for pick-up;
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that Official Receipts were issued by the payees acknowledging their receipt of checks; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the non-submitted paid disbursement vouchers, and observe the prescribed period for the submission of accounts.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection Reports, As-Built Plans and pictures for every infrastructure project implemented By Administration as this will be the basis for the issuance of the related Certificate of Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all progress billings pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and such other applicable laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management instruct its MDRRMO to meticulously plan the programs or projects under the LDRRM Fund. Additionally, stop the practice of charging those expenditures which are not directly related to disaster risk reduction and management. Finally, observe the guidelines and other issuances pertaining to the utilization of the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that the Management provide specific timeline for the implementation of the planned projects in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan, Project Procurement Management Plan, and Annual Procurement Plan. In addition, communicate with the DSWD for the planned projects to be funded out with LGU counterpart otherwise, realign said funds for other priority programs or projects.

We recommended Management strict adherence to proper recognition of an item of Property, Plant and Equipment as well as the fair presentation of financial statements provided in Philippine Application Guidance No. 3 of Paragraphs 19-20 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and Paragraph 13 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17.  Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up in books of accounts the unrecorded lots duly validated and evaluated by the Inventory Committee at their fair market value should the acquisition cost is not available.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office strictly observe compliance with the perpetual inventory method of accounting on the supplies and materials procured for construction projects implementation by administration by recording all purchases under the account Construction Materials Inventory.  Also, coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer or designate a Municipal GSO who will be tasked to implement controls on the receipt and utilization of the same and submit a summary of the utilized construction materials monthly to the Accounting Office.  Likewise, effect the necessary adjustment for the number of supplies and materials which remained unutilized for the intended projects or repairs.
We recommended and Management agreed to undertake all the procedures under COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate accounts to record financial transactions and prepare adjusting entries to correct the accounts affected.
Other payables					xxx
Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable			xxx

We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare the IIRUP and initiate proper disposal to avoid further deterioration of the properties.  Proper segregation must be made of these properties that are still saleable and which can be subjected to destruction.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also in the procurement of goods.  Henceforth, ensure that future procurements be deducted with at least 1% but not more than 5% retention money or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount, otherwise any expenses due to the defects within the warranty period be charged to concerned officials.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Chairman to make sure that the basic information on the Purchase Orders are completely and appropriately filled-up, including the date of receipt of the supplier.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit their reports on time to the Accounting Office, and the Municipal Accountant to ensure the on-time remittance of barangay shares from RPT collections in the succeeding quarters as provided under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code.  Likewise, stop the practice of transferring first to the Trust Fund Account before remittance to the respective barangays.
We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the Source Agencies on what to do with the excess, unimplemented, and non-moving accounts.

We recommend that Management instruct the MHO to prepare and submit the following documents to support their claims of AHDP and SRA:
List of the eligible HRHs and HWs determined by the Local Health board;
DTRs/accomplishment report; and
Approved working arrangement indicating the area of assignment.
Also, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation as provided by Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommend that management submit the necessary supporting documents to establish the propriety of the distribution of the supplies such as inventory of the supplies at year-end and distribution list signed by the recipients.  Henceforth, require the accounting department to follow the relevant rules and regulations in recording the procurement of supplies for distribution.

We shall appreciate submission as soon as possible of the following overdue Reports on the Physical Count of:
Inventories (RPCI)
Investment Property (RPCIP)
Land and Land Improvements (RPCLLI)
Local Road Network (RPCLRN)
Other Public Infrastructure (RPCOPI)
Buildings and Structures (RPCBS)
Equipment, Furniture and Fixtures and Other PPE (RPCEFFOPPE)
Henceforth, it is recommended that management abide by the deadline of submission of these reports pursuant to Sections 67 to 73 of the GAM, Volume II to substantiate the existence of the municipality’s inventories, property, plant and equipment.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year so that the RPT and SET Receivables can be adjusted by the Municipal Accountant to reflect the correct balances at year-end.

We recommend that management refrain from resorting to procurement and disbursement through reimbursement.  Acquisition of goods and services is recommended to   pass through the regular mode of procurement pursuant to R.A. 9184 and disbursement procedures in accordance with the NGAS.  Payments shall be made directly to supplier, legitimate payee or concerned persons.  Henceforth, comply with the requirement of adequate procurement planning in   accordance with Section 7 of R.A.9184.

We recommend that management submit the necessary supporting documents in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-01 on the payment of honoraria to focal person for special projects, otherwise the same will be subject to audit suspension or disallowance.  Moreover, we recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that claims for honoraria for special project be supported with sufficient documentary requirements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-01 and DBM Circular No. 2007-02.

We recommend that management submit the PIF to the GSIS as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, properties and interest of the government with the GIF and an explanation on why the insurable properties, assets and interests of the LGU were not yet insured with the GSIS.  Henceforth, it is recommended that management abide by the deadline of submission of the PIF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2018-02 to insure against any insurable risk the LGU’s properties, assets and interests.

We recommend that management submit the necessary supporting documents in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-01 on the payment of honoraria for lecturer/coordinator, otherwise the same will be subject to audit suspension or disallowance.  Moreover, we recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that claims for honoraria for lecturer/coordinator be supported with sufficient documentary requirements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-01 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-01.

We recommend that management submit an explanation on entitling the government project bearing the first name of the LCE contrary COA Circular No. 2012-003 and DMC No. 2010-101.  Henceforth, management is advised to strictly abide by the prohibition stated in both circulars to uphold good local governance.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to do the following:
For acquired PPEs after CY 2010, trace completely the PPE items from the books of accounts down to transaction documents/journals and compute for the required depreciation based on information gathered; and
PPEs recorded under SEF shall also be subjected to One-time Cleansing procedures to clean up the old balances. PPEs inventoried, other than those acquired after CY 2010 which are already completely accounted after tracing, shall be computed with accumulated depreciation and shall be considered fully depreciated.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to perform reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts and record the necessary adjustments in the books. A copy of the journal entries shall be provided to the Audit Team.

We recommended and Management agreed to process the derecognition of dormant unliquidated cash advance pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 by filing a request for write-off to be supported by certified relevant documents validating the reason for non-collectability, including the proof of exhaustion of all remedies to demand for liquidation of the cash advance.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the adjustments to rectify the errors in recording using the following entries:
Dr. Agricultural and Forestry Equipment 	₱499,000.00
Cr. Subsidy from NGAs				₱499,000.00
To record agricultural equipment purchased during implementation of  		Mango and Other Fruits Processing Facility project.
Dr. Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses	₱1,969,250.00
Cr. Transportation Allowance				₱1,969,250.00
To reclassify recording of expenses re: check # 878225
Dr. Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses 	₱126,711.68
Cr. Transportation Allowance				₱126,711.68
To reclassify recording of expenses re: check # 878201

We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the compliance with COA Circular 2020-006 on the cleansing of PPE accounts. The Municipal Mayor agreed to create a new Inventory Committee whose members shall be temporarily relieved of their regular duties to complete the physical inventory in three months or less. The Inventory Committee shall prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities, and shall ensure its full implementation.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the MDRRMO to submit a supplemental LDRRMF investment plan for CY 2021 with MDRRMC recommendation and approval of Sanggunian and Local Chief Executive to support the disbursements for projects charged to the unexpended balance. Henceforth, the MDRRMC shall ensure that the annual investment plan shall cover all projects and activities to be funded from the current year’s budget for LDRRMF and all unexpended balance from prior years.

We recommended and the BAC agreed that henceforth, Supplemental/Bid Bulletins shall be issued for clarifications and modifications to the Invitation to Bid or Bidding Documents and ensure timely posting in the Philgeps website to afford all prospective bidders the opportunity to submit competitive offers consistent with the revised requirements.

We recommended and Management agreed to establish implementing guidelines on the grant of assistance to individuals/family in crisis situation which will clearly set the qualification of the indigent, amount and frequency of assistance, documentary requirements and the like, concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan, to provide proper control in the disbursement of public funds.
