We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources in compliance with the provisions set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2020-1.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to verify the correctness of the recorded tax payable in prior years and ensure remittance to BIR of the reconciled total amount of unremitted taxes withheld.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Direct the Municipal Accountant (MACCO) to prioritize the adjustment in the books of all cash accounts affected in the bank reconciliation statements. Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up adjusting entries corresponding the reconciling items.

Direct the MT to verify the LBP-Panabo Branch the transaction incurred in LBP Account No. 1742-1039-26 on November 18, 2016 amounting to P688,792.11 and effect the necessary adjustment/ correction therein.

Instruct the person responsible for the preparation of BRS to require the MT to notify the payees concerned about the check payment that became stale.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MACCO to reclassify the non-current portion of Accounts Payable for better presentation of liabilities of the Municipality in the Statement of Financial Position as at year-end reporting date.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MACCO to reclassify the non-current portion of Receivable Account for better presentation of receivable of the Municipality in the Statement of Financial Position as at year-end reporting date.
We recommend that the LCE of the Municipality of Carmen enjoin the concerned officials to submit to COA the documents as requested, for the team to facilitate the conduct of audit.
We recommended that the LCE direct her MT and MACCO to submit the lacking documents to support the propriety, validity and correctness of such payments of hazard pay.

We recommended that the LCE instruct its accounting, budget, and other requisitioning offices, to submit the required documents as mandated by GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and other necessary documents, to ensure accountability, transparency and compliance with the provisions in the conduct of such procurement.

We recommended that the LCE:
Instruct all concerned offices to furnish the accounting office all the documents for the in-kind donations, received, and distributed for recording purposes; and
Adhere to the COA Circular No. 2020-09 dated April 9, 2020 to ensure donation’s transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the LCE:
Instruct the warehouse in-charge to prepare and maintain stock cards of each inventory items; Prepare Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances Report; and, Adhere to COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014, to ensure the accuracy of the inventory.

We recommended that the LCE enjoin the MSWDO to:
Submit a written explanation for the lapses on the documentation of SAP payment, which is not in accord with the guidelines set under the DSWD-DOLE-DTI-DA-DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 s. 2020, for our future evaluation; and
Adhere strictly with the guidelines embodied in the said JMC and DSWD MC No. 4 s. 2020 so that future cash subsidy releases be fully documented and could be relied upon, to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the LCE instruct its accounting, budget and other requisitioning offices, to submit the required documents as mandated by GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and other necessary documents to ensure accountability, transparency, validity and compliance with the provisions in the conduct of such procurement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Accounting Office thoroughly analyze abnormal/negative balance of the Inter-Agency Payable Due to BIR to ensure correctness of their balances as presented in the financial statements as at year-end; and
Require the responsible officials/employees to remit the employer/employee contributions and loans payments immediately to GSIS, Philhealth and PAG-IBIG amounting to P56,929.96 to avoid interest, penalties and surcharges.

We recommended that the responsible official(s) of the Municipality submit the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) entered into by the Municipality with other government agencies in CY 2019 and prior years. Henceforth, all MOAs covering fund transfers from other government agencies should be submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MT and MACCO to submit the lacking documents within three (3) days upon the receipt of this AOM, to support the validity and correctness of such payment of labor.
We recommended that the LCE direct its MACCO, and LDRMMO Operating Officer to observe due care in the use of LDRMMF to ensure that it is utilized solely for approved DRRM related PPAs.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Engineering and Accounting Department to reconcile their PPAs and CIP account, categorizing the projects as to its nature, status of completion, cost and funding source.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the implementing departments to coordinate with the Accounting Department to immediately process the documents of the completed projects necessary to support its transfer to the appropriate PPE account; and
Direct the MACCO to make disclosure in the Notes of the Financial Statements on the completed project that still remained in the Construction in Progress account.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Engineering Department provide relevant information in preparing PPAs as prescribed in COA Circular 2013-004 to adopt and implement full disclosure of all transactions involving public interest.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Municipal Engineer to accomplish the necessary POW and Status of Work Accomplished for every infrastructure projects implemented by administration and validated by the Municipality’s Inspectorate Committee and will be the basis for the issuance of the related Certificate of Completion and Acceptance by the head of the agency. Afterwards, submit to Audit Team the Program of Work, Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, pictures and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance for said infrastructure projects implemented by administration.

We recommended that the LCE create an Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of road networks in compliance with the requirement of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction: Conduct physical inventory of local roads and submit report to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and/or Treasurer to: Return immediately the P374,837.51 unused balances received from the Source Agencies and furnish, accordingly, the Audit Team a copy of the pertinent deposit slip; henceforth, all unspent balances of fund transfers should be returned to the SA unless otherwise provided by law/Memorandum of Agreement; Immediately submit to the office of the auditor the liquidation report of the P56,002.00 funds received from the DOH; and Prepare appropriate adjusting entries including the balance due to PTO to correct the recorded balance of Due to Other NGAs account.

We recommended that the LCE enjoin the concerned officials to ensure the annual preparation of the required reports, conduct of inventory of roads and submission of the corresponding report of the physical inventory taken to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended the following course of action: Employees employed by the Municipality rendering less than 8 hours per day shall be compensated through honorarium basis; and The Municipality should apply the qualification standard requirement in identifying the rightful workers vis-à-vis their compensation consistent with the directive of the said wage order.

We recommended the following course of action: The LCE cause the execution of renewed lease contract, containing therein, among others, the provisions for transfer of lease rights, penalty for non-payment of rentals and the rights and duties of the parties; The LCE enforce the prohibitions and penal provisions in the contract; The Local Chief Executive enjoin the Local Economic Enterprise Officer to keep an updated list of market stall lessees, noting therein that which are still occupied by the original lessees or transferred/subleased to others; and The Sanguniang Bayan reconsider the amendment of the Revenue Code of the Municipality, adjusting rental fees and examining the provision of the lease contract taking into consideration the current economic condition of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
The LCE, Eco Enterprise and MTO have updated the list of market stall lessee for 2020.

Not Implemented
The draft amendment of the Local Revenue Code was already submitted to the level of the Sangguniang Bayan for legislative actions.

observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
All of the book reconciling items for CY 2020 were properly adjusted and recorded in the books. The amount was transferred to the bank General Fund. In 2022, the amount was processing in one office to the bank. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall revisit the undetermined reconciling items (such as for LBP Account No. 2732-1064-59 under General Fund) to determine the appropriate action or disposition to pave the way for their elimination from their “open” status in the books. They shall combine their efforts to retrieve the supporting documents/ bank statements/ records and history of movements in cash balances of the said account, as well as to determine the date when the discrepancy started to show up. Moreover, they could also trace cash transactions in the cashbook of the said account since the balance in the cashbook is consistent with the confirmed bank balance. These actions will be of assistance to the preparation of monthly bank reconciliations for the said account. Once the reconciling item is validated by supporting documents, it should be immediately adjusted or recorded;
observations.reason: Some of the cashbooks for Cash in Bank accounts were not updated, thus reconciliation of some of the cashbooks is not done. Moreover, the handling of the documents/ supporting documents are not available and will be coming from the other agency/ies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:
1. direct the Municipal Accountant to record the unrecorded parcels of lands.
2. direct the appropriate funds for the Land and titling of lands already under the name of the Municipality and direct the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the processing of the Transfer Certificate of Titles (TCTs) in order to process absolute ownership over the untitled parcels of lands, and direct the Municipal Accountant to record the unrecorded parcels of lands.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Titling of lands still in process with Registry of Deeds; The Municipality of Carmen reached the maximum limit for owning agricultural lands (should not be more than 5 hectares); titling of land still in process. The Municipal Accountant recorded the unrecorded parcels of lands.
observations.reason: Titling of land still in process; some parcels of lands are already taken up in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:
a. Resolve the discrepancies in the unexpended balances of the Current Year Appropriations (MF-MOOE, MF-CO for completed projects and MF-CO for unimplemented/partially implemented projects) reflected in the accounting and other financial records of various departments of the Municipality in order to arrive at the correct balances of STF and DRMMF-Continuing Appropriations (from CY 2020) as of December 31, 2020; and
b. Direct the MDRRMO and MPDC to convene in the proper classification of the specific PPAs (either CO or MOOE, but not both) to be funded from the LDRRMF of the LGU and to ensure that disbursements are only made within the nature and budget appropriations of the PPAs for LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The unexpended allotment of 2021 is now transferred to the STF. It was agreed that the PPAs of the AIP of CY 2022 must be specific to determine the classification as its nature.
observations.reason: The overall continuing appropriations indicated in the CY 2021 report were understated. Few PPAs for the STF as of December 31, 2021 are still stated. PPAs as of 2021 are still being stated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
that the Municipal Accountant provide the disclosure requirements of IPSAS in the Notes to Financial Statements to ensure adequate disclosures such as: (1) Road Networks – disclose the total road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements (Annex B); (2) Material differences between the budget and the actual amounts in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts – provide explanatory notes as required by IPSAS 24 par 14; and (3) Reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA versus actual amount in the Financial Statements – provide reconciliation as required in paragraph 47 of IPSAS 24.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The Accounting Office admitted that they followed the pro-forma Notes to Financial Statements and that they were not aware of the other necessary disclosures to be provided. They however committed to take the recommended actions to disclose the pertinent information in the Notes.
observations.reason: Non-disclosure of total road network system and other related details of the system and lacking additional disclosures (additions, disposals, transfers) to strictly adhere to the disclosures required by IPSAS; no disclosure for the material departures from the approved budget and transfers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
(1) resolve the discrepancies in the LDRRMF balances that are reflected in the Monthly Utilization Reports for CY 2020; and
(2) ensure that the amount and details of the unutilized balances of the current, continuing, and previous years’ appropriations of the LDRRMF are accurately, properly, and adequately disclosed in the LDRRMF Utilization Reports and Notes to Financial Statements, in consonance with the provisions of Sections 5.1.5 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The LDRRMC, through its LDRRM Officer, shall prepare a complete and comprehensive annual LDRRMF Investment Plan which shall include the PPAs of current year appropriations and shall incorporate under a separate caption the list of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balances of previous years’ LDRRMF. The LDRRMO shall conduct periodic review of the LDRRM and prepare a revised and updated Plan whenever new expense item is introduced and integrated in the Revised/Supplemental AIP for approval of the LCE.
observations.reason: The balances are not accurately disclosed in CY 2020 reports; the CY 2021 report's beginning balances were understated; some STF balances were transferred; some PPAs from STF were still not included in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMC, through its LDRRM Officer, prepare a complete and comprehensive annual LDRRMF Investment Plan which shall include the PPAs of current year appropriations and shall incorporate under a separate caption the list of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balances of previous years’ LDRRMF. The LDRRMO shall conduct periodic review of the LDRRM and prepare a revised and updated Plan whenever new expense item is introduced and integrated in the Revised/Supplemental AIP for approval of the LCE.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The Management commented that the low level of utilization of the STF was attributable to low rate of administered vaccines and most PPA that were not implemented are the capacity building activities because of the COVID-19 restrictions. The LDRRMC plans to reprogram the STF from CY 2017-2021 and incorporate the PPAs in the supplemental investment plan for CY 2022. For the STF from CY 2016, the Accounting Office disclosed that this was already reverted to the General Fund.
observations.reason: The PPAs from the STF for prior years were still not included in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2021; some balances were reverted or transferred; implementation was affected by COVID-19 restrictions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
a. Re-assess the said fund and convene in resolution/ordinance as to its nature and purpose. Moreover, the handling of the fund could be further improved by using it in a more economic way; and
b. Disclose the nature, purpose and other relevant information about the fund in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
The Management stated that the handling of the funds was improved and the amount was transferred to the bank General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management return the balance to the respective source agencies as required under the aforementioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

Fully Implemented
The Management implemented the recommendations and the LFC and concerned offices coordinated and complied with the submission and posting requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the GAD Focal Point System-Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) to:
a. establish clear baselines and realistic estimates in budget preparation for PPAs translated from the demands/commitments identified in the GAD agenda; and
b. update and institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered age- and sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction:
Ongoing
The Management stated that efforts were exerted to collect these receivables and demand letters were already sent to the beneficiaries before COVID-19 pandemic. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office admitted that minimal amount was collected within CY 2021 and some of the debtors/borrowers already promised to pay while some stated their reasons for the non-payment of their payables. The GFPS-TWG admitted that the updating of the GAD database is still ongoing. The Management committed to ensure that GAD PPAs are relevant and aligned with the Municipality’s GAD Agenda.
observations.reason: Absence of clear baselines and realistic estimates during budget preparation; updating of GAD database still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management intensify its information campaign and enforce implementation of all existing regulations on the segregation of solid waste at the barangay level; and the Municipal Government strictly monitor and provide assistance to barangays in the proper establishment and operation of their Materials Recovery Facility.
observations.managementAction:
Ongoing
The Management intensified its information campaign and took measures to enforce segregation of waste and assist barangays in establishing MRFs. The Secured Landfill Facility (SLF) was included in the budget and personnel in-charge attended seminar on how to develop SLF.
observations.reason: Non-segregation persists in some barangays; some barangays do not have MRFs; the DENR closed the dumpsite; the 10-year SWM Plan was not submitted to the audit team for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate submission of updated list of insurable properties with the GSIS and to require the Municipal Finance Committee to include in its annual appropriation the amount necessary to cover the premiums for the insurance of its properties.
observations.managementAction:
Ongoing
Documentary supporting requirements to GSIS were submitted but those were not complete according to the GSIS. Only Motor Vehicles, and some Serviceable Construction and Heavy Equipment were insured.
observations.reason: Documentary requirements still in-process; only selected assets insured; other supporting documents incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake immediate appropriate action to dispose the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and possible loss of their value.
observations.managementAction:
Ongoing
The recommendation to prepare IIRUP was well-taken. The Municipal Treasurer acknowledged the need and indicated that the IIRUP will be prepared. The GSO will conduct tagging.
observations.reason: Disposal process still ongoing; unserviceable items remain as of report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management send Demand Letters to debtors to intensify collections of overdue accounts. If these efforts prove futile, the LGU should document all action undertaken to recover the accounts and file written request for authority to write-off the dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction:
Ongoing
The Management stated that efforts were exerted to collect these receivables and demand letters were already sent to the beneficiaries before COVID-19 pandemic. Minimal collections were made in CY 2021 and some debtors promised to pay. The Office of the Local Chief Executive will pursue the recommendation once the concerned offices find that collectability could no longer be ascertained.
observations.reason: Some borrowers’ whereabouts could no longer be located; lapse of time resulted in missing documents and records; collection is difficult.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management return to the respective source agencies the unutilized balance of funds for projects which are already fully implemented in accordance with Paragraph 4.9 of COA Circular 94-013.
observations.managementAction:
Ongoing
Some NGAs returned the unutilized balances for projects already implemented. The LGU is following-up for remaining returns.
observations.reason: Some unutilized balances were already devolved/returned; other returns still pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Submit the said lacking documentary requirements;

Comply the posting requirements provided under GPPB-TSO Advisory No. 05-2020 on the procurement projects undertaken through Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to meticulously review and ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all related disbursements in accordance with the applicable laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the lacking documents and complied with the posting requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

The Municipal Budget Officer-Designate and/or Acting Municipal Treasurer post the monthly reports in another conspicuous public place in the locality as provided in Item 4.3 of the DBM LBC No. 125; and

Send written notice on the posting of reports to other oversight bodies as provided in Item 4.4 of the same LBC.
observations.managementAction: The Management posted monthly reports in three conspicuous places in the locality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Supply/Property Unit:

Maintain Property/ Equipment Cards for each type of PPE;

Render a report of all defective or non-functional PPE items for proper recording and disposal using the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); and

Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE and devise some measures to reconcile such report with the accounting records, so that the reliability of the balances of PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not submit the CY 2021 RPCPPE. However, reconciliation is ongoing between the concerned offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Management create an Inventory Committee that is composed of two or more employees including the Supply/Property Officer who will conduct physical count of all inventories which shall be the basis in preparing the RPCI;

The Municipal Accountant and the Supply/Property Officer maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, for each type of supplies and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance and disposal, to easily determine their balances at the end of the year; and

The Accounting Unit and the Supply/Property Unit should reconcile their records at the end of each quarter.
observations.managementAction: The Management created an Inventory Committee; Stock Cards are still not maintained and records of the Accounting Unit and the Supply/Property Unit are still unreconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

The Municipal Accountant, Treasurer, and end-users to agree on procedures that can resolve the issue on compliance on the timely submission of DVs and its supporting documents; and

The Management submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers, duplicate copies of checks and supporting documents, and the original copies of the reported cancelled checks in accordance with Section 100 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2019.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the lacking DVs and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Management ensure strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the liquidation of cash advances;

The Municipal Accountant issue a demand letter to the accountable officers who are no longer connected to the Municipality, requiring him/her to liquidate all long outstanding advances; and

File the appropriate request for authority to write-off dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Management requested for authority to write-off dormant accounts but was denied by COA due to failure to comply with the documentary requirements provided under Item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the completeness of documents in drawing cash advances to support the payments and ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 97-002.

We recommended that the Management ensure that the utilization of 20% DF is compliant to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 1 in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements are supported with complete documentary requirements as required in COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and submit the identified lacking documents, the Property Officer - Designate to make certain that the Purchase Orders are properly filled-out, and the Inspection Officer - Designate and the Property Officer - Designate to check that the Inspection Reports bear the complete data prior to signing the said reports.

We recommended that Management:

Require the monthly deduction from the monthly compensation of employees the loan amortizations due to GSIS and remit the same in accordance with Sections 14.2 and 14.3 of RA No. 8291 to avoid the occurrence of delinquent loan repayments from members; and

Encourage the employees/personnel listed with delinquent loan repayments to request from the GSIS for loan restructuring to lessen the burden of paying penalties/surcharges, if applicable.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer:

Observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting of inventories;

Prepare and maintain the required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports, such as SLC, SC, RIS and RSMI, as prescribed in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs; and

Conduct periodic physical count of inventories to determine any necessary adjustments in the books and inventory records.
observations.managementAction: The Management observed the perpetual inventory method in accounting of inventories; however, timely SSMI were not prepared and not submitted by the Property Supply Officer- designate to the Accounting Office, thus, any necessary adjustments in the books were not made accordingly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a)	Prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JV) to adjust the properties below the capitalization threshold and its related accounts.                                    b)	Ensure that all future acquisitions of properties below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000 will be recorded in accordance with Section 5.4 of COA Circular 2016-006 and Chapter II Section 4.ee of GAM for LGU Volume I.
observations.managementAction: JEV was drawn to take the necessary adjustment and were recorded to other Supplies and Materials Inventory where Subsidiary Ledger is maintained to monitor its status.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management be updated with the new guidelines that are necessary for the operations, especially during the Emergency Phase. Prepare and issue Acknowledgement Receipt for In-Kind Donations to all future receipts to acknowledge the donations and for proper accounting and recording. Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations or any proof of receipt must be prepared to prove the distribution to the intended recipient. And monthly reports on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations for all in-kind donations by a private entity in compliance with COA Circular 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020, and strictly follow the provisions thereto.
observations.managementAction: Reports were already submitted to COA including the distribution list.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct LDRRM Officer to be updated with the new issuance related to DRRM policies and guidelines. Prepare and submit the one-time report of the donations received, distributed and balances to the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the Auditor’s office as required in COA Circular 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO designate is made aware and reports were prepared and eventually submitted to line agencies concerned
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management coordinate with the personnel from different departments involved in the specific operation to have the appropriate flow of the needed documents. Require the MSWDO and/or the LRDDMO to immediately submit the complete master/distribution list of beneficiaries of the said program to the office for further assessment and examination of the transaction's validity.
observations.managementAction: Distributed list already submitted to COA by the concerned offices/officials.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management instruct the LDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to secure Income tax Returns and Omnibus Sworn Statements of the suppliers pursuant to GPPB Circular 01-2020.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO already coordinated with the supplier to be able to secure the requirements
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to locate the missing documents and submit the same to our office for verification.
observations.managementAction: The MAO exerts continued efforts to locate the same. Photo copies of OR's from MDRRMO were secured.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: Hence, we recommended that Management stop the practice of transferring accountability of cash advance from one Accountable Officer to another pursuant to Section 4.1.6 of COA Circular 97-002 and observed the documentary requirements to support the liquidation as cited in Section 5.10 of the same Circular. Strictly observe the guidelines on the granting and liquidation of cash advances to avoid future suspensions/disallowances in the audit.
observations.managementAction: The Practice is stopped immediately.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Thus, we recommended that Management stop the practice of procuring various goods through cash advances and direct the Bids and Awards Committee to abide by the standards set forth by procurement law and its recent issuances such as COA-GPPB JMC No. 1 and GPPB Circular 01-2020 for COVID-19 emergency procurements.
observations.managementAction: BAC and the End users agreed to strictly follow procurement process in future transactions.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer in ensuring accurate assessment of business taxes by following the proper procedures as specified in the Local Treasury Operation Manual (LTOM) and by checking of books of accounts and/or ledger of business establishment with corresponding Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales and Income Tax Return in accordance to Items (d) & (e) of Section 2 C.04 of the Municipal Revised Revenue Code of 2016. Moreover, correct the erroneous interpretation in the computation of taxes based on the tax table.
observations.managementAction: Computation of business taxes were already based on correct schedule
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to render an accounting on the result of recently conducted physical inventory and reconcile differences of the two records.
observations.managementAction: Records were already reconciled and necessary reports were already submitted
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer coordinates with the Municipal Assessor to produce the financial records needed that will constitute the correct basis for the recording of the RPT/SET Receivables. Moreover, instruct her staff to update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer's index cards to facilitate the accurate balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list as the basis in adjusting the books of accounts as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were effected and setting/recording of RPT receivables were already based on the latest guidelines.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management for the full implementation of the projects so that the desired services and benefits be delivered to its beneficiaries in a timely manner. Further, employ regular monitoring mechanisms in assessing the rate of progress of project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.
observations.managementAction: Continuing Appropriations were given priority in the implementation
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.
observations.managementAction: The position created for LDRRMO II had been filled up last May and the LDRRMO I was republished and posted for competitive assessment of applicants
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the MDRRM Officer to formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRM Plan which will be used as the basis in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the PPAs are in accordance with the national, regional and provincial frameworks.
observations.managementAction: MDRRM Plan formulated, adopted and Implemented
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Vice Mayor make a representation to the VMLP Bohol Chapter to have the amount paid as annual dues to the league be reviewed; taking into consideration the needs of each municipality, the pandemic that may have cancelled the activities planned for CY 2020, thus, expected to have left material unexpended fund balance of the VMLP; and suggest that it be lessened to pave way for other necessary expenditures of the respective agency.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Management already made a representation before the VMLP Bohol Chapter to have the amount paid as annual dues to the league be reviewed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to make representation to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation of taxes due them from the exercise of corporate power in the LGU’s Chocolate Hills Complex.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to review the validity vis-à-vis the possibility of claim of the outstanding payables and cause the reversion of those without valid claim to the unappropriated surplus. The management is also reminded to be stringent in assessing expenses to be obligated at year end.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Management has conducted review on the validity of the claims and caused the reversion before yearend.  The remaining outstanding payables have actual claims and with or without legal issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to make representation to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation of taxes due them from the exercise of proprietary functions listed in its Revenue Code of 2015.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Business registration of Carmen Municipal Economic Enterprises with BIR were already undertaken.  Computation of taxes due from the exercise of propriety functions are continuing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management submit a written explanation on the acceptance of medicines worth ₱1,038,764.72 having dosage lower than that being ordered thus, non-compliant to the PO product specification.   Further, explanation on the defect of the CPRs of the medicines delivered is likewise required.  Otherwise, the same transactions are subject to audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Appropriate measures are in place in order to oblige suppliers to deliver procured medicines in accordance with the dosage specified in the Purchase Orders.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Drivers to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels for each vehicle certified by him and approved by the Administrative Officer for verification by the Auditor.  Also, a Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted on a monthly basis by the Agency personnel concerned in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61 to determine the reasonableness of the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Municipal Drivers were required to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels for each vehicle certified by him and approved by the Administrative Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the remaining long outstanding balance of ₱4,726,739.83 for errors and misclassifications and to effect the necessary adjustments in order to present a reliable balance of the Due to LGUs account and be fairly presented in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Reconciliation of balances is on-going and necessary adjustments were made before yearend.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management update its records of all the low-income and vulnerable sectors of the society to ensure that they are the actual and valid recipients of any cash assistance extended by the government in times of crisis situation such as the Covid-19 pandemic.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | The MSWD office in coordination with barangay officials is in the process of validating and updating its records of all the low-income and vulnerable sectors of the society.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to improve the subsequent Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plans by stating more specific programs, activities, projects (PAPs) which will serve as a straightforward guide for all officers-in-charge for easily implementation without further deliberations and redundant planning.
observations.managementAction:
Finding No. 7, AAR 2018
Finding No. 9, AAR 2017 | The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. The MDRRMC created a committee that will evaluate and oversee the disbursement of the calamity fund and then present to the above council the problems encountered and possible solutions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require Collecting Officers to turn over all their collections particularly RPT intact daily or not later than the next working day to the Liquidating Officer and issue ORs in strict numerical sequence.  Moreover, installation of the ETRAC System is highly recommended to improve and control the management of government funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Collecting officers were already instructed to turn over collections intact daily.  Management is in the process of undertaking preparatory steps in the installation of ETRAC System.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that proper planning be done so not to delay the project implementation due to lack of authority of the LCE to enter into contract with contractors.  Prospectively, we recommend the BAC to scrutinize contractors and be efficient in the procurement planning process and may blacklist erring contractors.  Lastly, strictly have the contract signing within the period set in Sections 37.2.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R. A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Coordination among concerned offices were strengthened to improve the linkages between project planning and implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.   Otherwise, abolish these items from the Annual Budget and replace them with positions/items necessary for the proper discharge of the Municipality of its functions, targets and programs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Management already initiated filling up the vacant some positions yet not all to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require justification from the officials who inspected the four (4) projects with total cost of ₱11,806,220.18, listed earlier, and furnish the same to the Audit Team for verification.  Further, we also recommend immediate remedy of the hazardous situation of the Municipal Evacuation Center so that the constituents can benefit the purpose for which it was constructed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Management immediately coordinated with the contractor to rectify the end flashing portion of the roofing.  With regards the uneven elevation of the roof ridge, it was decided by the LCE that the slope of the roof should be increased to avoid side lapping leak of the roof.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Treasury, Budgeting and Accounting Departments to work hand in hand and evaluate all available historical data leading to the identification of proper adjusting entries to correct the Cash in Bank Balance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 | The recommendation was well taken by the management. The accounting office in coordination with budget and treasury office tried to locate the missing vouchers but went in vain. The said missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents were not submitted/forwarded by concerned personnel to the accounting office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to create a well-planned detailed engineering plan and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all projects to ensure completion of programmed projects as scheduled.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 5, AAR 2019 | According to the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer, the LCE already instructed all technical personnel relative to plan preparation, program and design to clearly indicate every minor detail to every infrastructure projects to be procures.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to reconcile balances with concerned barangays and demand from them liquidation of the funds transferred for project implementation.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 8, AAR 2018 | Monitoring and reconciliation of accounts is on-going. Hiring of additional manpower compliment is under consideration by the Municipal Mayor. Proposed appropriation of additional manpower complement was included in the CY 2022 Annual Investment Plan.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that with the assistance of the MPDC projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the constituents and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices in materials and feasibility of the projects in order to address the needs of the Municipality.  Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects attune with the present needs and constraints.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 12, AAR 2017 | The management have tried to comply with COA recommendations. However, we cannot fully or 100% utilized the said fund at the end of the year considering the procurement process. The said long process will take time, as well as, the requirement of Certificate of Availability of Funds (CAF) in government procurement hinder us to comply with a higher fund utilization performance. Most of the procurements which start with its implementation at mid-year are paid on the following year. But if we performance were based on the physical accomplishments, maybe management can hit the 80% accomplishment mark.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management fast track the development of the waterworks system to provide full service to its constituents and start collecting the fees thereafter to maintain the service.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 13, AAR 2017 | Installation of Water Billing and Collection System is on-going in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management hire two more capable staff to complement the existing LDRRM Officer and a staff.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 8, AAR 2016 | Management already hired a staff and has been in search for another one to complete the team and is considering one of the trainees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require the Municipal Accountant to review these accounts, make adjusting entries if necessary and abide by provisions of COA Circular 97-001.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 12, AAR 2016 | Management stated that the biggest chunk of non-moving accounts is beyond their control. These are the P11M cash shortage of the former treasurer who is currently under litigation and the liquidated intelligence fund which credit notice is yet to be issued. On the other hand, the management expressed their intent to coordinate with COA representative in conducting inventory count after which the non-moving inventories will be closed. These inventories are perceived to be consumed but remained unadjusted in the books. Moreover, the management will also demand liquidation from the barangays for the financial assistance given.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to explain why it took the Local School Board more than seven months to approve CY 2016 Budget.  Henceforth, require that the SEF budgets are prepared and approved timely and regularly in pursuance to Section 7 of R. A. 5447.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 4, AAR 2016 | The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prospectively monitor each stage of project work item implementation so that the actual output thereof would be in accordance with the approved plans and specifications, thus, value for money is maximized and audit disallowances would be avoided in the future.  Correspondingly, we likewise recommend that the amount of ₱33,220.72 be deducted from the final payment yet to be made to the contractor, in order to effect the deficiencies noted in the conduct of inspection.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 6, AAR 2014 | Management is set to deduct the amount however final payment is yet to be made.  The BAC already called the attention of the contractor to add or complete the specified length of the reshaping works as programmed.

observations.recommendation:
Submit an explanation for the much delayed implementation of the projects resulting to the incurrence of interest expense of ₱2,396,553.52 and financial charges amounting to ₱305,225.90 to the disadvantage to the government.

Conduct a substantive study on how to maximize the loan usage and hasten the project implementation.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 8, AAR 2013 | According to the MPDC, These projects were initiated by the previous administration. The present Municipal Mayor however would rather prioritize income generating projects rather that these pending projects which have lesser impact on the municipality.  Based on the assessment of the current administration, other projects were not implemented because they were impractical e.g. swimming pool. Besides, the 1st tranche accomplishment requirements were not met for the second tranche to be released.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management should take proper action on government properties that are for disposal.  The General Services Officer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.  In the meantime, the Municipal Accountant should reclassify other unserviceable properties to Other Assets account while awaiting the disposal of such assets amounting to ₱3,949,410.61.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 3, AAR 2013 | Management has started to dispose unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Property Custodian to maintain the Property Cards to be her basis in the preparation of the Annual Inventory Report and adhere to Sections 123 to 125 of New Government Accounting System Volume I in order to properly monitor and control the properties of the municipality.
Instruct the Inventory Committee to perform the actual physical count.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 7, AAR 2012 & Finding No. 1, AAR 2011 | Physical count and property tagging are almost done for most of the office equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management henceforth:

Conduct an immediate investigation on the circumstances relating to the missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents and determine the persons responsible.
Send immediately letters to Municipal Treasurer, Fulgencio V. Paña to produce the disbursement vouchers covering these issued and negotiated checks.
If the previous Municipal Treasurer fails to produce the missing vouchers and based on the results of the investigation, the amount of the disbursement vouchers should be added to his accountability and file appropriate charges against him and the responsible officials and staff.
Determine if the check payments are valid and proper, if so require the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to validate the transactions and projects by retrieving the corresponding supporting documents, ALOBS and Accountant’s Advice and reconstruct said transactions for proper recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2010 | The accounting office in coordination with budget and treasury office tried to locate the missing vouchers but went in vain. The said missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents were not submitted/forwarded by concerned personnel to the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation and/or demand letters to individuals or the business establishment concerned.
For receivables from officials and employees arising from overpayment of claims or under remittance of collections, the Municipal Treasurer shall institute deductions on their subsequent claims and salaries in satisfaction of their indebtedness pursuant to the provision of Section 37 of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2009 | To be implemented in the ensuing year CY2020 P118,996.00 remained dormant due to an appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to return the unexpended balances of PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury and secure official receipt thereof.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 1, AAR 2015 | There was a request by the management to DBM for realignment of PDAF.  Still waiting for DBM’s favorable advice relative to request for realignment. Report is already submitted to DBM as dormant account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prospectively monitor every delivery of materials/items in each stage of project work item implementation so that the actual output thereof would be in accordance with the approved plans and specifications, thus, value for money is maximized and audit disallowances would be avoided in the future. Correspondingly, we recommend that the amount of ₱67,436.00 be deducted from the final payment yet to be made to the contractor, in order to effect the deficiencies noted in the conduct of inspection.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 5, AAR 2014 | According to the Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson, the contractor was already called up to replace the oversized materials and instructed them to deliver only the size as specified.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management deduct the liquidated damages of ₱89,765.24 from the final payment to the contractor in order to effect the deficiencies noted during the inspection. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management ensure the proper implementation and timely execution of the terms of the approved project contract so that the target completion date will be attained which leads to the opportune availment of the constituents of the benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 7, AAR 2014 | The project incurred some suspension and resumption orders during unworkable days due to heavy rain.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of P 100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007. Henceforth, fund transfers to NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts of the LGU in accordance with the same COA Circular in order to afford proper review and evaluation of the utilization of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 17, AAR 2014 | The Municipal Accountant commented that the Health District Inc. is not an NGO nor a PO but a GOCC since it was incorporated by the representatives of 10 LGUs and 1 Province. The management suggested that to better account their contributions, the auditor in charge to the GOCCs in Bohol should include the Health District in their audit. Moreover they can’t discontinue the contribution since it will be a breach to the memorandum of the agreement among the members.

observations.recommendation:
Verify the Memorandum of Understanding or Memorandum of Agreement entered into with the donor-agencies to ascertain the disposition/treatment of the balances of the financial assistance received.  
Transfer the unexpended balances in accordance with Sections 98 and 99 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 4, AAR 2013 | The recommendation was well taken by the management. These accounts have existed even before the assumption of the previous Municipal Accountant and can be traced as far back as 1980s.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.
Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.
Require the Leagues concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-laws as basis of reviewing bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 5, AAR 2012 | Management already issued this concern to the higher officials of the leagues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit duly approved payrolls covering the subject disbursements totaling ₱39,918,000.00 for confirmation and validation by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the duly approved payrolls to the audit team for verification.
observations.reason: The required payrolls were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit explanation on the aforementioned observation to justify that the prices of the medical supplies and devices availed were the most advantageous to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required justification.
observations.reason: An acceptable explanation was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts to correct the understatement of depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation. A copy of the journal entry voucher (JEV) shall be provided to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had made accounting adjustments.
observations.reason: A journal entry voucher was submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant follow the provisions of the aforementioned BIR memorandum circular to ensure that accurate amounts of taxes are computed and withheld from all payments to VAT/Non-VAT registered suppliers.
observations.managementAction: The computation of withheld taxes for CY 2021 was in accordance with BIR regulations.
observations.reason: Errors on computation of withheld taxes were no longer noted during CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to explain in writing, together with the required supporting documents, the non-compliance of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, and to prospectively comply with the said law, to avoid suspension or disallowance.
observations.managementAction: BAC had submitted explanation.
observations.reason: Deficiencies were no longer noted in CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC comply with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurements under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as required in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.   Henceforth, we enjoined the BAC to be continually updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid issues of non-compliance in the future.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted a justification on the observations noted.
observations.reason: Deficiencies noted were not anymore observed during CY 2021 audit.

observations.managementAction: Management had implemented the recommendation. However, some items in the form could not be filled in since some of the vehicle’s meters/reading instruments were no longer functioning.
observations.reason: Compliance with the audit recommendations were validated during CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant require the aforementioned supporting documents from all departments that facilitated the receipt and distribution of donations in-kind, prepare the required summary/list and submit to the audit team. The one-time separate reports for cash and in-kind donations shall also be prepared and submitted to the NDRRMC through the OCD, copy furnish the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: Management had submitted the required Summary/List to the Audit Team. However, the one-time report for donations, to be received by the NDRRMC, was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit explanation on the noted deficiencies and provide an approved payroll/s for the unsupported claims totaling ₱96,000.00 if there are any, otherwise, the returned claims shall be duly receipted and returned to the DSWD.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO had identified and confirmed 12 SAP beneficiaries. The remaining four SAP beneficiaries are yet to be identified.  The MSWDO is appealing for consideration to give ample time to validate and trace the four unidentified SAP beneficiaries.
observations.reason: The MSWDO had submitted a copy of the payrolls for the identified beneficiaries. However, a total of four  beneficiaries remained unidentified.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the amount in excess of P5,000.00 of PEI received, otherwise all those who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable.

Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of EO No. 80 as implemented by Budget circular No. 2013-3 and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: On-going refund of PEI through payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Partial settlements observed through payroll deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the statutory positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) dated April 4, 2014 be filled up immediately by the Municipality, instead of designating existing employees to comply with NDRRMC–DILG)–DBM–CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management currently has one personnel hired for the mandatory position. Staff support of the MDRRMO is mostly composed of JOs, specifically for the emergency/ ambulance services.
observations.reason: As confirmed with the DRRM Officer, one position was already filled in, A mandatory position was posted but hiring is currently on hold due to the election ban. Additional  required position is being processed and for posting on CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit a valid explanation as to the deficiencies noted to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: An explanation was submitted.
observations.reason: This audit observation is subject of a complaint.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the reconciling items be fully supported and appropriately/timely recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The accounts were reconciled.
observations.reason: Long oustanding reconciling items were still noted in the BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the MPOC Resolution approving the Municipality’s POP and the release of the CF duly approved and signed by at least two-thirds (2/3) of its members in accordance with the applicable provision of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01, otherwise a notice of suspension will be issued if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Minutes of the meeting was attached referencing Resolution No. 00x-2021 for the approved release and utilization of the confidential fund.
observations.reason: Copy of the resolutions were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the documents supporting a particular transaction before signing Certificate A of the voucher.

Submit the pertinent supporting documents to the transactions involving ₱19,480,960.09, otherwise, subject transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We further recommended that management see to it that all claims against the municipal funds are covered with duly supported complete documentation to evidence actual disbursements and delivery of items allegedly procured.

A repetition of the same will be dealt with in accordance with prescribed laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management had partially submitted the documents.
observations.reason: Partial compliance with the submission of required documents was observed.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the noted deficiencies be corrected and the required documents submitted immediately for validation. Thereafter, all the officers involved in the processing of claims should see to it that the supporting papers are complete and proper, before these are paid.
observations.managementAction: Management had partially submitted the documents.
observations.reason: Partial compliance with the submission of required documents was observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to return the unexpended balances of PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury and secure official receipt thereof.
observations.managementAction: There was a request by the management to DBM for realignment of PDAF.  Still waiting for DBM’s favorable advice relative to request for realignment. Report is already submitted to DBM as dormant account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prospectively monitor every delivery of materials/items in each stage of project work item implementation so that the actual output thereof would be in accordance with the approved plans and specifications, thus, value for money is maximized and audit disallowances would be avoided in the future. Correspondingly, we recommend that the amount of ₱67,436.00 be deducted from the final payment yet to be made to the contractor, in order to effect the deficiencies noted in the conduct of inspection.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson, the contractor was already called up to replace the oversized materials and instructed them to deliver only the size as specified.  Management is set to deduct the amount however final payment is yet to be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management deduct the liquidated damages of ₱89,765.24 from the final payment to the contractor in order to effect the deficiencies noted during the inspection. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management ensure the proper implementation and timely execution of the terms of the approved project contract so that the target completion date will be attained which leads to the opportune availment of the constituents of the benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The project incurred some suspension and resumption orders during unworkable days due to heavy rain.  Management is set to deduct the amount however final payment is yet to be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of P 100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007. Henceforth, fund transfers to NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts of the LGU in accordance with the same COA Circular in order to afford proper review and evaluation of the utilization of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: The management has studied this concern and found out that an existing memorandum of agreement between members is in place and stopping the release of their contribution would be a breach to the said memorandum. Further, the P100,000 paid is in a nature of an annual contribution/dues of LGU Carmen to the Carmen Health District, Inc. a duly recognized GOCC by the SEC under registration no. C200100299.

observations.recommendation:
Verify the Memorandum of Understanding or Memorandum of Agreement entered into with the donor-agencies to ascertain the disposition/treatment of the balances of the financial assistance received.  
Transfer the unexpended balances in accordance with Sections 98 and 99 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was well taken by the management. These accounts have existed even before the assumption of the previous Municipal Accountant and can be traced as far back as 1980s.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.
Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.
Require the Leagues concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-laws as basis of reviewing bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was well taken by the management. However, the management is not the proper authority to implement the issuance of receipts of the Republic of the Philippines in behalf of the concerned leagues. This issue should be settled by the leagues themselves, the local government merely pays the required amount of the leagues.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the completeness of documents in drawing cash advances to support the payments and ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Management still submitted cash advances with lacking documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation:
Utilize the 20% MDF to its optimum level by identifying projects which are capital expenditure in nature in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes.

Specify in the Municipal Resolution/Ordinance or AIP the projects to be implemented, otherwise, it will fall as generic and may require proper authority from the Sangguniang Bayan.
Comply strictly with the submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dump in accordance with Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: The LGU ensures the proper disposal of Solid Waste by alternative means
observations.reason: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management update its records of all the low-income and vulnerable sectors of the society to ensure that they are the actual and valid recipients of any cash assistance extended by the government in times of crisis situation such as the Covid-19 pandemic.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to review the validity vis-à-vis the possibility of claim of the outstanding payables and cause the reversion of those without valid claim to the unappropriated surplus. The management is also reminded to be stringent in assessing expenses to be obligated at year end.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the remaining long outstanding balance of ₱2,883,049.00 for errors and misclassifications and to effect the necessary adjustments in order to present a reliable balance of the Due to LGUs account and be fairly presented in the Financial Statements.

We shall appreciate submission as soon as possible of the following overdue Reports on the Physical Count of:
Inventories (RPCI)
Investment Property (RPCIP)
Land and Land Improvements (RPCLLI)
Local Road Network (RPCLRN)
Other Public Infrastructure (RPCOPI)
Buildings and Structures (RPCBS)
Equipment, Furniture and Fixtures and Other PPE (RPCEFFOPPE)
Henceforth, it is recommended that management abide by the deadline of submission of these reports pursuant to Sections 67 to 73 of the GAM, Volume II to substantiate the existence of the municipality’s inventories, property, plant and equipment.

We recommend management to require Collecting Officers to turn over all their collections particularly RPT intact daily or not later than the next working day to the Liquidating Officer and issue ORs in strict numerical sequence.  Moreover, installation of the ETRAC System is highly recommended to improve and control the management of government funds.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Drivers to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels for each vehicle certified by him and approved by the Administrative Officer for verification by the Auditor.  Also, a Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted on a monthly basis by the Agency personnel concerned in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61 to determine the reasonableness of the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses.

We recommend that proper planning be done so not to delay the project implementation due to lack of authority of the LCE to enter into contract with contractors.  Prospectively, we recommend the BAC to scrutinize contractors and be efficient in the procurement planning process and may blacklist erring contractors.  Lastly, strictly have the contract signing within the period set in Sections 37.2.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R. A. 9184.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to make representation to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation of taxes due them from the exercise of proprietary functions listed in its Revenue Code of 2015.

We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.   Otherwise, abolish these items from the Annual Budget and replace them with positions/items necessary for the proper discharge of the Municipality of its functions, targets and programs.

We recommend that management require justification from the officials who inspected the four (4) projects with total cost of ₱11,806,220.18, listed earlier, and furnish the same to the Audit Team for verification.  Further, we also recommend immediate remedy of the hazardous situation of the Municipal Evacuation Center so that the constituents can benefit the purpose for which it was constructed.

We recommend that the Municipal Vice Mayor make a representation to the VMLP Bohol Chapter to have the amount paid as annual dues to the league be reviewed; taking into consideration the needs of each municipality, the pandemic that may have cancelled the activities planned for CY 2020, thus, expected to have left material unexpended fund balance of the VMLP; and suggest that it be lessened to pave way for other necessary expenditures of the respective agency.

We recommend that the management submit a written explanation on the acceptance of medicines worth ₱1,038,764.72 having dosage lower than that being ordered thus, non-compliant to the PO product specification.   Further, explanation on the defect of the CPRs of the medicines delivered is likewise required.  Otherwise, the same transactions are subject to audit disallowances.

We recommended that:
The said lacking documentary requirements as shown in the attached schedule (Annex F) be submitted;
The Management comply with the posting requirements provided under GPPB-TSO Advisory No. 05-2020 on the procurement projects undertaken through Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act; and
The Municipal Accountant meticulously review and ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all related disbursements in accordance with the applicable laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Budget Officer-Designate and/or Acting Municipal Treasurer post the monthly reports in another conspicuous public place in the locality as provided in Item 4.3 of the DBM LBC No. 125; and
Send written notice on the posting of reports to other oversight bodies as provided in Item 4.4 of the same LBC.

We recommended that the Supply/Property Unit:
Maintain Property/Equipment Cards for each type of PPE;
Render a report of all defective or non-functional PPE items for proper recording and disposal using the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); and
Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE and devise some measures to reconcile such report with the accounting records, so that the reliability of the balances of PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.

We recommended that:
The Management create an Inventory Committee that is composed of two or more employees including the Supply/Property Officer who will conduct physical count of all inventories which shall be the basis in preparing the RPCI;
The Municipal Accountant and the Supply/Property Officer maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, for each type of supplies and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance and disposal, to easily determine their balances at the end of the year; and
