The Accounting Unit and the Supply/Property Unit should reconcile their records at the end of each quarter.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant, Treasurer, and end-users to agree on procedures that can resolve the issue on compliance on the timely submission of DVs and its supporting documents; and
The Management submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers, duplicate copies of checks and supporting documents, and the original copies of the reported cancelled checks in accordance with Section 100 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2019.

We recommended that:
Management ensures strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the liquidation of cash advances;
The Municipal Accountant issue a demand letter to the accountable officers who are no longer connected to the Municipality, requiring him/her to liquidate all long outstanding advances; and
File the appropriate request for authority to write-off dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the completeness of documents in drawing cash advances to support the payments and ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 97-002.

We recommended that the Management ensure that the utilization of 20% DF is compliant to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 1 in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements are supported with complete documentary requirements as required in COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and submit the identified lacking documents, the Property Officer - Designate to make certain that the Purchase Orders are  properly filled-out, and the Inspection Officer - Designate and the Property Officer - Designate to check that the Inspection Reports bear the complete data prior to signing the said reports.

We recommended that Management:
Require the monthly deduction from the monthly compensation of employees the loan amortizations due to GSIS and remit the same in accordance with Sections 14.2 and 14.3 of RA No. 8291 to avoid the occurrence of delinquent loan repayments from members; and
Encourage the employees/personnel listed with delinquent loan repayments to request from the GSIS for loan restructuring to lessen the burden of paying penalties/surcharges, if applicable.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer:
Observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting of inventories;
Prepare and maintain the required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports, such as SLC, SC, RIS and RSMI, as prescribed in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs; and
Conduct periodic physical count of inventories to determine any necessary adjustments in the books and inventory records.

We recommended that the GSO-designate ensure the submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, and completely accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JV) to adjust the properties below the capitalization threshold and its related accounts, as follows:
Account					Debit			Credit
Government Equity 			 694,320.31
Accumulated Depreciation Accounts	 683,567.19
PPE Accounts					1,377,887.50
To drop non-capitalizable PPEs from CY 1993 to 2020
Ensure that all future acquisitions of properties below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000 will be recorded in accordance with Section 5.4 of COA Circular 2016-006 and Chapter II Section 4.ee of GAM for LGU Volume I.

We recommended that Management be updated with the new guidelines that are necessary for the operations, especially during the Emergency Phase. Prepare and issue Acknowledgement Receipt for In-Kind Donations to all future receipts to acknowledge the donations and for proper accounting and recording. Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations or any proof of receipt must be prepared to prove the distribution to the intended recipient. And monthly reports on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations for all in-kind donations by a private entity in compliance with COA Circular 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020, and strictly follow the provisions thereto.

We recommended that Management direct LDRRM Officer to be updated with the new issuance related to DRRM policies and guidelines. Prepare and submit the one-time report of the donations received, distributed and balances to the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the Auditor’s office as required in COA Circular 2020-009.

We recommended the Management coordinate with the personnel from different departments involved in the specific operation to have the appropriate flow of the needed documents. Require the MSWDO and/or the LRDDMO to immediately submit the complete master/distribution list of beneficiaries of the said program to the office for further assessment and examination of the transaction's validity.
We recommended the Management instruct the LDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to secure Income tax Returns and Omnibus Sworn Statements of the suppliers pursuant to GPPB Circular 01-2020.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to locate the missing documents and submit the same to our office for verification.

Hence, we recommended that Management stop the practice of transferring accountability of cash advance from one Accountable Officer to another pursuant to Section 4.1.6 of COA Circular 97-002 and observed the documentary requirements to support the liquidation as cited in Section 5.10 of the same Circular. Strictly observe the guidelines on the granting and liquidation of cash advances to avoid future suspensions/disallowances in the audit.

Thus, we recommended that Management stop the practice of procuring various goods through cash advances and direct the Bids and Awards Committee to abide by the standards set forth by procurement law and its recent issuances such as COA-GPPB JMC No. 1 and GPPB Circular 01-2020 for COVID-19 emergency procurements.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the balances of the Cashbook and General Ledgers.  Identify the items that need adjustment and record them appropriately in the affected books.  Likewise, require the accountable officers to reconcile their cashbook balances with the accounting records at least quarterly pursuant to the Handbook of cash examinations, so that adjustments, if any can be promptly taken up in the affected books and correctness of the balances assured.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the balances was already conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to strictly impose the withholding of salary on employees with cash advances which are not liquidated within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: With consideration to the P5,000.00 net take home pay limit, management strictly observed the policy of no additional cash advance granted unless the previous cash advance is liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to send demand letter to those that are no longer in active government service.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already sent to those that are no longer in active government service.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management include and monitor the timely releasing of resolutions as a major final output (MFO) for the SB Secretariat’s Division Performance Commitment and Review (DPCR).  Henceforth, require the SB Secretariat to abide by Section 37.3 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of the Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: According to the SB Secretary, it has been incorporated in the DPCR of the SB Secretariat and also been stipulated in the Internal Rules of Procedures of the SB 2019-2022 specifying the time frame.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management include and monitor the timely submission of municipal ordinances to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan as a Major Final Output (MFO) for the SB Secretariat’s Division Performance Commitment and Review (DPCR).
observations.managementAction: According to the SB Secretary, it has been complied as reflected in the received copy from the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly ensure that nobody’s take home pay will be below ₱4,000.00 as prescribed. The management should as well assess carefully the effects of additional loan applications on the employee’s net take home pay considering their existing deductions before issuing certifications and clearances.
observations.managementAction: Additional loan applications were carefully assess to ascertain its effects on employee’s net take home pay before issuing certifications and clearances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the personnel concerned in the preparation of the Purchase Order as well as the Acceptance and Inspection Report should indicate all the necessary information especially the delivery terms, received date of both Purchase Orders and Acceptance and Inspect Report.
observations.managementAction: The personnel concerned were directed to indicate all necessary information in both Purchase Orders and Acceptance and Inspection Reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to take advantage of the 10-day period following each month to record, reconcile and improve the monitoring before making the payment on time.
observations.managementAction: The management has been paying timely the billings from GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the personnel concerned in the preparation of the Purchase Order as well as the Acceptance and Inspection Report should indicate all the necessary information especially the delivery terms, received date of both Purchase Orders and Acceptance and Inspect Report.
observations.managementAction: The personnel concerned were directed to indicate all necessary information in both purchase orders and acceptance and inspection reports.
observations.reason: As evident in the transmittals, some purchase orders with petty amount were overlooked due to the volume of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require the details of Official Receipts be entered in the Treasury Department.  Moreover, require the ECPAC focal person to link data from the Treasury Department to the Accounting Department to reduce redundancy in data entry.  Otherwise, install the ETRACS (Enhanced Tax Revenue Assessment and Collection System) which is free software available to local government units to computerize tax and revenue assessments and collection pending migration of the accounting system from the ECPAC to PPSAS in CY 2017.
observations.managementAction: The accounting department has already stopped the duplicate entry of OR details in ECPAC, however, the system of both departments are not yet linked.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management review the procurement process to determine the bottleneck and expedite the processing of payments.
observations.managementAction: Review on the procurement process uncovered that the bottleneck was due to suppliers/contractors failure to submit proof of timely and complete payment of taxes to BIR in compliance with by some EO no. 2005-398.  According to the BAC Chairperson, they have instructed the contractor to collect payment immediately after the final acceptance of their work accomplishment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to explain the overpayment.  Otherwise, the total amount of ₱36,493.00 is subject to disallowance.  Henceforth, remit only the amount that is withheld against employees’ salary and immediately offset all the overpayments, if any in the next remittances.
observations.managementAction: Management has remitted only the amounts withheld from employees’ salaries. | Computations reflected in the vouchers on loan payments and remittances were coming from the Accounting Office.  All checks issued for remittances were based on the approved voucher supported by the master list of payments issued from the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to ask a formal approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) on the use of above bank accounts based on the provisions in DOF Circular 001-2005 and transfer immediately any amount in excess of the authorized cash balance to any GFIs in Section 5.2.
observations.managementAction: Section 5.2 of DOF Department Circular No.01-2017 Amended Guidelines on the Authorized Government Depository Banks.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to avail of the remedies by administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action. Steps are properly enumerated in section 254 to 270 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Sec. 260 of RA 7160 was implemented and conducted the Sale or Auction of delinquent real properties last March 14, 2018 & March 20, 2019.  There are other factors that affected the total amount of real property tax collectibles like, duplication of tax declaration, CARP covered properties and other assessment matters.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly the balances of liabilities due to government agencies/corporations concerned in order not to compromise the benefits/due the intended beneficiaries and at the same time avoid penalties/surcharges arising from late payments. Henceforth, regularly remit all trust liabilities of like nature on the required due dates.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of vouchers supported with remittance list is done by the accounting office. Remittances of liabilities due to government agencies/corporations are based on the approved vouchers issued with checks.  For this, only those trust liabilities with approved vouchers were remitted by the Municipal Treasurer to concern agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
Require the municipal engineer to make a study on how to rehabilitate and improve the waterworks system.
Appropriate funds for the rehabilitation/improvement of the quality of water distributed by the waterworks system and eventually implement new water rates equitable enough for the quality of potable water supply distributed.
observations.managementAction: Rehabilitation and Improvement of Waterworks System is still on-going.  It has purchased a lot which is a good source for potable water.   It has also implemented various projects to improve the waterworks system.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that proper sanctions as stipulated in the contracts, like liquidated damages, blacklisting of poor or non-performing contractors, and others, be strictly imposed upon defaulting contractors.  We also recommend to the Municipal Engineer’s Office to validate the actual accomplishment of the Construction In Progress projects.
observations.managementAction: According to the BAC Chairperson, the erring contractors were already banned to enter into any LGU procurement.  Based on the assessment of the current administration, other projects were not implemented because they were impractical e.g. swimming pool. Besides, the 1st tranche accomplishment requirements were not met for the second tranche to be released.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Treasury, Budgeting and Accounting Departments to work hand in hand and evaluate all available historical data leading to the identification of proper adjusting entries to correct the Cash in Bank Balance.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was well taken by the management. The accounting office in coordination with budget and treasury office tried to locate the missing vouchers but went in vain. The said missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents were not submitted/forwarded by concerned personnel to the accounting office.
observations.reason: The accounting office in coordination with budget and treasury office tried to locate the missing vouchers but went in vain. The said missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents were not submitted/forwarded by concerned personnel to the accounting office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to create a well-planned detailed engineering plan and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all projects to ensure completion of programmed projects as scheduled.
observations.managementAction: According to the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer, the LCE already instructed all technical personnel relative to plan preparation, program and design to clearly indicate every minor detail to every infrastructure projects to be procures.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to make representation to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation of taxes due them from the exercise of corporate power in the LGU’s Chocolate Hills Complex.
observations.managementAction: It was agreed during the Exit Conference that the Municipal Treasurer together with an Accounting staff will make representations with the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to reconcile balances with concerned barangays and demand from them liquidation of the funds transferred for project implementation.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring and reconciliation of accounts is on-going. Hiring of additional manpower compliment is under consideration by the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that with the assistance of the MPDC projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the constituents and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices in materials and feasibility of the projects in order to address the needs of the Municipality.  Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects attune with the present needs and constraints.
observations.managementAction: According to the MPDC, management can not 100% utilize the 20% Development Fund considering the long procurement process.  Mostly procurement process starts at mid-year and payments made the following year.  However, if we base implementation on the physical accomplishment, management may be able to hit the 80% accomplishment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to improve the subsequent Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plans by stating more specific programs, activities, projects (PAPs) which will serve as a straightforward guide for all officers-in-charge for easily implementation without further deliberations and redundant planning.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. The MDRRMC created a committee that will evaluate and oversee the disbursement of the calamity fund and then present to the above council the problems encountered and possible solutions.
observations.reason: Report on Utilization of DRRM Funds showed that unutilized seventy percent (70%) allocation for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery has increased as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management fast track the development of the waterworks system to provide full service to its constituents and start collecting the fees thereafter to maintain the service.
observations.managementAction: Installation of Water Billing and Collection System is on-going in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.
observations.reason: The management has yet to report income from waterworks in the statement of financial performance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management hire two more capable staff to complement the existing LDRRM Officer and a staff.
observations.managementAction: Management hired 12 Job Order workers to act as a medical response unit.  Moreover, management has published the one vacant position on February 11, 2021.
observations.reason: The annual budget of the municipality tagged the positions as vacant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require the Municipal Accountant to review these accounts, make adjusting entries if necessary and abide by provisions of COA Circular 97-001.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of balances was conducted and made necessary adjusting entries based on certifications issued by the concerned barangays that the project was fully implemented and physical complete. Further, the biggest chunk of non-moving accounts is beyond management control. These are the P11M cash shortage of the former treasurer who is currently under litigation and the liquidated intelligence fund which credit notice is yet to be issued.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of balances was conducted and made necessary adjusting entries based on certifications issued by the concerned barangays that the project was fully implemented and physical complete. Further, the biggest chunk of non-moving accounts is beyond management control. These are the P11M cash shortage of the former treasurer who is currently under litigation and the liquidated intelligence fund which credit notice is yet to be issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to explain why it took the Local School Board more than seven months to approve CY 2016 Budget.  Henceforth, require that the SEF budgets are prepared and approved timely and regularly in pursuance to Section 7 of R. A. 5447.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management.
observations.reason: Budget for CY 2020 was approved on December 2, 2019 similarly with the Budget for CY 2019 which was approved on December 7 of the preceding year compared with the Budget in 2017 which was approved in March of the same year showed improvement. However, the improvement in budget preparation still did not meet the timeline set which should be not later than Oct 31 of the preceding year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prospectively monitor each stage of project work item implementation so that the actual output thereof would be in accordance with the approved plans and specifications, thus, value for money is maximized and audit disallowances would be avoided in the future.  Correspondingly, we likewise recommend that the amount of ₱33,220.72 be deducted from the final payment yet to be made to the contractor, in order to effect the deficiencies noted in the conduct of inspection.
observations.managementAction: Management is set to deduct the amount however final payment is yet to be made.  The BAC already called the attention of the contractor to add or complete the specified length of the reshaping works as programmed.
observations.reason: Management is set to deduct the amount however final payment is yet to be made.

observations.recommendation:
Submit an explanation for the much delayed implementation of the projects resulting to the incurrence of interest expense of ₱2,396,553.52 and financial charges amounting to ₱305,225.90 to the disadvantage to the government.

Conduct a substantive study on how to maximize the loan usage and hasten the project implementation.
observations.managementAction: According to the MPDC, these projects were initiated by the previous administration.  The present Municipal Mayor however would rather prioritize income generating projects rather than these pending projects which have lesser impact on the municipality. Based on the assessment of the current administration, other projects were not implemented because they were impractical e.g. swimming pool. Besides, the 1st tranche accomplishment requirements were not met for the second tranche to be released.  The Municipal Engineer added that the second tranche of the loan release were no longer materialized due to lack of supporting documents.  The management did not report any further activity and procurement related to the said loan.
observations.reason: The management did not report any further activity and procurement related to the said loan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management should take proper action on government properties that are for disposal.  The General Services Officer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.  In the meantime, the Municipal Accountant should reclassify other unserviceable properties to Other Assets account while awaiting the disposal of such assets amounting to ₱3,949,410.61.
observations.managementAction: Management has started to dispose unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Property Custodian to maintain the Property Cards to be her basis in the preparation of the Annual Inventory Report and adhere to Sections 123 to 125 of New Government Accounting System Volume I in order to properly monitor and control the properties of the municipality.
Instruct the Inventory Committee to perform the actual physical count.
observations.managementAction: Physical count and property tagging are almost done for most of the office equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management henceforth:

Conduct an immediate investigation on the circumstances relating to the missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents and determine the persons responsible.
Send immediately letters to Municipal Treasurer, Fulgencio V. Paña to produce the disbursement vouchers covering these issued and negotiated checks.
If the previous Municipal Treasurer fails to produce the missing vouchers and based on the results of the investigation, the amount of the disbursement vouchers should be added to his accountability and file appropriate charges against him and the responsible officials and staff.
Determine if the check payments are valid and proper, if so require the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to validate the transactions and projects by retrieving the corresponding supporting documents, ALOBS and Accountant’s Advice and reconstruct said transactions for proper recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office in coordination with budget and treasury office tried to locate the missing vouchers but went in vain. The said missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents were not submitted/forwarded by concerned personnel to the accounting office.
observations.reason: The accounting office in coordination with budget and treasury office tried to locate the missing vouchers but went in vain. The said missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents were not submitted/forwarded by concerned personnel to the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
Compute depreciation expense for Road Networks account.

Prepare the RPCPPE by classifying the PPE according to their type.

Ensure the inclusion of all necessary and relevant information in the RPCPPE, and that all the required fields in the RPCPPE form are properly and completely filled out.

Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE and devise some measures to reconcile such report with the accounting records, so that the reliability of the balances of PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: The Management still submitted an incomplete RPCPPE. However, reconciliation is ongoing between the concerned offices.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to reconcile the Cash in Bank account balances with the Treasurer’s cash books, and;

Thereafter, conduct quarterly reconciliation of such balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be adjusted and posted in their respective books.

Advise the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation and submission of the financial reports and Bank Reconciliation Statements and adhere to the prescribed period for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.

Moreover, provide additional manpower to the Accounting Office to facilitate timely generation of financial reports.
observations.managementAction: Submission of financial reports was still not on time. The Management provided additional manpower to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
Advise the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the requirements on the prescribed period for submission of the required reports and transaction documents to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.

Moreover, provide additional manpower to the Accounting Office to facilitate timely submission of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Submission of financial transactions and corresponding supporting documents were still not on time. The Management provided additional manpower to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer, and end-users to agree on procedures that can resolve the issue on compliance on the submission of DVs and its supporting documents especially the inspection reports, ORs, payrolls and etc.

Submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers, duplicate copies of checks and supporting documents, and the original copies of the reported cancelled checks in accordance with Section 100 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2019.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted some of the lacking paid DVs and supporting documents and cancelled checks.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to those erring accountable officers, including those retired/transferred employees to settle their outstanding cash advances. 

For long outstanding cash advances with less to no chance of being settled caused by death or whose whereabouts could no longer be traced, we reiterate our recommendation to file a request for authority to write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The amount of unliquidated cash advances may have decreased, however, long outstanding cash advances were not acted upon. The Management is processing requirements of the request for authority to write-off dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Ensure that future realignment of funds or projects be done by way of appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget and be transmitted to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review.

Likewise, submit a justification why the realignment or transfers of appropriations were not covered by an appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget.

Comply strictly to the provisions in the revised IRR of RA 9184 especially on the requirements in undertaking projects by administration. 

Instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to consider the availability of tools, heavy equipment and construction equipment to be used or if the Municipality have access to such tools and equipment owned by other government agencies before undertaking projects by administration.

Direct the MDRRM Council to include in the LDRRMF Investment plan under a separate caption the programs, projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, and review and update the plan annually to increase the disaster-resilience capability of the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: Since the LGU has no sufficient funds for implementation of all PPAs, the Management will carry out yearly implementation until fully implemented PPAs funded under 20% MDF.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Economic Enterprise Development and Management Officer/Municipal Treasurer to submit monthly to the Accounting Office the uncollected rentals so that the latter can record its receivables on time.

Direct the Market Supervisor to strengthen collection among the stallholders in its market and bus terminal operations and impose strictly what was stipulated in the lease contracts.  

Ensure that the renewal and notarization of the contracts of lease of stallholders be complete with the details required so that accurate computation of the amounts of rentals due from stallholders can be determined.
observations.managementAction: The Management collected rentals, set-up receivables in the books of accounts for CY 2020, and maintained ledgers for stallholders.

observations.recommendation:
Review the GAD Plan and Budget to identify programs, projects and activities based on the results of gender analysis and gender assessment, taking into account the identified priorities of the LGU and the gender issues and concerns faced by the LGU’s constituents and employees; and 

Conduct Gender Sensitivity Training, Gender Analysis, Gender-responsive Planning and Budgeting and hire gender specialists/experts to capacitate the   LGU GAD Focal Point System’s on Gender and Development.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted Focused Group Discussion (FGD).

observations.recommendation: Identify and specify in the plan, the priority programs, projects and activities including the required materials, resources and equipment, and its beneficiaries or location and ensure its implementation to attain the LGU’s goal of having a disaster-resilient community.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entries and to ensure recording of depreciation once a project has been completed.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted through drawing of JEV – June 2020
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary journal entry to correct the stale checks in the books and revert it back to Cash in Bank.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted through drawing of JEV – March 2020
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to strictly comply with budgetary limitations to ensure that actual expenditures for Personal Services are within the 55% limitation to avoid financial dislocations.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer in ensuring accurate assessment of business taxes by following the proper procedures as specified in the Local Treasury Operation Manual (LTOM) and by checking of books of accounts and/or ledger of business establishment with corresponding Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales and Income Tax Return in accordance to Items (d) & (e) of Section 2 C.04 of the Municipal Revised Revenue Code of 2016. Moreover, correct the erroneous interpretation in the computation of taxes based on the tax table.
observations.managementAction: Computation of business taxes were already based on correct schedule
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to render an accounting on the result of recently conducted physical inventory and reconcile differences of the two records.
observations.managementAction: Records were already reconciled and necessary reports were already submitted
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer coordinates with the Municipal Assessor to produce the financial records needed that will constitute the correct basis for the recording of the RPT/SET Receivables. Moreover, instruct her staff to update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer's index cards to facilitate the accurate balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list as the basis in adjusting the books of accounts as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: Recording/Setting of RPT receivables for 2020 is based on the actual number of RPTARs as properly and accurately computed by the office of the Municipal Assessor that includes the delinquent RPTs.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management for the full implementation of the projects so that the desired services and benefits be delivered to its beneficiaries in a timely manner. Further, employ regular monitoring mechanisms in assessing the rate of progress of project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.
observations.managementAction: The procurement process is strengthened to fast track the implementation of the project.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.
observations.managementAction: The position created for LDRRMO II had been filled up last May and the LDRRMO I was republished and posted for competitive assessment of applicants
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all implementing offices to submit Quarterly Reports on Government Projects, Programs and Activities from all sources to the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator who will then consolidate the reports for submission to municipal officials for monitoring and decision-making purposes.
observations.managementAction: MPDC has required copies of Quarterly reports to concerned offices
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the MDRRM Officer to formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRM Plan which will be used as the basis in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the PPAs are in accordance with the national, regional and provincial frameworks.
observations.managementAction: Formulation Plan is on process
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend to stop the practice of charging the payments of LBP loan amortization to the 20% development fund because although such expenditures partake the nature of capital expenditures, it is administrative in nature. Adhere strictly to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the Road Network Account.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant not to provide for residual value in the computation of depreciation for Road Networks and prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended that Management provide necessary appropriation to facilitate the processing of titles of lots of the Municipality to safeguard real properties and to document owning ownership over the said properties.
observations.reason: Titling of land still in process with Registry of Deeds; The Municipality already reached the maximum limit for owning agricultural lands (should not be more than 5 hectares); Conversion of land takes a long time to process. Reiterated in Finding No. 2 of Part II of the report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe and comply with the provisions prescribed in Sec. 325(h) of RA 7160.

We recommended that: The management intensify its information campaign and enforce implementation of all existing regulations on the segregation of solid waste at the barangay level; and The Municipal Government strictly monitor and provide assistance to barangays in the proper establishment and operation of their Materials Recovery Facility.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government strictly monitor and provide assistance to barangays in the proper MRFs in all barangays; The Secured Landfill Facility (SLF) was included in the budget and the personnel in-charge attended seminar on how to develop SLF.
observations.reason: The DENR closed the dumpsite.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management fast tract the implementation of the programs/projects of the municipality so that beneficiaries can enjoy the projects intended to them. Make a representation to the source agency for the continuation of the project.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate submission of the update list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS and to require the Municipal Finance Committee to include in its annual appropriation the amount necessary to cover the premiums for the insurance of its properties.
observations.managementAction: Documentary requirements submitted still in-process

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management that immediate appropriate action to dispose the unserviceable properties be undertaken to avoid further deterioration and possible loss of their value.
observations.managementAction: Disposal process still ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management send Demand Letters to debtors to intensify collections of overdue accounts. If these efforts prove futile, the LGU should document all action undertaken to recover the accounts and the head of the agency should seek authority from the members of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to write off the doubtful accounts. Once the legislative body approves, submit the listings of doubtful accounts together with SB Resolution to the COA and request authority for its writing-off supported by a list of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were served to debtors who had difficulty locating residents of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The municipality conducts continuous collection of these accounts. Some debtors no longer located; municipality plans to request write-off for those accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management return to the respective source agencies the unutilized balance of funds for projects which are already fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: Unutilized balances were returned for some projects.
observations.reason: Some NGAs were already devolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management include and monitor the timely releasing of resolutions as a major final output (MFO) for the SB Secretariat’s Division Performance Commitment and Review (DPCR). Henceforth, require the SB Secretariat to abide by Section 37.3 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of the Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommend that management include and monitor the timely submission of municipal ordinances to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan as a Major Final Output (MFO) for the SB Secretariat’s Division Performance Commitment and Review (DPCR).

We recommend the management to improve the subsequent Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plans by stating more specific programs, activities, projects (PAPs) which will serve as a straightforward guide for all officers-in-charge for easily implementation without further deliberations and redundant planning.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMC creates a committee that will evaluate and oversee the disbursement of the calamity fund and then present to the above council the problems encountered and possible solutions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management review the procurement process to determine the bottleneck and expedite the processing of payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to return the unexpended balances of PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury and secure official receipt thereof.
