observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of P100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007. Henceforth, fund transfers to NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts of the LGU in their Audit.
observations.managementAction: The management studied this concern and found out that the Health District Inc. is not a NGO nor a PO but a GOCC since it was incorporated by the representatives of 10 LGUs and 1 Province. The accountant corrected that the Health District Inc. is not a NGO. The management suggested that to better account their contributions, the auditor in charge to the GOCCs in Bohol should include the Health District in their Audit. Moreover, they can’t discontinue the contribution since it will be a breach to the memorandum of agreement among the members.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, the Municipality obligated through AP2018-12-072 the amount of ₱100,000.00 for Carmen Health District Inc.

observations.recommendation: 1. Verify the Memorandum of Understanding or Memorandum of Agreement entered into with the donor-agencies to ascertain the disposition/treatment of the balances of the financial assistance received. 2. Transfer the unexpended balances in accordance with Sections 98 and 99 of PD 1445.
observations.reason: These accounts have existed even before the assumption of the previous Municipal Accountant and can be traced as far back as 1980s.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that proper sanctions as stipulated in the contracts, like liquidated damages, blacklisting of poor or non-performing contractors, and the others, be strictly imposed upon defaulting contractors. We also recommend to the Municipal Engineer’s Office to validate the actual accomplishment of the Construction In Progress projects.
observations.managementAction: These projects were initiated by the previous administration. The present Municipal Mayor however would rather prioritize income generating projects rather than these pending projects. Based on the assessment of the current administration, other projects were not implemented because they were impractical e.g. swimming pool. Besides, the 1st tranche accomplishment requirements were not met for the second tranche to be released.
observations.reason: No further progress reported; management prioritized other projects; some projects impractical and tranche requirements not met.

observations.recommendation: 1. Submit an explanation for the very delayed implementation of the projects resulting to the incurrence of interest expense on of ₱2,396,553.52 and financial charges amounting to ₱305,225.90 to the projects. 2. Conduct a substantive study on how to maximize the loan usage and hasten the project implementation.
observations.managementAction: These projects were initiated by the previous administration. The present Municipal Mayor however would rather prioritize income generating projects rather than these pending projects. Based on the assessment of the current administration, other projects were not implemented because they were impractical e.g. swimming pool. Besides, the 1st tranche accomplishment requirements were not met for the second tranche to be released.
observations.reason: No further action reported; tranche requirements not met; projects impractical.

observations.recommendation: a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I. b. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. c. Require the Leagues concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-laws as basis of reviewing bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: Management already issued this concern to the higher officials of the leagues. The management is not the direct authority to implement the issuance of receipts of the Republic of the Philippines in behalf of the concerned leagues.
observations.reason: Payments to leagues are supported with customized official receipts for the leagues, not accountable form 51. Issue should be settled by the leagues themselves.

observations.recommendation: a. Conduct an immediate investigation on the circumstances relating to the missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents and determine the persons responsible. b. Send immediately letters to Municipal Treasurer, Fulgencio V. Paña to produce the disbursement vouchers covering these issued and negotiated checks. c. If the previous Municipal Treasurer fails to produce the missing vouchers and based on the results of the investigation, the amount of the disbursement vouchers should be added to his accountability and file appropriate charges against him and the responsible officials and staff. d. Determine if the check payments are valid and proper, if so require the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to validate the transactions and projects by retrieving the corresponding supporting documents, ALOBS and Accountant’s Advice and reconstruct said transactions for proper recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant tried to locate the missing vouchers but in vain.
observations.reason: Balances remain to be a reconciling item in the bank reconciliations. Reiterated in Part II Finding No. 3.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the statutory positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) dated April 4, 2014 be filled up immediately by the Municipality, instead of designating existing employees to comply with NDRRMC–DILG)–DBM–CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
Require the Supply Officer-designate to prepare and submit SSMI to the Accounting Office for the recording of the issuance of the supplies; maintain Stock Cards and RPCI.

Conduct semestral physical count of inventory items by type and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
Comply strictly with the submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61.
We recommended that  management  strictly comply with the minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dump in accordance with Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU ensures strict observance of the provisions of the law

Dumpsite is validated in pursuant to EMB issuances
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2017

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to make
representation to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation of taxes
due them from the exercise of corporate power in the LGU’s Chocolate Hills
Complex.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the balances of the
Cashbook and General Ledgers. Identify the items that need adjustment and
record them appropriately in the affected books. Likewise, require the
accountable officers to reconcile their cashbook balances with the accounting
records at least quarterly pursuant to the Handbook of cash examinations, so
that adjustments, if any can be promptly taken up in the affected books and
correctness of the balances assured.

We recommend that the Treasury, Budgeting and Accounting
Departments to work hand in hand and evaluate all available historical data
leading to the identification of proper adjusting entries to correct the Cash in
Bank Balance.

We recommend the management to strictly impose the withholding of
salary on employees with cash advances which are not liquidated within the
prescribed period.

We recommend the management to send demand letter to those that are
no longer in active government service.

We recommend the management to create a well-planned detailed
engineering plan and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all
projects to ensure completion of programmed projects as scheduled.

We recommended that Management designate specific personnel to do the tasks of preparing a complete and detailed RPCPPE after conduct of annual physical count, and to assist the accounting department in the preparation of PPE ledger cards. A review of existing workforce may be feasible and select personnel with less workload who can be designated to handle these tasks so that these recurring deficiencies can be resolved efficiently.
We recommended that Management submit a valid explanation as to the deficiencies noted to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer ensure preparation of the As-Built Plans for the subject completed infrastructure projects totaling ₱17,963,348.20 and submit to the auditor for verification. Henceforth, As-Built Plans shall be required by the Municipal Accountant in the processing of final payments for all completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments for procurement of goods are duly supported with the required warranty security from the supplier in compliance with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, as amended by GPPB Resolution No. 30-2017, to safeguard the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the monitoring and assessment of the Municipal Engineer, ensure that the defects liability period or one year from date of completion has lapsed before finally issuing the Certificate of Final Acceptance, to guarantee that defects in the construction works within this period are rectified at the contractor’s expense.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the subsidiary ledgers of these accounts showing the details of receivables. Furthermore, follow the foregoing procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 to ensure that dormant receivables/fund transfers in the books are analyzed, verified, reconciled, provided with impairment loss and written-off accordingly for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the reconciling items be fully supported and appropriately/timely recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant work back on tracing entries to CIP accounts and ascertain the status of the infrastructure projects recorded. Completed projects shall be immediately classified to proper PPE account and its corresponding accumulated depreciation computed and recorded. A copy of the JEV, supporting documents and schedules shall be provided to the auditor for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor follow the provisions of Section 219 of RA 7160 and undertake the procedures for the general revision of real property assessments. Prospectively, regular general revision for every three years as required by the law shall be undertaken.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant obtain a formal estimate of recoverable service amount for the unserviceable items, compare with the carrying amount and make as basis in providing an allowance for impairment. Disclosures shall be provided in the financial statement in accordance with this standard. Management shall also consider disposal of the above items.

We recommended that Management revisit the provisions of the existing Revenue Code of the Municipality of Carmen to come up with amendments for the interest of the local government and its constituents.

We recommended that the Accounting Office strictly observe Section 350
of Local Government Code of 1991 on accounting for  obligations  and  proper
recording of Account Payable  as  defined  in  COA  Circular  No.  2015-009.
Furthermore, no obligations will be recorded unless  items  were  completely
delivered  and  received.  Any  payment  made  without  recognition  of   an
obligation may be disallowed/suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  create  an  Inventory
Committee to conduct an inventory of road networks in  compliance  with  the
requirement of COA  Circular  No.  2015-008  and  submit  the  corresponding
reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

We  recommended  that  the  Disbursing  Officer  stamps   “PAID”   all
supporting documents and provide space for the number of  the  check  issued
and date of actual payment after payment is made to prevent  the  re-use  of
the same on similar transactions thereby strengthening the internal  control
system of the Municipality.

We recommended that the:


   a. Municipal Accounting Office require the recipient LGUs to  submit  the
      Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and  the  Report  of  Disbursement  (RD)
      within ten (10) days after the end of each  month/end  of  the  agreed
      period for the Project;


   b. Management submit to the Office of the Auditor the copies of  all  the
      MOAs covering fund transfers to other LGUs;


   c.  Municipal  Accountant  conduct  regular  and  periodic  verification,
      analysis and validation of the existence  of  all  fund  transfers  to
      other LGUs; and

   d. Management require the Implementing  Agencies  to  return  any  unused
      balance for the completed projects.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant and/or Treasure to:

   1. Return immediately the P374,837.51 unused balances received  from  the
      Source Agencies and furnish, accordingly, the Audit Team a copy of the
      pertinent deposit slip;  henceforth,  all  unspent  balances  of  fund
      transfers should be returned to the SA unless  otherwise  provided  by
      law/ Memorandum of Agreement;


   2. Immediately submit to the office of the auditor the liquidation report
      of the P56,002.00 funds received from the DOH; and


   3. Prepare appropriate adjusting entries including the balance due to PTO
      to correct the recorded balance of Due to Other NGAs account.

We recommended that charges to  20%  Development  Fund  shall  be  for
projects  which  contribute  to  social   and   economic   development   and
environmental management in accordance with pertinent  provisions  of  DILG-
DBM JMC No. 2017-1 to  preclude  disallowances  in  an  audit.  Purchase  of
administrative office furniture, fixtures, or appliances  are  appropriately
charged against the General Fund.

We recommended that charges to  20%  Development  Fund  shall  be  for
projects  which  contribute  to  social   and   economic   development   and
environmental management to preclude disallowances in an  audit  and  attain
optimum  utilization  of  the  fund  for  socio-economic   development   and
environmental management.

We recommended that responsible officials of the Municipality  observe
the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 on the application of  the
5% LDRRMF to attain full utilization and avoid the incurrence  of  irregular
expenditures resulting in possible audit suspensions/disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD  Focal  Person
to submit  the  GAD  Plan  and  Budget  after  approval.  Also,  ensure  the
submission by the implementing offices  of  the  report  on  the  status  of
implementation of each project and  activities  funded  with  GAD  funds  to
enable the timely consolidation  of  accomplishment  reports  by  the  focal
person,  and  eventually  its  submission  to  the  Audit  Team  within  the
prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  enjoin  the  concerned
officials to ensure the annual preparation of the required reports,  conduct
of inventory of roads and submission of  the  corresponding  report  of  the
physical inventory taken to the Office of the Auditor within the  prescribed
period.

We recommended the following course of action:

   a. Employees employed by the Municipality rendering less than 8 hours per
      day shall be compensated through honorarium basis; and

   b. The Municipality should apply the qualification  standard  requirement
      in identifying  the  rightful  workers  vis-à-vis  their  compensation
      consistent with the directive of the said wage order.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  enjoins  the  Municipal
Treasurer, Local Economic Enterprise Officer, and the  Municipal  Accountant
to  maintain  and  update  the  records  of   market   rental   receivables.
Afterwards, send demand  letters  and  aggressively  enforce  collection  by
utilizing the remedies provided in the lease contract and  revenue  code  of
the Municipality.

We recommended the following course of action:

   1. The Local  Chief  Executive  cause  the  execution  of  renewed  lease
      contract,  containing  therein,  among  others,  the  provisions   for
      transfer of lease rights, penalty for non-payment of rentals  and  the
      rights and duties of the parties;


   2.  The  Local  Chief  Executive  enforce  the  prohibitions  and   penal
      provisions in the contract;


   3. The Local Chief Executive enjoin the Local Economic Enterprise Officer
      to keep an updated list of market stall lessees, noting  therein  that
      which   are   still   occupied   by   the    original    lessees    or
      transferred/subleased to others; and


   4. The Sanguniang Bayan reconsider the amendment of the Revenue  Code  of
      the Municipality, adjusting rental fees and examining the provision of
      the lease contract taking  into  consideration  the  current  economic
      condition of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting
entries to reflect the correct balance of the Road Network Account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant not to provide for residual
value in the computation of depreciation for road network and prepare the necessary
adjusting entries.

We recommended that Management should provide necessary appropriation to
facilitate the processing of land titles of lots of the Municipality to safeguard real
properties and to document ownership over the said properties.

We recommended that management observe and comply with the provisions
prescribed in Sec. 325(h) of RA 7160. Appropriations and actual expenses for EME
in the ensuing year should be within the limitation.

We recommended that the management intensify its information campaign and
enforce implementation of all existing regulations on the segregation of solid waste at
the barangay level; and
The Municipal Government strictly monitor and provide assistance to barangays
in the proper establishment and operation of their Materials Recovery Facility.

Compute depreciation expense for Road Networks account.
Prepare the RPCPPE by classifying the PPE according to their type.
Ensure the inclusion of all necessary and relevant information in the RPCPPE, and that all the required fields in the RPCPPE form are properly and completely filled out.
Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE and devise some measures to reconcile such report with the accounting records, so that the reliability of the balances of PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to reconcile the Cash in Bank account balances with the Treasurer’s cash books, and;
Thereafter, conduct quarterly reconciliation of such balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be adjusted and posted in their respective books.

Advise the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation and submission of the financial reports and Bank Reconciliation Statements and adhere to the prescribed period for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.
Moreover, provide additional manpower to the Accounting Office to facilitate timely generation of financial reports.

Advise the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the requirements on the prescribed period for submission of the required reports and transaction documents to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.
Moreover, provide additional manpower to the Accounting Office to facilitate timely submission of accounts.

Require the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer, and end-users to agree on procedures that can resolve the issue on compliance on the submission of DVs and its supporting documents especially the inspection reports, ORs, payrolls and etc.
Submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers, duplicate copies of checks and supporting documents, and the original copies of the reported cancelled checks in accordance with Section 100 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2019.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to those erring accountable officers, including those retired/transferred employees to settle their outstanding cash advances.
For long outstanding cash advances with less to no chance of being settled caused by death or whose whereabouts could no longer be traced, we reiterate our recommendation to file a request for authority to write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the completeness of documents in drawing cash advances to support the payments and ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 97-002.

Utilize the 20% MDF to its optimum level by identifying projects which are capital expenditure in nature in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes.
Specify in the Municipal Resolution/Ordinance or AIP the projects to be implemented, otherwise, it will fall as generic and may require proper authority from the Sangguniang Bayan.

Ensure that future realignment of funds or projects be done by way of appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget and be transmitted to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review.
Likewise, submit a justification why the realignment or transfers of appropriations were not covered by an appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget.

Comply strictly to the provisions in the revised IRR of RA 9184 especially on the requirements in undertaking projects by administration.
Instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to consider the availability of tools, heavy equipment and construction equipment to be used or if the Municipality have access to such tools and equipment owned by other government agencies before undertaking projects by administration.

Direct the MDRRM Council to include in the LDRRMF Investment plan under a separate caption the programs, projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, and review and update the plan annually to increase the disaster-resilience capability of the Municipality.

Require the Municipal Economic Enterprise Development and Management Officer/Municipal Treasurer to submit monthly to the Accounting Office the uncollected rentals so that the latter can record its receivables on time.
Direct the Market Supervisor to strengthen collection among the stallholders in its market and bus terminal operations and impose strictly what was stipulated in the lease contracts.
Ensure that the renewal and notarization of the contracts of lease of stallholders be complete with the details required so that accurate computation of the amounts of rentals due from stallholders can be determined.

Review the GAD Plan and Budget to identify programs, projects and activities based on the results of gender analysis and gender assessment, taking into account the identified priorities of the LGU and the gender issues and concerns faced by the LGU’s constituents and employees; and
Conduct Gender Sensitivity Training, Gender Analysis, Gender-responsive Planning and Budgeting and hire gender specialists/experts to capacitate the   LGU GAD Focal Point System’s on Gender and Development.
Comply strictly with the submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61.

Identify and specify in the plan, the priority programs, projects and activities including the required materials, resources and equipment, and its beneficiaries or location and ensure its implementation to attain the LGU’s goal of having a disaster-resilient community.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entries and to ensure recording of depreciation once a project has been completed.
We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary journal entry to correct the stale checks in the books and revert it back to Cash in Bank.
We recommended Management to strictly comply with budgetary limitations to ensure that actual expenditures for Personal Services are within the 55% limitation to avoid financial dislocations.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer in ensuring accurate assessment of business taxes by following the proper procedures as specified in the Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) and by checking of books of accounts and/or ledger of business establishment with corresponding Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales and Income Tax Return in accordance to Items (d) & (e) of Section 2C.04 of the Municipal Revised Revenue Code of 2016. Moreover, correct the erroneous interpretation in the computation of taxes based on the tax table.

We recommend management to require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and control the grant of cash advance to the Disbursing Officer by requiring the list of approved payrolls for each pay period to preclude excessive cash in his/her custody. Also, strictly require that cash advance be only granted if the previous cash advance has been fully liquidated. Cash advance for payroll fund shall be liquidated after the purpose have been accomplished specifically five days after each pay period and require the Disbursing Officer to refund the excess cash advance duly receipted to the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the AAR recommendation.
observations.reason: Nothing was found during the cash examination.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management be more efficient with their work force by eliminating and avoiding redundant positions. Otherwise, the related wages paid to redundant positions shall be subject to audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the AAR recommendation.
observations.reason: Market Supervisor post was filled in by Salvador Reyes, Casual Plantilla, and his salary was recorded to Salaries and Wages – Casual/Contractual 5-01-01-020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management utilize the budget intended for the market supervisory post and appropriately charge wages to the Salaries and Wages – Casual/Contractual account.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the AAR recommendation.
observations.reason: Market Supervisor post was filled in by Salvador Reyes, Casual Plantilla, and his salary was recorded to Salaries and Wages – Casual/Contractual 5-01-01-020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to reconcile balances with concerned barangays and demand from them liquidation of the funds transferred for project implementation.
observations.reason: Adjusting Journal Entry was effected to take up completion of projects and liquidation of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check at least one month before a check becomes stale and prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the cash in bank and accounts payable balances.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management.
observations.reason: Adjusting Journal Entry was effected to correct cash in bank and accounts payable balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management review the validity vis-à-vis the possibility of claim of the outstanding payables and cause the reversion in the total amount of ₱511,307.52 for GF and ₱45,175 for SEF as per JEV Nos. 100-2019-01-31582 to 31584, 200-2019-01-02216 dated 01-02-2019. The remaining outstanding payables have actual claims with and without legal issue.
observations.managementAction: Reversion was done in CY 2019 through JEV Nos. 100-2019-01-31582 to 31584, 200-2019-01-02216 dated 01-02-2019.
observations.reason: The remaining outstanding payables have actual claims with and without legal issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer updates her cashbook daily to monitor each of the balances of the LGU’s bank accounts and be cautious of any service charges which can actually be avoided with due diligence. The total penalty should be shouldered by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Automated cashbook system is already installed for accurate balances of various funds.
observations.reason: Adjusting Journal Entry was effected to record bank penalty to due from officers and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management retrace the entries made, perform reconciliation and make necessary adjusting entries to close the total amount of ₱1,605,496.22 to the appropriate account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant traced the entries made, perform reconciliation and make necessary adjusting entries to close the total amount of ₱1,605,496.22 to the appropriate account.
observations.reason: The account is already closed to prior period adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management retrace the entries made and review the source documents with which brought the Cash in Vault to a negative balance. Check if there were unrecorded cash receipts/inflows used to fund the overspending. Validate as well the related disbursements which caused the overdraft if these were valid. Finally, prepare adjusting entries when necessary.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant traced the entries made, through bank reconciliations discovered that these are proper deductions to the receivable from the former municipal treasurer supported with complete documents. The review with the management, supreme court. While waiting for the final decision, it is better to let the record as it is.
observations.reason: The negative cash in bank balance was already reclassified as Other Payables. As per certification from the Clerk of Court dated February 20, 2020, the status of litigation is pending before the supreme court. While waiting for the final decision, it is better to let the record as it is.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to strictly impose the withholding of salary on employees with cash advances which are not liquidated within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. Withholding of salary on employees with no outstanding cash advances was partially implemented subject to ₱5,000.00 minimum take home pay.
observations.reason: Payroll still shows very few unliquidated balances; thus reiterated in finding no. 7 of part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to send demand letter to those that are no longer in active government service.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. Demand letters were already sent to those that are no longer in active government service.
observations.reason: There are still no improvements in the collection; thus, reiterated in finding no. 7 of part II. Some of these advances were disclosed to be under Ombudsman proceedings.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly ensure that nobody’s net take home pay will be below ₱4,000.00 as prescribed. The management should as well assess carefully the effects of additional loan applications on the employee’s net take home pay considering their existing deductions before issuing certifications and clearances.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. Additional loan required applications were carefully assessed to ascertain its effects on employee’s net take home pay before issuing certifications and clearances.
observations.reason: Payroll still shows very few Employees with net take home pay below ₱5,000.00 though they expressly requested through a formal letter and waiver allowing the management to deduct their loans for personal convenience.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the personnel concerned in the preparation of the Purchase Order as well as the Acceptance and Inspection Report should indicate all the necessary information especially the delivery terms, received date of both Purchase Orders and Acceptance and Inspect Report.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. The petty personnel concerned were directed to indicate all necessary information in both purchase orders and acceptance and inspection reports.
observations.reason: As evident in the transmittals, some purchase orders with petty amount were overlooked due to the volume of transactions.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that with the assistance of the MPDC identified projects should be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the constituents and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices in materials and feasibility of the projects in order to address the needs of the Municipality. Conduct actual studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects attune with the present needs and constraints.
observations.managementAction: The management tried to comply with COA recommendations. With the procurement which hinders implementation, projects to proceed with the implementation at the beginning of the year. IRA is downloaded monthly to their account and needs to be accumulated to fund a certain project not to mention the lengthy procurement process from POW preparation to issuance of notice to proceed or purchase order. However, the management has been trying their best to at least achieve 80% physical implementation since most of the payments are made in the following year.
observations.reason: Out of the ₱30,097,995.44 allotment, only 39% was obligated as of December 31, 2019. Implementation are on-going but were yet to be billed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to create a well-planned detailed engineering plan and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all projects to ensure completion of programmed projects as scheduled.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendations were well taken by the management. The management has been careful in planning to minimize delays.
observations.reason: Variation orders are still issued for some projects though fewer compared to previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to explain why it took the Local School Board more than seven months to approve CY 2016 Budget. Henceforth, require that the SEF budgets are prepared and approved timely and regularly in pursuance to Section 7 of R. A. 5447.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management.
observations.reason: Budget for 2019 was approved in December 7 of the preceding year compared to the budget in 2017 which was approved in March of the same year. Improvement in budget preparation still did not meet the timeline which should be not later than Oct 31 of the preceding year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to take advantage of the 10-day period following each month to record, reconcile and improve the monitoring before making the payment on time.
observations.managementAction: The management has been paying timely the billings from GSIS.
observations.reason: Upon reconciliation, differences are still evident in the amount withheld for GSIS and payment remittances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management hire two more capable staff to complement the existing LDRRM Officer and a staff.
observations.managementAction: The management already hired a staff and has been looking for another one to complete the team is considering one of the trainees.
observations.reason: The annual budget of the municipality tagged the position as vacant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require the details of Official Receipts be entered in the Treasury Department. Moreover, require the ECPAC focal person to link data from the Treasury Department to the Accounting Department to reduce redundancy in data entry. Otherwise, install the ETRACS (Enhanced Tax Revenue Assessment and Collection System) which is free software available to local government units to computerize tax and revenue assessments and collection pending migration of the accounting system from the ECPAC to PPSAS in CY 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department has already stopped the duplicate entry of OR details in the ECPAC; however, the system of both departments are not yet linked.
observations.reason: The management will look into providing budget to purchase server to facilitate the link not only between Accounting and Treasury but with the Budget as well.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require the Municipal Accountant to review these accounts, make adjusting entries if necessary and abide by provisions of COA Circular 97-001.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken. Reconciliation is on-going. Demand letters were already sent to concerned barangays.
observations.reason: Management stated that the biggest chunk of non-moving accounts is beyond their control. These are the ₱11M cash shortage of the former treasurer who is currently under litigation and the liquidated intelligence fund which credit notice is yet to be issued. Other non-moving inventories perceived to be consumed but remained unadjusted in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to explain the overpayment. Otherwise, the total amount of ₱36,493.00 is subject to disallowance. Henceforth, remit only the amount that is withheld against employees’ salary and immediately offset all the overpayments, if any in the next remittances.
observations.managementAction: The management stated that these are timing differences due to the payment deadline. These are corrected in the succeeding month except for the beginning balances which are inherited from previous accountant that needed reconciliations.
observations.reason: Upon reconciliation, some accounts were found to differ from the withheld amount. These are being improved this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to ask a formal approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) on the use of above bank accounts based on the provisions in DOF Circular 001-2005 and transfer immediately any amount in excess of the authorized cash balance to any GFIs in Section 5.2.
observations.managementAction: Bank accounts in Private bank trust fund were mostly transferred to LBP, DBP and Postal Bank. FCB accounts as of Dec 31, 2019 have a total of approximately ₱30.7M (GF: ₱27.6M+ SEF: ₱2.5M+ TF: ₱.6M) compared to the allowed maximum maintaining balance of ₱15M for municipalities.
observations.reason: There are no authorized government depository banks near the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to avail of the remedies by administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action. Steps are properly enumerated in section 254 to 270 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Noted. Notices of Delinquency were demanded and received from previous and current years combined but not fully. Balances as of Dec 31, 2019 for Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax are ₱10.7M and ₱4.5M respectively.
observations.reason: Not fully collected; subsidiary ledger balances remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly the balances of liabilities due to government agencies/corporations concerned in order not to compromise the benefits/due the intended beneficiaries and at the same time avoid penalties/surcharges arising from late payments. Henceforth, regularly remit all trust liabilities of like nature on the required due dates.
observations.managementAction: The management has been paying monthly all the amounts provided by the national and corporate government.
observations.reason: Upon reconciliation, there are still balances to be reconciled. These are inherited balances from former accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management should take proper action on government properties that are for disposal. The General Services Officer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445. In the meantime, the Municipal Accountant should reclassify other unserviceable properties to Other Assets account while awaiting the disposal of such assets amounting to ₱3,949,410.61.
observations.managementAction: Management has started to dispose unserviceable properties. The management has disposed of some but not all with proceeds of ₱280,000.00. The remaining was already inspected by COA which is bound for auction. No additional activity was reported.
observations.reason: Some properties disposed with proceeds ₱280,000.00. Remaining inspected by COA and bound for auction; no further activity reported.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: 1. Require the municipal engineer to make a study on how to rehabilitate and improve the waterworks system. 2. Appropriate funds for the rehabilitation/improvement of the quality of water distributed by the waterworks system and eventually implement new water rates equitable enough for the quality of potable water supply distributed.
observations.managementAction: Management purchased a lot which is a good source for potable water. The waterworks system implemented various projects to improve the waterworks system.
observations.reason: The waterworks system implemented various projects; however, management yet to report income from waterworks in the statement of financial performance.

observations.recommendation: a. Instruct the Property Custodian to maintain the Property Cards to be her basis in the preparation of the Annual Inventory Report and adhere to Sections 123 to 125 of New Government Accounting System Volume I in order to properly monitor and control the properties of the municipality. b. Instruct the Inventory Committee to perform the actual physical count.
observations.managementAction: Physical count and property tagging are almost done for most of the office properties as the physical count of public infrastructures is still ongoing.
observations.reason: Physical count and property tagging are almost done; public infrastructures count still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation and/or demand letters to individuals or the business establishment concerned. 2. For receivables from officials and employees arising from overpayment of claims or under remittance of collections, the Municipal Treasurer shall institute deductions on their subsequent claims and salaries in satisfaction of their indebtedness pursuant to the provision of Section 37 of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management.
observations.reason: ₱118,996.00 remained dormant due to an appeal.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation: 
We recommended that Management stop the charging of projects not aligned with DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1 against the 20% Development Fund to avoid the sanctions provided under RA No. 7160 and other applicable laws.
observations.managementAction: Management referred to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular in planning the projects that are charged to the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace, review and analyze the identified abnormal balances so that the corresponding adjustments could be made to ensure the reliability and accuracy of its presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already made the necessary entries to correct the abnormal balances.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation: 
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous recording of obligations of the Municipality. Furthermore, we recommended that Management submit a copy of the signed Loan Agreement, together with the amortization schedules of the two (2) existing loans for further verification in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already corrected the loan account balance in the books.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
Concerned officials are required to refund the excess payment of their RATA. Furthermore, the management should refrain from implementing the new RATA rates until such time that the PS cap/ limitation shall have been observed.
observations.managementAction: Continued refund thru payroll deduction except those who retired or were separated from service.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 85-55-A and 2012-003. Also, exercise due prudence so that government funds are safeguarded and conserved to avoid unnecessary and extravagant expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Management had complied with the recommendations of COA.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the municipal accountant ensure that all DV for CF cash advance with complete supporting documents be submitted to the COA ATL within (7) days from release of the check pursuant to the above cited circular.  Failure or refusal to do so without justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for administrative disciplinary action pursuant to the Administrative Provisions/Penal Sanctions of the circular.
observations.managementAction: Timely submission of documents for cash advance for 2018 confidential fund.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that henceforth, Management comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30 2013 to promote transparency and accountability in government projects.
observations.managementAction: Installed signboards which are compliant to COA regulation

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC submit a valid explanation why the subject purchases appeared to have been split instead of a single procurement under competitive public bidding. Prospectively, comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to avoid disallowance
observations.managementAction: BAC had submitted an explanation and ensured no splitting of PRs on CY 2019.
observations.reason: No splitting observed on CY 2019. BAC cited that 2 of this requisitions were sourced from different funds and implemented due to its immediate need.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the accounting department recognize depreciation of the aforesaid PPE computed since the date of acquisition when the assets were already available for use in compliance with the accounting standards. In addition, adjustments shall also be provided for the over-depreciation of PPE after taking into account their salvage values.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already provided depreciation of the identified accounts.
observations.reason: Management had complied with the recommendation but the provisions for depreciation were all charged to current year 2019 which overstated the expenses. Another observation was issued for CY 2019 audit.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
It is recommended that the noted deficiencies be corrected and the required documents submitted immediately for validation. Thereafter, all the officers involved in the processing of the claims should see to it that the supporting papers are complete and proper, before they are paid.
observations.managementAction: Management had already submitted the required documents to COA.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
It is recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Units should strictly enforce the provisions of GAA 2010 pertaining to the statutory net pay to aid in the promotion of an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel will receive adequate salary for their daily subsistence. Also, all salary payments to persons other than the payee should be with written authority from the concerned payees to avoid future misunderstanding and/ or arguments.

Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher and be submitted to COA, together with the supporting documents, and reconciling items should be booked up immediately, in accordance with COA Circular 96-011.
observations.managementAction: Efforts had been exerted to locate the old records since the Municipality kept on transferring office upon the request of the new administration.  Management later found out that documents were eaten by termites including the manual Trial Balance prior to moving in to the new location. Accountant will try other means to locate data to be able to make adjustments to clear the remaining deficiencies for this account.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that all concerned Accountable Officers be required to immediately liquidate their cash advances whose purpose had already been served.  Strictly adhere to the aforementioned provisions of Sections 89 of Presidential Decree No. 14445 and provisions of Circular No. 97-002 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already enforced collection and liquidation of the old cash advances.
observations.reason: Cash advance other than for intelligence/ confidential purpose by a deceased official amounting to P86,337.00 was left unliquidated in the list, but will be applied for write off by Management once proven collection is not anymore feasible.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the RPCPPE prepared by the Municipality after the physical count, be detailed and complete so that information on the PPE items’ existence, status, condition and location can be reliable. A copy of the report shall be submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124, Volume 1 of the NGAS.

We also reiterated prior years’ recommendation that PPE Ledger Cards shall be maintained by the Municipal Accountant so that the recording, monitoring and accountability of the PPE can be assured.
observations.managementAction: Pending compliance by management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2019 audit.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that Management conduct competitive bidding or any alternative mode when justified by conditions in the procurement of catering services for its various activities following the guidelines prescribed in GPPB Resolution No. 12-2017.
observations.managementAction: BAC had already instructed the Department Heads to plan their procurement for catering services. Full compliance, however, started on August 2019.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
It is recommended that the noted deficiencies be corrected and the required documents submitted immediately for validation. Thereafter, all the officers involved in the processing of claims should see to it that the supporting papers are complete and proper, before these are paid.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office will verify the vouchers for compliance.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the amount in excess of P5,000.00 of PEI received, otherwise all those who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable.

Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of EO No. 80 as implemented by Budget circular No. 2013-3 and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: The excess of P5,000.00 was already booked-up and deductions were made thru the payroll.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to submit the duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names of the taxpayers and the tax due at the start of each year to the Municipal Accountant as basis for recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables in order to present reliable and accurate receivable and deferred income accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant requested from the Municipal Treasurer the submission of the certified list of taxpayers.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit the MPOC Resolution approving the Municipality’s POP and the release of the CF duly approved and signed by at least two-thirds (2/3) of its members in accordance with the applicable provision of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01, otherwise a notice of suspension will be issued if warranted.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC submit a valid explanation on the foregoing issues of non-compliance with the requirement on the prohibition of referencing brand names and on the required number of suppliers to be invited under the Negotiated Procurement mode (two failed biddings), to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that BAC submit a valid explanation on why the planned purchase of airfare was not procured in accordance with the regulations in RA 9184. We recommended further to discontinue the practice of reimbursement and employ the allowed modalities of procurement to assure that the objective of attaining the most competitive and advantageous price is achieved.
observations.managementAction: No action by management.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that all the provisions of the Philippine Bidding Documents are complied, such that all the required bidding documents are issued to the bidders and completely submitted during conduct of procurement of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through the BAC, submit a written explanation on why the regulations for the procurement of consultancy contract involving individual consultants were not observed.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant follow the provisions of the aforementioned BIR memorandum circular to ensure that accurate amounts of taxes are computed and withheld from all payments to VAT/Non-VAT registered suppliers.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation on the 2nd semester of CY 2019 after the conduct of the exit conference by COA.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
It is recommended that the noted deficiencies be corrected and the required documents submitted immediately for validation. Thereafter, all the officers involved in the processing of the claims should see to it that the supporting papers are complete and proper, before these are paid or else these would be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Will coordinate with the OIC Accountant during that time for compliance of the deficiencies noted.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts under the three funds and book the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement.  Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission.  Moreover, whenever possible, settle immediately transactions between funds to minimize efforts in monitoring and reconciliation and more importantly avoid erroneous account balances.
observations.managementAction: Management already started the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the documents supporting a particular transaction before signing Certificate A of the voucher.

Submit the pertinent supporting documents to the transactions involving P19,480,960.09, otherwise, subject transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We further recommended that management see to it that all claims against the municipal funds are covered with duly supported complete documentation to evidence actual disbursements and delivery of items allegedly procured.

A repetition of the same will be dealt with in accordance with prescribed laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: These were the transactions during the time of the previous accountant Ms. Vicenta Camongay, now Budget Officer. The Municipal Accountant will coordinate with her for the compliance of the deficiencies noted soon.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to explain in writing, together with the required supporting documents, the non-compliance of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, and to prospectively comply with the said law, to avoid suspension or disallowance
observations.managementAction: No management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the discrepancies noted in the Bank Reconciliation Statements be adjusted immediately to correct the ending balance of the Cash in Bank Accounts. Moreover, to furnish Bank Reconciliation Statements to the bank, especially if the identified bank error is for adjustment by the bank.

Municipal Treasurer be directed to prioritize the titling of those untitled lands in order to establish absolute ownership thereof, and for the Municipal Budget Officer to consider the funding requirement for titling of lands in the budget preparation for the ensuing year. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the unrecorded real assets and to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements other relevant information on the Land account.
observations.reason: Titling of land still in process

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to the following: a. The Municipality Government through the LDRRMO should conduct periodic review of the LDRRM Investment Plan and prepare a revise and updated Plan whenever reprogramming of projects or new expense item is introduced and integrated in the Revised AIP for approval of the LCE. b. Revisit the provision of the above cited laws, rules and regulations to enhance disaster preparedness and mitigation.

We recommended that management fast tract the implementation of the programs/projects of the municipality so that intended beneficiaries can enjoy the projects intended to them. Make a representation to the source agency for the continuation of the project.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate submission of the update list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS and to require the Municipal Finance Committee to include in its annual appropriation the amount necessary to cover the premiums for the insurance of its properties.
observations.reason: Appropriation for insurance premiums is insufficient to cover all properties; requirement of documentary process still in-process

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management that immediate appropriate action to dispose the unserviceable properties be undertaken to avoid further deterioration and possible loss of their value.

We recommended that the Management send Demand Letters to debtors to intensify collections of overdue accounts. If these efforts prove futile, the LGU should document all action undertaken to recover the accounts and the head of the agency should seek authority from the members of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to write off the doubtful accounts. Once the legislative body approves, submit the listings of doubtful accounts together with SB Resolution to the COA and request authority for its writing-off supported by a list of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts.
observations.reason: The municipal letters were served; difficulty in locating the debtors

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management return to the respective source agencies the unutilized balance of funds for projects which are already fully implemented.
observations.reason: Some balances were already devolved; some projects' NGAs already returned balances for some projects

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the management must secure local sanggunian authority/approval for all contracts/projects entered into/implemented by the local chief executive that were described in generic terms in the Appropriation Ordinance/AIP for CY 2017 to ratify the same and avoid disallowances pursuant to G.R. No. 175527 and COA Memorandum 2010-014. Furthermore, the Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Council should properly plan, prioritize and identify the specific programs/projects to be funded under the 20% EDF and incorporate the same in the AIP.

We recommended that the management ensure that all copies of approved contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted within five (5) working days from approval or issuance and within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries for review, evaluation and inspection of items procured by the Agency.

Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE so that reconciliation with accounting records can be attained, and so that reliability of the value of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: The Management still submitted an incomplete RPCPPE . However, reconciliation is ongoing between the concerned offices.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly refrain from granting cash advances without legally authorized specific purpose.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the erring accountable officers, including retired/transferred employees to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately. 

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officers and employees with unsettled cash advances, pursuant to Section 37 of Presidential Decree 1445. 

For long outstanding cash advances with less to no chance of being settled caused by death or whose Accountable Officer’s whereabouts could no longer be traced, the agency may file a request for authority to write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The amount of unliquidated cash advances may have decreased, however, long outstanding cash advances were not acted upon.

observations.recommendation:
Advise the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation and submission of the financial reports and adhere to the prescribed period for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.

Moreover, avail of the eNGAS from the COA to facilitate systematic generation of financial reports.
observations.managementAction: Submission of financial reports was not on time.

observations.recommendation: Advise the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the requirements on the prescribed period for submission of the required reports and transaction documents to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.
observations.managementAction: Submission of financial transactions and corresponding supporting documents were not on time.

observations.recommendation:
Refrain from charging non-related expenses to the MDRRM Fund and comply with the above-stated regulation in prioritizing expenses; and
Provide a separate appropriation for the maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay of the MDRRM Office.

Require the BAC/BAC Secretariat to thoroughly and meticulously review and validate the eligibility of the documents submitted by the suppliers before the issuance of the Notice of Award.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the required documents to support the propriety and regularity of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submitted the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from hiring consultant for legal matters instead follow the provision of Section 481 of RA 7160.

Stop the payment of prior year’s transaction charged to the current year’s appropriations as provided in the aforementioned provision.

Determine in advance all expenses incurred which are due and demandable in the current year which cannot be processed during the year to be recorded as Accounts Payable.

Likewise, the Budget Office and Accountant are advised to be strict in enforcing the provision.

Expedite the processing of these lacking/deficient documents, more importantly, the Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) and Tax Declaration in the name of the LGU, to protect the interest of the government.

Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the supporting documents in all transactions before certifying the completeness and propriety thereof. Require those in-charge of the project/program to attach the necessary documents as required in the claim.
observations.managementAction: Tax Declaration was already available and copy of which was submitted to COA, however, TCT was not yet transferred because the management planned to acquire in CY 2020 a lot adjacent to the property, thus the TCT shall be processed together.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that the SEF shall be utilized solely for its purpose; i.e., programs, projects and activities enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160, DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
Direct the GAD Focal Point System to use the HGDG and PIMME in monitoring and evaluating GAD programs and activities as required under JMC 2013-01.

Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment of a sustainable Sanitary Landfill consistent with the standards and criteria set under RA 9003 for the protection of the environmental and human health.
observations.managementAction:
Establishment of sustainable Sanitary Landfill is on- going.

Perimeter fence is existing

Ongoing construction of MRF and Vermiculture Shed.

Ongoing construction of PCA Building project for coconut husk waste management.

observations.recommendation: Comply the requirement of the DILG guidelines as provided and hasten the implementation of the projects.  Monitor the completion of the delayed project in 2017 for the immediate delivery of basic services as intended.
observations.managementAction: The fund was returned to DILG in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
Monitor the implementation of the planned developmental projects to ensure the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.  

Submit reasons why the projects were not implemented as planned.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to render an accounting on the result of recently conducted physical inventory and reconcile differences of the two records.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the Reconciled Report to COA

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management increase the fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer and other accountable officers enough to cover their accountability.
observations.managementAction: Bond required for accountable officers were increased and paid to the Bureau of Treasury

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer coordinates with the Municipal Assessor to produce the financial records needed that will constitute the correct basis for the recording of the RPT/SET Receivables. Moreover, instruct her staff to update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer's index cards to facilitate the accurate balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list as the basis in adjusting the books of accounts as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: Recording/Setting of RPT receivables for 2019 is based on the actual number of RPTARs as properly and accurately computed by the office of the Municipal Assessor that includes the delinquent RPTs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to stop the practice of charging the payments of LBP loan amortization to the 20% development fund because although such expenditures partake the nature of capital expenditures, it is administrative in nature. We recommend that management review the procurement process to determine the bottleneck and expedite the processing of payments.
observations.managementAction: The management conducted review on the procurement process and discovered the inability of the supplier or contractor to immediately provide updated licenses and tax clearance cause the delay of payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the MDRRMC to monitor and evaluate the use and disbursements of the Calamity Fund and to deliberate and discuss problems encountered by the LGU during actual disasters/calamities during the past year/s to be included the LDRRM Plan of the ensuing year.  Henceforth, maximize utilization of the pre disaster preparedness programs and/or activities so that the intended purpose is attained/achieved as provided in Rule 18 of RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. The MDRRMC creates a committee that will evaluate and oversee the disbursement of the calamity fund and then present to the above council the problems encountered and possible solutions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management fast track the development of the waterworks system to provide full service to its constituents and start collecting the fees thereafter to maintain the service.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require the Municipal Accountant to review these accounts, make adjusting entries if necessary and abide by provisions of COA Circular 97-001.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken. Reconciliation is on-going. Demand letters were already sent to concerned barangays. The management stated that the biggest chunk of non-moving accounts is beyond their control. These are the ₱11M cash shortage of the former treasurer who is currently under litigation and the liquidated intelligence fund which credit notice is yet to be issued. On the other hand, the management expressed their intent to coordinate with COA representative in conducting inventory count after which the non-moving inventories will be closed. These inventories are perceived to be consumed but remained unadjusted in the books. Moreover, the management will also demand liquidation from the barangays for the financial assistance given.

Correspondingly, we recommend that the amount of ₱67,436.00 be deducted from the final payment yet to be made to the contractor, in order to effect the deficiencies noted in the conduct of inspection.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of P 100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007. Henceforth, fund transfers to NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts of the LGU in accordance with the same COA Circular in order to afford proper review and evaluation of the utilization of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: The management has studied this concern and found out that an existing memorandum of agreement between members is in place and stopping the release of their contribution would be a breach to the memorandum. The accountant corrected that the Health District Inc. is not a NGO nor a PO but a GOCC since it was incorporated by the representatives of 10 LGUs and 1 Province. The management suggested that to better account their contributions, the auditor in charge to the GOCCs in Bohol should include the Health District in their Audit. Moreover, they can’t discontinue the contribution since it will be a breach to the memorandum of agreement among the members.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that proper sanctions as stipulated in the contracts, like liquidated damages, blacklisting of poor or non-performing contractors, and others, be strictly imposed upon defaulting contractors. Besides, the 1st tranche accomplishment requirements were not met for the second tranche to be released.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management henceforth:

Conduct an immediate investigation on the circumstances relating to the missing disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents and determine the persons responsible.
Send immediately letters to Municipal Treasurer, Fulgencio V. Paña to produce the disbursement vouchers covering these issued and negotiated checks.
If the previous Municipal Treasurer fails to produce the missing vouchers and based on the results of the investigation, the amount of the disbursement vouchers should be added to his accountability and file appropriate charges against him and the responsible officials and staff.
Determine if the check payments are valid and proper, if so require the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to validate the transactions and projects by retrieving the corresponding supporting documents, ALOBS and Accountant’s Advice and reconstruct said transactions for proper recording in the books.

More so, the Chief Accountant shall maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each category of assets.  The treasurer should properly classify/identify the PPEs thru the maintenance of property cards to ascertain its accuracy and existence and reconcile any difference with that of the records of the Municipal Accountant.
