observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and control the grant of cash advance to the Disbursing Officer by requiring the list of approved payrolls for each pay period to preclude excessive cash in his/her custody.  Also, strictly require that cash advance be only granted if the previous cash advance has been fully liquidated.  Cash advance for payroll fund shall be liquidated after the purpose have been accomplished specifically five days after each pay period and require the Disbursing Officer to refund the excess cash advance duly receipted to the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that management be more efficient with their work force by eliminating and avoiding redundant positions.  Otherwise, the related wages paid to redundant positions shall be subject to audit disallowance.
We recommend that management utilize the budget intended for the market supervisory post and appropriately charge wages to the Salaries and Wages – Casual/Contractual account.

We recommend that management include and monitor the timely releasing of resolutions as a major final output (MFO) for the SB Secretariat’s Division Performance Commitment and Review (DPCR).  Henceforth, require the SB Secretariat to abide by Section 37.3 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of the Republic Act No. 9184.
We recommend the management to strictly impose the withholding of salary on employees with cash advances which are not liquidated within the prescribed period.

We recommend that management include and monitor the timely submission of municipal ordinances to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan as a Major Final Output (MFO) for the SB Secretariat’s Division Performance Commitment and Review (DPCR).

We recommend the management to improve the subsequent Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plans by stating more specific programs, activities, projects (PAPs) which will serve as a straightforward guide for all officers-in-charge for easily implementation without further deliberations and redundant planning.

We recommend that management provide immediate attention and action to the monitoring and reconciliation of these accounts and at the same time evaluate the need and the capacity of the Municipality to hire additional manpower complement skilled in accounting preferably Certified Public Accountants.
We recommend the management to reconcile balances with concerned barangays and demand from them liquidation of the funds transferred for project implementation.

We recommended that the BAC submit a valid explanation on the foregoing issues of non-compliance with the requirement on the prohibition of referencing brand names and on the required number of suppliers to be invited under the Negotiated Procurement mode (two failed biddings), to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the BAC submit a valid explanation why the subject purchases appeared to have been split instead of a single procurement under competitive public bidding. Prospectively, comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the RPCPPE prepared the Municipality after the physical count, be detailed and complete so that information on the PPE items’ existence, status, condition and location can be reliable. A copy of the report shall be submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124, Volume 1 of the NGAS.
We also reiterated prior years’ recommendation that PPE Ledger Cards shall be maintained by the Municipal Accountant so that the recording, monitoring and accountability of the PPE can be assured.

We recommended that the accounting department recognize depreciation of the aforesaid PPE computed since the date of acquisition when the assets were already available for use in compliance with the accounting standards. In addition, adjustments shall also be provided for the over-depreciation of PPE after taking into account their salvage values.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that all the provisions of the Philippine Bidding Documents are complied, such that all the required bidding documents are issued to the bidders and completely submitted during conduct of procurement of infrastructure projects.

We recommended that BAC submit a valid explanation on why the planned purchase of airfare was not procured in accordance with the regulations in RA 9184. We recommended further to discontinue the practice of reimbursement and employ the allowed modalities of procurement to assure that the objective of attaining the most competitive and advantageous price is achieved.
We recommended that Management, through the BAC, submit a written explanation on why the regulations for the procurement of consultancy contract involving individual consultants were not observed.

We recommended that Management conduct competitive bidding or any alternative mode when justified by conditions in the procurement of catering services for its various activities following the guidelines prescribed in GPPB Resolution No. 12-2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant follow the provisions of the aforementioned BIR memorandum circular to ensure that accurate amounts of taxes are computed and withheld from all payments to VAT/Non-VAT registered suppliers.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the required information and/or documents to the Head of the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Office, copy furnished the Office of the Audit Team Leader, this Municipality that would show what confidential related activities in relation to Item II.3 of DILG MC 99-65 were performed on the said cash advances.

We recommended that the Accounting Office collate the necessary documents relative to the completed projects funded out by various National Government Agencies.  If evidence warrants, prepare journal entries to close the account.  Also, the savings amounting to P75,067.18 should be returned to source agency.

We recommended and the management agreed that the discrepancies noted in
the Bank Reconciliation Statements be adjusted immediately to correct the balances of
the Cash in Bank Accounts. Moreover, to furnish Bank Reconciliation Statements to
the bank, especially if the identified bank error is for adjustment by the bank.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to prioritize the
titling of those untitled lands in order to establish absolute ownership thereof, and for
the Municipal Budget Officer to consider the funding requirement for the titling of
lands in the budget preparation for the ensuing year.We also recommend that the
Municipal Accountant record the unrecorded real properties and to disclose in the
Notes to the Financial Statements other relevant information on the Land account.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
a. The Municipality Government through the LDRRMO should conduct periodic
review of the LDRRM Investment Plan and prepare a revise and updated Plan
whenever reprogramming of projects or new expense item is introduced and
integrated in the Revised AIP for approval of the LCE.
b. Revisit the provision of the above cited laws, rules and regulations to enhance
disaster preparedness and mitigation.

We recommended that management fast tract the implementation of the
programs/projects of the municipality so that intended beneficiaries can enjoy the
projects intended to them. Make a representation to the source agency for the
continuation of the project.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to
facilitate submission of the update list of inventory of insurable properties with the
GSIS and to require the Municipal Finance Committee to include in its annual
appropriation the amount necessary to cover the premiums for the insurance of its
properties.

1.7 Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE so that reconciliation with
accounting records can be attained, and so that reliability of the value
of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.

2.9 Require the Supply Officer-designate to prepare and submit SSMI to
the Accounting Office for the recording of the issuance of the supplies;
maintain Stock Cards and RPCI.
2.10 Conduct semestral physical count of inventory items by type and
submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the
Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

3.12 Strictly refrain from granting cash advances without legally
authorized specific purpose.
3.13 Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the erring
accountable officers, including retired/transferred employees to settle
their outstanding cash advances immediately.
3.14 Direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officers and
employees with unsettled cash advances, pursuant to Section 37 of
Presidential Decree 1445.
3.15 For long outstanding cash advances with less to no chance of being
settled caused by death or whose Accountable Officer’s whereabouts
could no longer be traced, the agency may file a request for authority
to write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016.

4.6 Advise the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation and
submission of the financial reports and adhere to the prescribed period
for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on
deficiencies noted.
4.7 Moreover, avail of the eNGAS from the COA to facilitate systematic
generation of financial reports.

5.8 Advise the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the requirements on the
prescribed period for submission of the required reports and
transaction documents to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies
noted.

6.6 Require the BAC/BAC Secretariat to thoroughly and meticulously
review and validate the eligibility of the documents submitted by the suppliers
before the issuance of the Notice of Award.
6.7 Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the
required documents to support the propriety and regularity of the
transactions.

7.6 Refrain from charging non-related expenses to the MDRRM Fund and
comply with the above-stated regulation in prioritizing expenses; and
7.7 Provide a separate appropriation for the maintenance and other
operating expenses and capital outlay of the MDRRM Office.

8.10 Refrain from hiring consultant for legal matters instead follow the
provision of Section 481 of RA 7160.

9.5 Comply strictly with the submission of Monthly Report of Official
Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, as prescribed
under COA Circular No. 77-61.

10.6 Stop the payment of prior year’s transaction charged to the current
year’s appropriations as provided in the aforementioned provision.
10.7 Determine in advance all expenses incurred which are due and
demandable in the current year which cannot be processed during the
year to be recorded as Accounts Payable.
10.8 Likewise, the Budget Office and Accountant are advised to be strict in
enforcing the provision.

11.5 Expedite the processing of the Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the
name of the LGU to protect the interest of the government.
11.6 Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the
supporting documents in all transactions before certifying the
completeness and propriety thereof. Require those in-charge of the
project/program to attach the necessary documents as required in the
claim.

12.6 Ensure that the SEF shall be utilized solely for its purpose; i.e.,
programs, projects and activities enumerated in Section 272 of RA
7160, DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-B dated June 25,
2001 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

13.7 Direct the GAD Focal Point System to use the HGDG and PIMME in
monitoring and evaluating GAD programs and activities as required
under JMC 2013-01.

14.8 Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment of a sustainable
Sanitary Landfill consistent with the standards and criteria set under
RA 9003 for the protection of the environmental and human health.

15.7 Comply the requirement of the DILG guidelines as provided and hasten the
implementation of the projects. Monitor the completion of the delayed
project in 2017 for the immediate delivery of basic services as intended.

16.9 Monitor the implementation of the planned developmental projects to
ensure the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes.

1.9	We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to render an accounting on the result of recently conducted physical inventory and reconcile differences of the two records.
2.7	We recommended that the Management increase the fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer and other accountable officers enough to cover their accountability.

3.6	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer coordinates with the Municipal Assessor to produce the financial records needed that will constitute the correct basis for the recording of the RPT/SET Receivables. Moreover, instruct her staff to update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer's index cards to facilitate the accurate balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list as the basis in adjusting the books of accounts as of December 31, 2018.

4.7	We recommended that the GFPS use the GAD Plan and Budget in the formulation of the GAD Accomplishment Report as required in the above-cited regulation to facilitate the evaluation and monitoring on the implementation of GAD PPAs.
5.8   We recommended that the Management adhere to the provisions of Section 119 of PD 1445 to properly account obligations for the current year.

6.10	We recommended Management for the full implementation of the projects so that the desired services and benefits be delivered to its beneficiaries in a timely manner. Further, employ regular monitoring mechanisms in assessing the rate of progress of project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.

7.6 	We recommended that Management instruct the head of implementing office to expedite the implementation of future projects in order to ensure the delivery of expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
We recommend that management include the timely deposit as a major final output (MFO) for the Treasury’s Division Performance Commitment and Review (DPCR).
observations.managementAction: Timely deposit was included in the Major Final Output of the MTO OPCR and Cash advances for payroll fund was strictly based on approved payroll to avoid large amount of unused funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management enhance the link between the Treasury and Accounting Department by using the capabilities of the available ECPAC system. The Accounting Department may be given direct access without overriding options to the entries of the Treasury Department for them to help remind the Treasury Department of any collections which needed to be reported and included in a certain period.
observations.managementAction: Proposed appropriation to upgrade the present ECPAC General Collection and Information System was included in the CY 2019 Annual Investment Plan for funding purposes. This project will enhance local network among Treasury, Assessor, Accounting and Budget Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit timely all collections and input in RCD actual data as events happen.
observations.managementAction: Daily deposit of collection has been practiced and correction on the hard copy of the report of collection and deposit was done to reconcile the actual vis-a-vis reported date of deposit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the MDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented. Further, we recommend submission of required report on or before the 15th day of each month as required in COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to facilitate timely review of the required LDRMM report.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund was regularly submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management explain on the improper charging of the wages of Job Order employees.  Otherwise, the same could be subjected to disallowance.  Henceforth, management is requested to source from General Fund the budget for personnel services in the LDRRM Office to carry on functions related to disaster management.
observations.managementAction: The wages for job order were supported with activity design authorized by the LDRRM Council to gather information which were used in the formulation of LDRRM Plan and as well perform the rounds with the municipality to monitor and assess areas prone to disaster such as landslide. It is within the purview of disaster preparedness.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that a room with a lock and key may be used as stock room for goods/supplies for sale separate from that of the office of the Stock Clerk who maintains the ledger/records of the supplies/goods for sale.  Moreover, the Purchaser may only be the one who is tasked to access the goods/supplies for sale and control the lock and key of the stockroom so that in instances of loss, accountability can be established.  Further, require that the Purchaser, Stock Clerk and a representative from the Accounting Office to regularly conduct physical count of the goods/supplies for sale to reconcile their balances and report accurate figures in the financial statements.  Finally, in the procurement of goods for sale, public bidding may be conducted and not on a reimbursement basis.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation are well taken by the management. The stock room has a lock installed. Bookkeeper regularly conducts physical count of the goods/supplies for sale.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) chart of accounts.  Moreover, maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 45% Personal Services Limitation and to minimize the hiring of casual employees as to necessity.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendations are well taken by the management. The charging and accounting of casual and contractual/job order employee were done in accordance with CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular no. 1, s. 2017 and CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular no. 1, s. 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately reinstall the post that used to block the entrance of the Complex.  Require that the Cashier’s Form be serially pre-numbered and issued at the entrance to visitors/tourists who opt to be issued official receipts instead of the cash tickets.  Also, require the Toll Gate Checker to see to it that before  visitors/tourists exit the Complex, they can present the cash tickets or the  Cashier’s Form duly signed by the Cashier as to OR number and amount paid  indicating that the entrance fee has been duly paid for.  Lastly, install safety measures in the booth where the Toll Gate Collectors are stationed such as providing for a cash vault, window grills and a security officer.
observations.managementAction: The management has deployed tourist policemen to add security to the collectors while the rehabilitation of structures in the chocolate hills complex is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to strictly impose the withholding of salary on employees with cash advances which are not liquidated within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. Withholding of salary on employees with outstanding cash advances was partially implemented subject to ₱5,000.00 minimum take home pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to send demand letter to those that are no longer in active government service.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. Demand letters were already sent to those that are no longer in active government service.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly ensure that nobody’s take home pay will be below ₱4,000.00 as prescribed. The management should as well assess carefully the effects of additional loan applications on the employee’s net take home pay considering their existing deductions before issuing certifications and clearances.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. Additional loan applications were carefully assess to ascertain its effects on employee’s net take home pay before issuing certifications and clearances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check at least one month before a check becomes stale and prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the cash in bank and accounts payable balances.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. However, the management found difficulty in communicating with indigent constituents who may have migrated to other places. Although, reconciliation and verification of stale checks is on-going to ascertain its correctness and availability before the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management review the validity vis-à-vis the possibility of claim of the outstanding payables and cause the reversion of those without valid claim to the unappropriated surplus. The management is also reminded to be stringent in assessing expenses to be obligated at year end.
observations.managementAction: The management conducted review on validity of the claim of the outstanding payables and cause the reversion in the total amount of ₱511,307.52 for GF and ₱45,175 for SEF as per JEV Nos. 100-2019-01-31582 to 31584, 200-2019-01-02216 dated 01-02-2019. The remaining outstanding payables have actual claims with and without legal issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer updates her cashbook daily to monitor each of the balances of the LGU’s bank accounts and be cautious of any service charges which can actually be avoided with due diligence. The total penalty should be shouldered by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Automated cashbook and print check system is already installed for accurate balances of various funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the personnel concerned in the preparation of the Purchase Order as well as the Acceptance and Inspection Report should indicate all the necessary information especially the delivery terms, received date of both Purchase Orders and Acceptance and Inspect Report.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management. The personnel concerned were directed to indicate all necessary information in both purchase orders and acceptance and inspection reports.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that with the assistance of the MPDC projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the constituents and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices in materials and feasibility of the projects in order to address the needs of the Municipality.  Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects attune with the present needs and constraints.
observations.managementAction: The management has tried to comply with COA recommendations. However, the regulation which requires the availability of funds prior to any procurement hinders them to proceed with the implementation at the beginning of the year. IRA is downloaded monthly to their account and needs to be accumulated to fund a certain project not to mention the lengthy procurement process from POW preparation to issuance of notice to proceed or purchase order. However, the management has been trying their best to at least achieve 80% physical implementation since most of the payments are made in the following year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to create a well-planned detailed engineering plan and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all projects to ensure completion of programmed projects as scheduled.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendations were well taken by the management. The management has been careful in planning to minimize delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to explain why it took the Local School Board more than seven months to approve CY 2016 Budget.  Henceforth, require that the SEF budgets are prepared and approved timely and regularly in pursuance to Section 7 of R. A. 5447.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation were well taken by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to take advantage of the 10-day period following each month to record, reconcile and improve the monitoring before making the payment on time.
observations.managementAction: The management has been paying timely the billings from GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management hire two more capable staff to complement the existing LDRRM Officer and a staff.
observations.managementAction: The management already hired a staff and has been looking for another one to complete the team and is considering one of the trainees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require the details of Official Receipts be entered in the Treasury Department.  Moreover, require the ECPAC focal person to link data from the Treasury Department to the Accounting Department to reduce redundancy in data entry.  Otherwise, install the ETRACS (Enhanced Tax Revenue Assessment and Collection System) which is free software available to local government units to computerize tax and revenue assessments and collection pending migration of the accounting system from the ECPAC to PPSAS in CY 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department has already stopped the duplicate entry of OR details in the ECPAC; however, the  system of both departments are not yet linked.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to explain the overpayment.  Otherwise, the total amount of ₱36,493.00 is subject to disallowance.  Henceforth, remit only the amount that is withheld against employees’ salary and immediately offset all the overpayments, if any in the next remittances.
observations.managementAction: The management stated that these are not overpayments but are timing differences due to the payment deadline. These are corrected in the succeeding month except for the beginning balances which are inherited from previous accountant that needed reconciliations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to ask a formal approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) on the use of above bank accounts based on the provisions in DOF Circular 001-2005 and transfer immediately any amount in excess of the authorized cash balance to any GFIs in Section 5.2.
observations.managementAction: Bank accounts in trust fund were mostly transferred to LBP, DBP and Postal Bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to avail of the remedies by administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action. Steps are properly enumerated in section 254 to 270 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Notices of Delinquency were posted in the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly the balances of liabilities due to government agencies/corporations concerned in order not to compromise the benefits/due the intended beneficiaries and at the same time avoid penalties/surcharges arising from late payments. Henceforth, regularly remit all trust liabilities of like nature on the required due dates.
observations.managementAction: The management has been paying monthly all the amounts withheld for the national and corporate government. However, there are still balances to be reconciled. These are inherited balances from former accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management retrace the entries made, perform reconciliation and make necessary adjusting entries to close the total amount of ₱1,605,496.22 to the appropriate account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant traced the transactions and found them to be accruals which was forgotten to be reversed when the corresponding expenses were recorded. He was able to close newer entries (approx. ₱.3M) but unfortunately he found it difficult to trace the accumulation of old balances (approx. ₱1.5M). He suggested to close the account to ‘prior years’ to clean up.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management retrace the entries made and review the source documents which brought the Cash in Vault to a negative balance. Check if there were unrecorded cash receipts/inflows used to fund the overspending. Validate as well the related disbursements which caused the overdraft if these were valid. Finally, prepare adjusting entries when necessary.
observations.managementAction: The management through reconciliations discovered that these are proper deductions to the receivable from the former municipal treasurer supported with complete documents. The management, however, didn’t offset the amount because the receivable was already presented in court before the related documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management should take proper action on government properties that are for disposal.  The General Services Officer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.  In the meantime, the Municipal Accountant should reclassify other unserviceable properties to Other Assets account while awaiting the disposal of such assets amounting to ₱3,949,410.61.
observations.managementAction: The management has started to dispose unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Require the municipal engineer to make a study on how to rehabilitate and improve the waterworks system.
Appropriate funds for the rehabilitation/improvement of the quality of water distributed by the waterworks system and eventually implement new water rates equitable enough for the quality of potable water supply distributed.
observations.managementAction: Management has purchased a lot which is a good source for potable water.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Property Custodian to maintain the Property Cards to be her basis in the preparation of the Annual Inventory Report and adhere to Sections 123 to 125 of New Government Accounting System Volume I in order to properly monitor and control the properties of the municipality.
Instruct the Inventory Committee to perform the actual physical count.
observations.managementAction: Physical count and property tagging are almost done for most of the office equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation and/or demand letters to individuals or the business establishment concerned.
For receivables from officials and employees arising from overpayment of claims or under remittance of collections, the Municipal Treasurer shall institute deductions on their subsequent claims and salaries in satisfaction of their indebtedness pursuant to the provision of Section 37 of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation are well taken by the management.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the concerned barangays to immediately liquidate the subsidies received in compliance with the signed Memorandum of Agreement to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit. Also prior to release of funds, the Municipal Accountant should certify that funds previously released to the requesting agency had been liquidated, verified and accounted for in the books, and that all disbursements for financial assistance be supported with complete documentation as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001
observations.managementAction:
The Barangays had fully liquidated their cash advances.

A certification is now issued that the prior cash advance granted is fully liquidated before granting another cash advance.
observations.reason: Recommendation fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
Due to the foregoing, the grant of said benefits exceeded the 55% limitation. Municipal officials should refrain from disbursing said benefits in the future, otherwise municipal officials and employees concerned shall refund the said amounts.
observations.managementAction: Municipality had prudent thereafter in the granting of benefits to officials and employees not to exceed the PS limitation.
observations.reason: Municipal officials refrained from granting benefits in excess of PS limitation.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
Concerned municipal official and employees are required to refund the amount of P22,000 each or a total of P1,936,000.00 and management should limit the granting of additional benefits for personal services within the 45% level for a third class municipality as provided by law.
observations.managementAction: Payments made partially thru salary deduction.
observations.reason: NFD was issued last 9/15/14

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
Required all the municipal employees to refund P20, 625.00 each, or a total of P1,842, 270.91.
observations.managementAction: Payments made partially thru salary deduction.
observations.reason: NFD was issued last 10/2/14

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 85-55-A. Also, exercise due prudence so that government funds are safeguarded and conserved to avoid unnecessary, extravagant and excessive expenditures.   Also, send only those officials and personnel to trainings and seminars relevant to the nature of their work.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, LGU did not spend funds to attend trainings/ seminars conducted by private organizations.
observations.reason: No participants were sent to attend trainings/ seminars hosted by private organizations.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
It is recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Units should strictly enforce the provisions of GAA 2010 pertaining to the statutory net pay to aid in the promotion of an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel will receive adequate salary for their daily subsistence. Also, all salary payments to persons other than the payee should be with written authority from the concerned payees to avoid future misunderstanding and/ or arguments.
observations.managementAction: Lessened the number of employees receiving below statutory net pay.
observations.reason: Only few employees were observed to receive net pays below the statutory take home pay.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
Concerned officials are required to refund the excess payment of their RATA. Furthermore, the management should refrain from implementing the new RATA rates until such time that the PS cap/ limitation shall have been observed.
observations.managementAction: Refund thru payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Continued refund thru payroll deduction except those who retired or were separated from service.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
It is recommended that the noted deficiencies be corrected and the required documents submitted immediately for validation. Thereafter, all the officers involved in the processing of the claims should see to it that the supporting papers are complete and proper, before they are paid.
observations.managementAction: Partial submission of required documents.
observations.reason:
Copies of the pre and post inspection reports for the purchase of spare parts were submitted. 

Most of the CY 2018 transactions have been properly supported with proper supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
A. We recommended that the municipal accountant ensure that all DV for CF cash advance with complete supporting documents be submitted to the COA ATL within (7) days from release of the check pursuant to the above cited circular.  Failure or refusal to do so without justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for administrative disciplinary action pursuant to the Administrative Provisions/Penal Sanctions of the circular.

Audit Recommendation:
B. We recommended that Management submit the MPOC Resolution approving the Municipality’s POP and the release of the CF duly approved and signed by at least two-thirds (2/3) of its members in accordance with the applicable provision of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01, otherwise a notice of suspension will be issued if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Timely submission of documents for cash advance for 2018 confidential fund | Documents for cash advance of CF were submitted on time as of CY 2018
observations.reason: The document was not yet submitted to the audit team as of CY 2018

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that all concerned Accountable Officers be required to immediately liquidate their cash advances whose purpose had already been served.  Strictly adhere to the aforementioned provisions of Sections 89 of Presidential Decree No. 14445 and provisions of Circular No. 97-002 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
The CY 2018 balance of P5,161,327.00 for Advances to Officers and Employees included a total of P5,074,990.00 representing unliquidated cash advances for intelligence fund from CY 2011-2015.

A follow-up will be made by the LGU to COA central office on the status of the liquidation of the CY 2011-2015 Confidential Fund
observations.reason:
Liquidations for the intelligence fund for the year were submitted on time as of CY 2018. 

Likewise, other cash advances granted during the year were promptly liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
It is recommended that the noted deficiencies be corrected and the required documents submitted immediately for validation. Thereafter, all the officers involved in the processing of claims should see to it that the supporting papers are complete and proper, before these are paid.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office will verify the vouchers for compliance.
observations.reason:
Supporting documents were not yet submitted as of CY 2018.

For CY 2018, disbursement vouchers were observed to be substantially supported with proper supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
Audit Recommendation:
We recommended that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the amount in excess of P5,000.00 of PEI received, otherwise all those who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable.

Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of EO No. 80 as implemented by Budget circular No. 2013-3 and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: The excess of P5,000.00 was already booked-up and deductions were made thru the payroll. | Previously, a letter was sent to management regarding the payments, however no reply was received.

observations.recommendation: Fast track the implementation of projects/programs funded out by Fund Transfers. Further, the unspent balances of projects/programs shall be returned to source agency as required by laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Three ADM projects shall be scheduled to be re-bid this year for two failure of bidding. Storage van project is scheduled for re-bid this year. Also, for unimplemented projects, the Municipality will ask approval from the source agency to realign the said budget for another projects.
observations.reason: 2 ADM Projects in CY 2017 amounting to P10,075,000 still on-going construction.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality should exert extra efforts in the Information and Education Campaign for Solid Waste Management.  Also, continue allocating in the annual budget a fund for the programs/ projects/ activities until such time that the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 are fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Management stated that since compliance with the required sanitary landfill could be not attained under the present circumstances, the Municipality focuses on the implementation and monitoring of the Barangay’s compliance with the establishment of material recovery facilities.  Further, the use of leaflets and conduct of seminars were utilized to strengthen information and education campaign to reduce, re-use and recycle solid wastes in the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipality is in low lying zone, thus, disposal site may trigger immediate distribution of diseases.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should observe timeliness in the remittance of the provincial share in the SEF collection within 15 days of every succeeding month.
observations.managementAction: The Management observed timeliness in the remittance of the provincial share in the SEF collection in 2018.

observations.recommendation: Management should submit justification on the utilization of SEF in the amount of P245,083.44 for programs not among those authorized under RA 7160.  Further, LSB shall focus its budget on the needs of students as well as the maintenance and provision school facilities.
observations.managementAction: The Management submit their justification on the utilization of SEF. Likewise, LSB already provided budget on the needs of students as well as the maintenance and provision of school facilities.

observations.recommendation: Management should comply with the submission of complete documentary requirements before effecting payment to supplier or contractor.  Otherwise, the payment shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the submission of complete documentary requirements on various procurements.

observations.recommendation: Management should exercise prudence and due diligence of a good father of the family in accepting donated drugs and medicines to avoid being tagged as a conduit of wasted government properties and funds.   Henceforth, disposal of government property should be approved by the Municipal Mayor and witnessed by the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Disposal of government property had been duly approved by the Municipal Mayor

observations.recommendation: Accounting Office should abide with the accounting system provided for in the Notes to Financial Statements.  Also, recognize in the books of accounts rental receivables from lessees of market stalls amounting to P338,730.10.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already recorded the rental receivables in the books.

observations.recommendation: Accounting Office coordinate with the General Services Office should reconcile their records.  Should there be differences, the Municipal Accountant shall make proper adjusting entries.  Further, Ledger Cards shall be maintained for proper monitoring purposes and Requisition and Issue Slip shall be prepared before issuance of deliverables to end-users.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office and GSO’s records were already reconciled.

observations.recommendation: Management should recognize in the books of accounts the amount of interest payable and interest expense.  Also, set-up an interest expense and liability in accordance with the prevailing rate at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The management had observed proper recognition of interest payable and interest expense in the books.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality officials coordinate with the beneficiary Barangays to fast track the installation of the electrical service entrance, electric meters and water supplies to ensure immediate operation of the health centers.
observations.managementAction: Electric meters and water supplies were already installed in the Barangays.

observations.recommendation:
Management should make the necessary actions to facilitate the implementation of the projects so that the intended beneficiaries may enjoy the benefits, avoid the incurrence of penalty deductions and project termination.

Also, Management should coordinate with the offices concerned to comply with the documentary requirements to effect liquidation in both participating agencies and the LGU’s books.

Management should study the possibility of establishing a LDRRM Office with assigned Officer and staff pursuant to NDRRMC/ DILG/ DBM/ CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.  Also, the LDRRM Council should revisit the prior year’s MDRRM Plan and implement programs/projects not yet fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: Permanent employees are designated and assigned at Disaster Center.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive shall coordinate public efforts with that of the private sectors in preserving and protecting the environment and public health as required under RA No. 9003. 

The LCE shall encourage the full participation of the Barangays in the implementation of the law. In addition, require the segregation of wastes by every household.

Further, we recommended the maximum utilization of machines costing P3,500,000.00 funded by the DTI-Province which was launched last December 21, 2016. These machines are plastic melting machine, mechanical dryer, and crusher which will be used in melting recyclable wastes to form useful products.
observations.managementAction: The systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management are already adopted.

observations.recommendation: Dep-Ed officials shall reconcile their records with the records of the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer. Once reconciled, said officials together with the representative of the Office of the Municipal Mayor shall conduct actual physical count to determine its existence. Once determined as to its existence, LGU-Carmen shall transfer the PPEs used by Dep-Ed in order that accountability of the items be established.  The Accountant shall prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to transfer of SEF-properties to Dep-Ed Province of Davao del Norte for the account of Dep-Ed - Carmen and to the respective national high schools with complete set of books of accounts as end-users pursuant to generally accepted accounting and auditing rules and regulations and pertinent provisions of P.D.1445.
observations.managementAction: Proper supervision on school properties was already initiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management that immediate appropriate action to dispose the unserviceable properties be undertaken to avoid further deterioration and possible loss of their value.

We recommended that the Management send Demand Letters to debtors to intensify collections of overdue accounts. If these efforts prove futile, the LGU should document all action undertaken to recover the accounts and the head of the agency should seek authority from the members of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to write off the doubtful accounts. Once the legislative body approves, submit the listings of doubtful accounts together with SB Resolution to the COA and request authority for its writing-off supported by a list of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts.
observations.reason: The municipality had difficulty in locating the debtors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management return to the respective source agencies the unutilized balance of funds for projects which are already fully implemented.
observations.reason: Findi already returned for some projects.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Government adopt a system of payroll payment through the Automated Teller Machine.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management must secure local sanggunian authority/approval for all the contracts/projects entered into/implemented by the local chief executive that were described in generic terms in the Appropriation Ordinance/AIP for CY 2017 to ratify the same and avoid disallowances pursuant to G.R. No. 175527 and COA Memorandum 2010-014. Furthermore, the Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Council should properly plan, prioritize and identify the specific programs/projects to be funded under the 20% EDF and incorporate the same in the AIP.
observations.reason: The management has overlooked the required number of contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the Property, Plant, and Equipment list and reclassify semi-expendable items as inventory or expense upon its issuance.

We recommended that the management ensure that all copies of approved contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted within five (5) working days from approval or issuance and within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries for review, evaluation and inspection of items procured by the Agency.
observations.reason: The management has overlooked the required number of contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct semestral physical count of inventory and submit its report not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively. Moreover, require the Property Officer to record all receipts and issuances of supplies by using the Stock Card pursuant to Section 44 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

Prepare the RPCPPE by
classifying the PPE according to
their type for purposes of
comparison/reconciliation with
the general ledger balances.
Hasten the completion of the
RPCPPE so that reconciliation
with accounting records can be
attained, and so that reliability of
the value of PPE in the Statement
of Financial Position can be
ascertained.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted incomplete RPCPPE as at December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly refrain from granting
additional cash advances if the
previous cash advances were not
yet settled.
Direct the Municipal Accountant
to issue demand letters to the
erring accountable officers,
including retired/transferred

Formulating the LDRRM Plan is on process

The management is enjoined to strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

Some programs are included already in the office appropriation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

1.	Require all implementing offices to submit Quarterly Reports on Government Projects, Programs and Activities from all sources to the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator who will then consolidate the reports for submission to municipal officials for monitoring and decision making purposes.

2.	Conduct regular periodic meetings with the heads of departments/offices to assess whether the implementation of PPAs is proceeding as planned and to ensure that implementation constraints, if any, are immediately resolved.
observations.managementAction:
All concerned offices are required to submit reports for consolidation.

MDDO required quarterly report.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that fund transfers from the 20% Development Fund be supported with documents such as project proposals or program of expenditures to establish the validity of the transfer.   Any transfer of fund for expenditures not chargeable to the 20% Development Fund as provided under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, issued by the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) should not be allowed.
observations.managementAction: Transfer are made only after complete documentation requirements are complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of appropriating and utilizing funds of the 20% development fund for expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures.  The municipal officials are enjoined to follow strictly the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommend to require the BAC to prepare regularly the Procurement Monitoring Report (see attached PMR Form) for all procurement activities of the municipality for submission to the GPBB every semester as required under Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184.  A printed copy of the report shall be furnished to this Office to allow the audit team to verify the status of procurement activities specified in the Annual Procurement Plan as basis for auditor’s action, if any.
observations.managementAction: BAC assured the preparation and submission of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 2012-01 of PCW, NEDA and DBM in the utilization of the GAD fund that relates to the activities that would address specific gender issues and concerns.

We recommend to stop the practice of charging the payments of LBP loan amortization to the 20% development fund because although such expenditures partake the nature of capital expenditures, it is administrative in nature. Adhere strictly to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended that Management direct the Inventory committee together with the property and accounting units to prepare the reconciliation of the result of the inventory count with the Property and Accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The conduct of physical inventory by the Inventory Committee is on going
observations.reason: The agency already submitted RCPPE. However, not in the format prescribed and amounts of PPE are not reconciled with the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the requirements of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 that all development projects to be funded under the 20% DF shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio economic development and environmental management outcome of the LGU and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Expenditures included under the 20% LDF which can be accommodated in the GF proper are no longer included in the 20% appropriation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the  LDRRM Officer to coordinate with the Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund on monthly basis in order to submit the said report to the concerned offices on the due dates pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Monthly submission if said report was done
observations.reason: The LDRRMO has already submitted monthly reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.
observations.managementAction: The position of LDRRMO II was created which had been filled up last May 2, 2018 and the LDRRMO I position was already re-published and posted for competitive assessment of applicant

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports /documents mentioned in Section 4 C.8 (5) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.
observations.managementAction: All implementing offices were required by the LCE  to submit terminal reports including pictures as supporting documents.
observations.reason: The GAD AR was already supported with terminal reports or activity accomplishment reports

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the GFPS to administer HGDG for those LGU programs and projects attributed wholly or a portion thereof during the preparation of the GAD plan and budget and the accomplishment report.
observations.managementAction: HGDG was already administered to the PPAs of GAD PB for accurate preparation of the same

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management appropriate one percent of the Local Government Unit’s Internal Revenue Allotment as required in Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and to prepare the Local Development Plan and Local Investment Plan for Children with programs, projects and activities that promote and protect the rights of the children and disadvantaged sector of society.
observations.managementAction: DSWD submitted LCPC Plan , programs of which were funded in the annual budget
observations.reason: The Municipality has allocated 1% for PPAs under LCPC

observations.reason: To be implemented in 2019. Budget for 2018 was approved in December of the preceding year as compared to the budget in 2017 which was approved in March of the same year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the MDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented. observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require the Municipal Accountant to review these accounts, make adjusting entries if necessary and abide by provisions of COA Circular 97-001.
observations.managementAction: #12, AAR 2016 Not Implemented. The management stated that the biggest chunk of non-moving accounts is beyond their control. These are the ₱11M cash shortage of the former treasurer who is currently under litigation and the liquidated intelligence fund which credit notice is yet to be issued. On the other hand, the management expressed their intent to coordinate with COA representative in conducting inventory count after which the non-moving inventories will be closed. These inventories are perceived to be consumed but remained unadjusted in the books. Moreover, the management will also demand liquidation from the barangays for the financial assistance given.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) chart of accounts. Moreover, maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 45% Personal Services Limitation and to minimize the hiring of casual employees as to necessity.
observations.managementAction: #9, AAR 2014 No action has been done. The accountant explained that pursuant to COA Circular 2015-009, salaries & wages-casual/contractual account is used to record the pay for services rendered by contracted/hired by the agency. However, under CSC resolution no. 02-0790, there is no employer-employee relationship created under either a job order or contract for service.
observations.reason: Salaries & wages-casual/contractual account used per COA Circular 2015-009; no employer-employee relationship per CSC resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of P100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25,2007. The management suggested that to better account their contributions, the auditor in charge to the release of contribution should include the contribution in their Audit. observations.reason: Cannot discontinue contributions due to MOA among member LGUs; classification issue between NGO/PO and GOCC.

If the analysis/review of the accounts is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, the head of the agency should seek authority from the members of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan (SP) to write off the doubtful accounts. Once the legislative body approves, submit the listings of the doubtful accounts together with the SP Resolution to the COA and request authority for its writing-off supported by a list of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts stating the reasons why the books of accounts/ records, financial statements/ schedules and supporting vouchers/ documents cannot be located.
