observations.recommendation:
Recommendation –
We recommend that management include the timely deposit as a major final
output (MFO) for the Treasury’s Division Performance Commitment and
Review (DPCR).

Recommendation –
We recommend that the management enhance the link between the
Treasury and Accounting Department by using the capabilities of the available
ECPAC system. The Accounting Department may be given direct access without
overriding options to the entries of the Treasury Department for them to help
remind the Treasury Department of any collections which needed to be reported
and included in a certain period.

Recommendation –
We recommend that the management require the Municipal Treasurer to
deposit timely all collections and input in RCD actual data as events happen.

Recommendation
We recommend the management to strictly impose the withholding of
salary on employees with cash advances which are not liquidated within the
prescribed period.

Recommendation
We recommend the management to send demand letter to those that are
no longer in active government service.

Recommendation
We recommend that management strictly ensure that nobody’s take
home pay will be below ₱4,000.00 as prescribed. The management should as well
assess carefully the effects of additional loan applications on the employee’s net
take home pay considering their existing deductions before issuing certifications
and clearances.

Recommendation
We recommend that management send a written notice to the payee of the
existence of the check at least one month before a check becomes stale and
prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the cash in bank and accounts
payable balances.

Recommendation
We recommend that management review the validity vis-à-vis the
possibility of claim of the outstanding payables and cause the reversion of those
without valid claim to the unappropriated surplus. The management is also
reminded to be stringent in assessing expenses to be obligated at year end.

Recommendation
We recommend that management review the procurement process to
determine the bottleneck and expedite the processing of payments.

Recommendation –
We recommend that management require the MDRRMC to monitor and
evaluate the use and disbursements of the Calamity Fund and to deliberate and
discuss problems encountered by the LGU during actual disasters/calamities
during the past year/s to be included the LDRRM Plan of the ensuing year.
Henceforth, maximize utilization of the pre disaster preparedness programs
and/or activities so that the intended purpose is attained/achieved as provided in
Rule 18 of RA 10121.

Recommendation –
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer updates her cashbook daily
to monitor each of the balances of the LGU’s bank accounts and be cautious of
any service charges which can actually be avoided with due diligence. The total
penalty should be shouldered by the Municipal Treasurer.

Recommendation –
We recommend that the personnel concerned in the preparation of the
Purchase Order as well as the Acceptance and Inspection Report should indicate
all the necessary information especially the delivery terms, received date of both
Purchase Orders and Acceptance and Inspect Report.

Recommendation –
It is recommended that with the assistance of the MPDC projects
identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended
benefits to the constituents and to get the maximum value of fund utilization
considering the increasing prices in materials and feasibility of the projects in
order to address the needs of the Municipality. Further, conduct actual studies
for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and
practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects attune
with the present needs and constraints.

Recommendation –
We recommend that management fast track the development of the
waterworks system to provide full service to its constituents and start collecting
the fees thereafter to maintain the service.

We recommend that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to explain
in writing, together with the required supporting documents, the non-compliance of
the Revised IRR of RA 9184, and to prospectively comply with the said law, to avoid
suspension or disallowance.

We recommend that Management require the concerned barangays to
immediately liquidate the subsidies received in compliance with the signed
Memorandum of Agreement to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit. Also prior
to release of funds, the Municipal Accountant should certify that funds previously
released to the requesting agency had been liquidated, verified and accounted for
in the books, and that all disbursements for financial assistance be supported with
complete documentation as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend that henceforth, Management comply with the provisions of COA
Circular 2013-004 dated January 30 2013 to promote transparency and
accountability in government projects.

We recommend that Management stop the charging of projects not aligned with
DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1 against the 20% Development Fund to
avoid the sanctions provided under RA No. 7160 and other applicable laws.

We recommend that the statutory positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-
DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) dated April 4, 2014 be filled up
immediately by the Municipality, instead of designating existing employees to
comply with NDRRMC–DILG)–DBM–CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC)
No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace, review and analyze the
identified abnormal balances so that the corresponding adjustments could be
made to ensure the reliability and accuracy of its presentation in the financial
statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make adjusting journal entries to
correct the erroneous recording of obligations of the Municipality. Furthermore,
we recommend that Management submit a copy of the signed Loan Agreement,
together with the amortization schedules of the two (2) existing loans for further
verification in audit.

We recommend that the municipal accountant ensure that all DV for CF cash
advance with complete supporting documents be submitted to the COA ATL
within (7) days from release of the check pursuant to the above cited circular.
Failure or refusal to do so without justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for
administrative disciplinary action pursuant to the Administrative
Provisions/Penal Sanctions of the circular.

We recommend that Management submit the MPOC Resolution approving the
Municipality’s POP and the release of the CF duly approved and signed by at least
two-thirds (2/3) of its members in accordance with the applicable provision of
COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01, otherwise a notice
of suspension will be issued if warranted.

We recommended to fast track the implementation of projects/programs funded out by Fund Transfers. Further, the unspent balances of projects/programs shall be returned to source agency as required by laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipality exert extra efforts in the Information and Education Campaign for Solid Waste Management.  Also, continue allocating in the annual budget a fund for the programs/projects/activities until such time that the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 are fully implemented.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer observe timeliness in the remittance of the provincial share in the SEF collection within 15 days of every succeeding month.

We recommended that management submit justification on the utilization of SEF in the amount of P245,083.44 for programs not among those authorized under RA 7160.  Further, LSB shall focus its budget on the needs of students as well as the maintenance and provision school facilities.

We recommended that Management should comply with the submission of complete documentary requirements before effecting payment to supplier or contractor.  Otherwise, the payment shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended that management should exercise prudence and due diligence of a good father of the family in accepting donated drugs and medicines to avoid being tagged as a conduit of wasted government properties and funds.   Henceforth, disposal of government property should be approved by the Municipal Mayor and witnessed by the Auditor.

We recommended that Accounting Office abide with the accounting system provided for in the Notes to Financial Statements.  Also, recognize in the books of accounts rental receivables from lessees of market stalls amounting to P338,730.10.

We recommended that Accounting Office coordinate with the General Services Office to reconcile their records.  Should there be differences, the Municipal Accountant shall make proper adjusting entries.  Further, Ledger Cards shall be maintained for proper monitoring purposes and Requisition and Issue Slip shall be prepared before issuance of deliverables to end-users.

We recommended that management recognize in the books of accounts the amount of interest payable and interest expense.  Also, set-up an interest expense and liability in accordance with the prevailing rate at the end of the year.

We recommended to the Management that immediate appropriate action to
dispose the unserviceable properties be undertaken to avoid further deterioration and
possible loss of their value.

We recommended that the Management send Demand Letters to debtors to
intensify collections of overdue accounts. If these efforts prove futile, the LGU should
document all action undertaken to recover the accounts and the head of the agency
should seek authority from the members of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to write off
the doubtful accounts. Once the legislative body approves, submit the listings of
doubtful accounts together with SB Resolution to the COA and request authority for its
writing-off supported by a list of available records and extent of validation made on the
accounts.

We recommended that the management return to the respective source
agencies the unutilized balance of funds for projects which are already fully
implemented.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Government adopt a
system of payroll payment through the Automated Teller Machine.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management must secure local
sanggunian authority/approval for all the contracts/projects entered into/implemented by
the local chief executive that were described in generic terms in the Appropriation
Ordinance/AIP for CY 2017 to ratify the same and avoid disallowances pursuant to G.R.
No. 175527 and COA Memorandum 2010-014.

Furthermore, the Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning
and Development Council should properly plan, prioritize and identify the specific
programs/projects to be funded under the 20% EDF and incorporate the same in
the AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the Property,
Plant, and Equipment list and reclassify semi-expendable items as inventory or
expense upon its issuance.

1.9 Prepare the RPCPPE by classifying the PPE according to their type
for purposes of comparison/reconciliation with the general ledger
balances.
1.10 Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE so that reconciliation with
accounting records can be attained, and so that reliability of the
value of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position can be
ascertained.

2.8 Strictly refrain from granting additional cash advances if the
previous cash advances were not yet settled.
2.9 Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the erring
accountable officers, including retired/transferred employees to settle
their outstanding cash advances immediately.
2.10 Direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officers
and employees with unsettled cash advances, pursuant to Section 37
of Presidential Decree 1445.

3.10 Direct the Municipal Accountant to require compliance of relevant
documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 before
certifying the completeness of documents in the disbursement
voucher.
3.11 Likewise, we require the Management to submit the deficient
documents enumerated in Annex G, to enable us to make a decision in
audit.

4.9 Direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer
(MSWDO) to prepare an LCPC plan with corresponding budget
equal to the one percent of the IRA. Consequently, enjoin the Budget
Officer to appropriate the same plan prepared by the MSWDO in the
Annual Budget, as required by RA 9344. Ensure that all projects
under this Program be implemented so that intended beneficiaries can
avail of the benefits.

5.10 Advise the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to hasten the
preparation and submission of the financial reports, SAAOB and
transaction documents and adhere to the requirements on the
prescribed period for submission of the required reports and
transaction documents to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies
noted.
5.11 Moreover, upgrade the Accounting and Budget Offices by providing
them with computerized accounting and budgeting systems.

6.7 Direct the Municipal Treasurer to instruct the Supply Officer which is
under his Office to furnish COA with the copies of Purchase Orders
and Contracts together with its supporting documents, five days after
its execution/perfection.

7.6 Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the completeness of
documents to support the payment of labor wages and require the
Municipal Engineer to prepare the accomplishment reports or status
of work accomplished on every payment of labor wage.

8.7 Conduct training/workshop on how to administer the HGDG tool or
hire an expert that could perform gender analysis using the said tool.
8.8 Require the GAD Focal Point System to administer the HGDG in the GAD
Plan and Budget, and GAD Accomplishment Report and use the new
formats as prescribed under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No.
2016-01.

9.7 Monitor closely the implementation of planned developmental
programs and projects to ensure the attainment of desirable socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes.
Follow-up and monitor those projects with documentary requirements
to enable their implementation.
9.8 Submit a list of programs, projects and activities of the 20%
Development Fund for CYs 2016 and 2017, including their status of
implementation.

10.8 Ensure the implementation of the planned activities and projects as
incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to increase the level of
disaster preparedness and response capability of the LGU.

11.7 Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to pay
the total penalties of ₱13,300.21 as this is a personal liability of the
persons responsible as stated in Section 105 (2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management direct the Inventory committee together with the property and accounting units to prepare the reconciliation of the result of the inventory count with the Property and Accounting records.

We recommended that Management direct the  LDRRM Officer to coordinate with the Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund on monthly basis in order to submit the said report to the concerned offices on the due dates pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

3.5     We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.

4.5      We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports /documents mentioned in Section 4 C.8 (5) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.

5.5    We recommend that Management require the GFPS to administer HGDG for those LGU programs and projects attributed wholly or a portion thereof during the preparation of the GAD plan and budget and the accomplishment report.
6.4    We recommend strict adherence to the provisions of Section 37 of Republic Act No. 9003 on the use of Sanitary Landfill.

7.6     We recommended that the Management appropriate one percent of the Local Government Unit’s Internal Revenue Allotment as required in Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and to prepare the Local Development Plan and Local Investment Plan for Children with programs, projects and activities that promote and protect the rights of the children and disadvantaged sector of society.

8.5    We recommended that Management require all implementing offices to submit Quarterly Reports on Government Projects, Programs and Activities from all sources to the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator who will then consolidate the reports for submission to municipal officials for monitoring and decision making purposes.

9.5   We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the requirements of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 that all development projects to be funded under the 20% DF shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio economic development and environmental management outcome of the LGU and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

We recommend to management that Collecting Officers turn over their collections intact daily or not later than the next working day to the Liquidating Officer pursuant to Section 31, Volume I of the NGAS Manual. Moreover, install the ETRACS (Enhanced Tax Revenue Assessment and Collection System) which is a free software available to local government units to computerize tax and revenue assessments and collection. A portion of the Report of Collection and Deposit generated by ETRACS segregates the cash from the non-cash collections as shown in the picture below, hence facilitating review on whether collections were remitted intact or not.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that a room with a lock and key may be used as stock room for goods/supplies for sale separate from that of the office of the Stock Clerk who maintains the ledger/records of the supplies/goods for sale. Moreover, the Purchaser may be the one who is tasked to access the goods/supplies for sale and to control the lock and key of the stockroom so that in instances of loss, accountability can be established. Further, require that the Purchaser, Stock Clerk and a representative from the Accounting Office to regularly conduct physical count of the goods/supplies for sale to reconcile their balances and report accurate figures in the financial statements. Finally, in the procurement of goods for sale, public bidding may be conducted and not on a reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation:
Management should strategize the implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program which includes, but not limited to, the following:

From time to time, evaluate the performance of each member of the BAC and Technical Working Group.  The output of the Office shall be the basis in the computation of honorarium.

We recommended for the full implementation of the projects funded by the 20% Development Fund to speed up development and progress and eventually uplift the lives of the constituents of the Municipality.

It is further recommended to identify the target beneficiaries of the programs, projects and activities during the planning stage so that the funds intended for that purpose will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

Management should study the possibility of establishing a LDRRM Office with assigned Officer and staff pursuant to NDRRMC/ DILG/ DBM/ CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.  Also, the LDRRM Council should revisit the prior year’s MDRRM Plan and implement programs/projects not yet fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: Casual employees are designated and assigned at Disaster Center.
observations.reason: Permanent personnel were not yet hired.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive shall coordinate public efforts with that of the private sectors in preserving and protecting the environment and public health as required under RA No. 9003. 

The LCE shall encourage the full participation of the Barangays in the implementation of the law. In addition, require the segregation of wastes by every household.

Further, we recommended the maximum utilization of machines costing P3,500,000.00 for melting recyclable wastes which was launched last December 21, 2016. These machines are plastic melting machine, mechanical dryer, and crusher which will be used in melting recyclable wastes to form useful products.
observations.reason: Machines worth P3,500,000.00 for melting recyclable wastes are not yet operational.

observations.recommendation:
Expenses charged to SEF should conform to existing laws, rules and regulations governing the utilization of such fund, otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit.

Henceforth, evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.

BAC should adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and furnish the Auditor with copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and its supporting bidding documents within five (5) days after its perfection. Otherwise, we shall recommend that appropriate sanctions as provided for in Section 4.0 of the same Circular be imposed against those responsible Official/employees for non-compliance.

Also, BAC Secretariat should document the flow or movement of the contracts, bidding documents and purchase orders to establish accountability for delays in the submission to the Office of the Auditor.

Management ought to consider the needed manpower requirements and IT equipment of the office of the BAC to expedite the submission of contracts, bidding documents and purchase orders to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction:
Submit timely to the COA all copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and its supporting documents. 
Monitor the flow of all perfected contracts.

observations.recommendation: Management should enhance and strengthen the internal control system of the agency to safeguard assets from loss and misappropriation. Further, strictly follow the existing laws, rules and regulations prescribed in the settlement of obligations to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

Dep-Ed officials shall reconcile their records with the records of the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer. Once reconciled, said officials together with the representative of the Office of the Municipal Mayor shall conduct actual physical count to determine its existence. Once determined as to its existence, LGU-Carmen shall transfer the PPEs used by Dep-Ed in order that accountability of the items be established.  The Accountant shall prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to transfer of SEF-properties to Dep-Ed Province of Davao del Norte for the account of Dep-Ed - Carmen and to the respective national high schools with complete set of books of accounts as end-users pursuant to generally accepted accounting and auditing rules and regulations and pertinent provisions of P.D.1445.
observations.managementAction: Continue conducting physical count and prepare reports for reconciliation and submit the same to Sangguniang Bayan for authority to donate and transfer accountability to different schools.
observations.reason: School Representatives and Inventory committee conflict of schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommend discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/ criteria for the mentioned in the said act.
observations.managementAction: Findi closed ng the open dump and has acquired parcel of land

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management ensure that all copies of approved contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted within five (5) working days from approval or issuance and within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries for review, evaluation and inspection of items procured by the Agency.
observations.reason: Findi has overlooked the required number of days for the submission of the contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Health Office coordinate with the barangay health centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the government’s goal of providing preventive care services to indigents will be fully implemented, and thus, best results could be achieved in the most opportune time.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) and the LDRRMO Designate shall submit the said report to COA Auditor within the prescribed period as required in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012–002.

We recommend that management conduct semestral physical count of inventory and submit its report not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively. Moreover, require the Property Officer to record all receipts and issuances of supplies by using the Stock Card pursuant to Section 44 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Findi does not have a General Service Officer to monitor and maintain records on receipts and issuances of supplies. The Treasurer has not yet completed the inventory of supplies.
observations.reason: The municipality does not have General Service Officer to monitor and maintain records on receipts and issuances of supplies. The Treasurer has not yet completed the inventory of supplies.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management intensify efforts in the establishment and maintenance of a GAD database containing all sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the PCW/ NEDA/ DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and to ensure that the GAD Focal Person should undergo trainings related to GAD to better equip her on the task at hand.

a. Direct the Municipal General Services Officer to locate documents pertinent to the LGU properties preparatory to the conduct of the complete physical count / inventory of all properties in accordance with existing regulations and in order to establish the actual existence; b. Submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the latter's ledger cards; c. Require the Accounting and GSO to maintain ledger and property cards as control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records; and d. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommend that Management: a. Ensure completeness of the supporting documents of all contracts/ purchase orders submitted for review pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. b. Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts/ purchase orders and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.
observations.managementAction: Findi: all copies submitted for review pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 dated Feb. 12, 2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal's Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall remit promptly and intact all taxes withheld by the Municipality to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in compliance with the BIR regulations.

We recommended the following:
a. Reconcile the amounts of PPE in the Subsidiary Ledgers and in the Statement of Financial Position.
b. Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE so that reconciliation with accounting records can be attained, and so that reliability of the value of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position can be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: Physical count of all PPEs is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct review and validation on each of the journal entries made under Advances and Other Receivables accounts to determine the correctness and propriety of the subject entry and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, when necessary.

a. Refrain from granting additional cash advances if the prior cash advances were not yet settled; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the erring accountable officers, including the retired/transferred employees to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances.

Refrain from granting cash advances to elected officials except for travel and confidential fund only, making sure that prior advances were already settled before granting additional cash advances. More so, refrain from granting cash advances to officials and employees without specific purposes but salary deductions because the cash advances lack legal basis aside from the fact that the officials and employees concerned are not appointed or designated disbursing officers of the LGU.
Likewise, instruct the Budget Officer and Accountant to review thoroughly and meticulously the cash advances, making sure that those granted did not violate any of the provisions of the rules and regulations of cash advances.
Require the attachments of the pertinent documents before certifying the existence of appropriations and the validity of expenses.

Ensure the timely submission of the related cash advance disbursement vouchers and complete supporting documents, as required under Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

Refrain from granting honoraria without legal basis and follow the provisions set in Budget Circular No. 2007-2. Moreover, the Accountant is enjoined to meticulously check the legal basis and supporting documents of the claims of all honoraria before certifying the completeness, propriety and validity thereof.

Refrain from obligating and charging prior year’s unpaid transactions to the current year’s appropriations which is contrary to Section 350 of RA 7160.
Unpaid expenses that are already due and demandable during the year must be book up as Accounts Payable.
Likewise, the Budget Office and Accountant are advised to be strict in enforcing the provision.

Require the Municipal Engineer to accomplish the necessary Status of Work Accomplished for every infrastructure projects implemented by administration, which will be validated by the Municipality’s Inspectorate Committee and will be the basis for the issuance of the related Certificates of Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency.
Afterwards, submit the Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance for said infrastructure projects implemented by administration for review.

We recommended that Management:
a. Adhere to the requirement in the submission of financial accounts and reports to COA;
b. Instruct the concerned Accountant and Treasurer to agree on the dates of submission of the disbursement vouchers, Reports of Collections and Deposits with its supporting documents so that the Accountant can submit the financial reports on time; and
c. Further, Section 122 (2) of P.D. 1445 provides that failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.
Instruct the Local Waste Management Board to follow-up the approval of the ESWM 10-year plan and hasten its implementation to provide safe and sanitary management of solid waste.
Establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
Ensure the implementation of the planned activities and projects as incorporated in the LDRRMFIP to increase the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the LGU.

We recommended the following:
a. Ensure appropriate compliance with Section 272 of RA 7160 on the various PPAs that need to be included in the SEF plan.
b. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to be guided with the above observation in the succeeding preparation of budget.

We recommended that management require the concerned offices to perform the following:

Inventory Committee:

Complete the conduct of physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment.

Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE by type showing the balance of property and equipment per cards and per count and the shortage/overage, if any.

Accounting Office:

Reconcile the report of the inventory committee with the control accounts in the general ledger.

Maintain and update regularly the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC).

Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer:

1. Maintain and update regularly the Property/Equipment Cards for each class of fixed asset.

Require all implementing offices to submit Quarterly Reports on Government Projects, Programs and Activities from all sources to the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator who will then consolidate the reports for submission to municipal officials for monitoring and decision making purposes.

2.	Conduct regular periodic meetings with the heads of departments/offices to assess whether the implementation of PPAs is proceeding as planned and to ensure that implementation constraints, if any, are immediately resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) chart of accounts. Moreover, maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 45% Personal Services Limitation and to minimize the hiring of casual employees as to necessity.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management henceforth: a. observations.recommendation: Establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01; and coordinate with the GAD Focal Point to ascertain the alignment of the GAD Plan with the GAD Agenda and the correctness and alignment of the entries in each column of the GPB template, e.g. if the proposed activities respond to the identified gender issue or cause of the issue, the issues are correctly identified or formulated, if there are clear indicators and targets, if the proposed budget is realistic, if the number of proposed activities are doable within the year.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget were already prepared and submitted. The GAD focal person will establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics starting CY 2017.
BAC assured the preparation and submission of the report.
observations.reason: No report was prepared by the management.
Disbursement Vouchers were submitted late, thus reiterated in the CY 2016 AAR.
Accomplishment Report for CY 2016 disclosed that disbursement that are not related to or would address specific gender issues and concerns were made out of the fund.
Payment of loan still included in the 20% development budget and expenditure as the LGU cannot accommodate the payment for the loan  in its General Fund Proper budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to management that Collecting Officers turn over their
collections intact daily or not later than the next working day to the Liquidating
Officer pursuant to Section 31, Volume I of the NGAS Manual. Moreover,
install the ETRACS (Enhanced Tax Revenue Assessment and Collection System)
which is a free software available to local government units to computerize tax
and revenue assessments and collection. A portion of the Report of Collection
and Deposit generated by ETRACS segregates the cash from the non-cash
collections as shown in the picture below, hence facilitating review on whether
collections were remitted intact or not.

We recommend the management to strictly impose the withholding of
salary on employees with cash advances which are not liquidated within the
prescribed period and send demand letter to those that are no longer in active
government service.

We recommend the management to create a well-planned detailed
engineering plan and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all
projects to ensure completion of programmed projects as scheduled.

We recommend management to explain why it took the Local School Board
more than seven months to approve CY 2016 Budget. Henceforth, require that
the SEF budgets are prepared and approved timely and regularly in pursuance
to Section 7 of R. A. 5447.

We recommend that management explain why prior year’s expenditures and
capital outlays were charged to current appropriations. Otherwise, these are
subject to disallowance due to absence of appropriations. Henceforth,
management is required to stop the practice of charging prior year’s
expenditures to current appropriations.

We recommend the management to take advantage of the 10-day period
following each month to record, reconcile and improve the monitoring before
making the payment on time.

We recommend that management require the MDRRMO to closely
monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented.
Further, we recommend submission of required report on or before the 15th day
of each month as required in COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012
to facilitate timely review of the required LDRMM report.

We recommend that management hire two more capable staff to
complement the existing LDRRM Officer and a staff.

We recommend that management explain on the improper charging of the
wages of Job Order employees. Otherwise, the same could be subjected to
disallowance. Henceforth, management is requested to source from General
Fund the budget for personnel services in the LDRRM Office to carry on
functions related to disaster management.

We recommend management to require the details of Official Receipts be
entered in the Treasury Department. Moreover, require the ECPAC focal
person to link data from the Treasury Department to the Accounting
Department to reduce redundancy in data entry. Otherwise, install the
ETRACS (Enhanced Tax Revenue Assessment and Collection System) which is
free software available to local government units to computerize tax and revenue
assessments and collection pending migration of the accounting system from the
ECPAC to PPSAS in CY 2017.

We recommend that the management require the Treasury Department to
use the prescribed form of the RCD as instructed in the NGAS Manual, Volume
II. Moreover, install the ETRACS (Enhanced Tax Revenue Assessment and
Collection System) which is a free software available to local government units to
computerize tax and revenue assessments and collection.

We recommend the management to require the Municipal Accountant to
review these accounts, make adjusting entries if necessary and abide by
provisions of COA Circular 97-001.

We recommend the management to explain the overpayment. Otherwise,
the total amount of ₱36,493.00 is subject to disallowance. Henceforth, remit
only the amount that is withheld against employees’ salary and immediately
offset all the overpayments, if any in the next remittances.

We again reiterate our recommendation that the Local School Board limit the
utilization of the Special Education Fund to the programs and projects defined
under Section 1 of Republic Act No. 5447 and the priority listing provided in
Section 100 and Section 272 of Republic Act (RA) 7160 otherwise known as the
Local Government Code, and Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of DECS, DBM
and DILG, in order to maximize the benefits that the public school students could
avail under this fund.

We recommend that Management should comply with the provisions of Section 9 of
Republic Act No. 5447 on the timely remittance of the Special Education Fund.

We recommend that the municipal officials concerned adhere strictly to the
provisions of DILG – DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the
guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of 20% of the Annual Internal
Revenue Allotment (IRA) for development projects.

We recommend that the LDRRMO submit the LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report of
Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal
Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Henceforth, the
provisions of Par. 5.1.2 and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September
12, 2012 should be properly observed as well as the Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act 10121, otherwise known as the Philippine Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Act of 2010.

Recommendations: We recommended that Management make the necessary actions to facilitate the implementation of the projects so that the intended beneficiaries may enjoy the benefits, avoid the incurrence of penalty deductions and project termination.
We also recommended to the Management to coordinate with the offices concerned to comply with the documentary requirements to effect liquidation in both participating agencies and the LGU’s books.

Recommendations: We recommended that Management strategize the implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program which includes, but not limited to, the following:
From time to time, evaluate the performance of each member of the BAC and Technical Working Group.  The output of the Office shall be the basis in the computation of honorarium.
The SFP Focal Person should closely coordinate with the members of the BAC in order to fast track the procurement of menu ingredients according to the prescribed schedule of feeding program.
Refund the unused fund in the amount of P146,633.00 should the SFP Cycle 4 ended on December 31, 2016 pursuant to Item 2 (l) of the Memorandum of Agreement.

Recommendations: In view hereof, we recommended for the full implementation of the projects funded by the 20% Development Fund to speed up development and progress and eventually uplift the lives of the constituents of the Municipality.
It is further recommended to identify the target beneficiaries of the programs, projects and activities during the planning stage so that the funds intended for that purpose will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

Recommendations: In view hereof, we recommended that Management study the possibility of establishing a LDRRM Office with assigned Officer and staff pursuant to NDRRMC/ DILG/ DBM/ CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.  Also, the LDRRM Council should revisit the prior year’s MDRRM Plan and implement programs/projects not yet fully implemented.

Recommendations: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) shall coordinate public efforts with that of the private sectors in preserving and protecting the environment and public health as required under RA No. 9003.
We also recommended that the LCE shall encourage the full participation of the Barangays in the implementation of the law. In addition, require the segregation of wastes by every household.
Further, we recommended the maximum utilization of machines costing P3,500,000.00 funded by the DTI-Province which was launched last December 21, 2016. These machines are plastic melting machine, mechanical dryer, and crusher which will be used in melting recyclable wastes to form useful products.

Recommendations:  We recommended that expenses charged to SEF should conform to existing laws, rules and regulations governing the utilization of such fund, otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit.
Henceforth, evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.

Recommendations: We recommended that BAC adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and furnish the Auditor with copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and its supporting bidding documents within five (5) days after its perfection. Otherwise, we shall recommend that appropriate sanctions as provided for in Section 4.0 of the same Circular be imposed against those responsible Official/employees for non-compliance.
Also, BAC Secretariat should document the flow or movement of the contracts, bidding documents and purchase orders to establish accountability for delays in the submission to the Office of the Auditor.
Management ought to consider the needed manpower requirements and IT equipment of the office of the BAC to expedite the submission of contracts, bidding documents and purchase orders to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendations: We recommended that Management should enhance and strengthen the internal control system of the agency to safeguard assets from loss and misappropriation. Further, strictly follow the existing laws, rules and regulations prescribed in the settlement of obligations to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

We recommend discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and identify possible
sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This
landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria
mentioned in the said act.

We recommend that the management ensure that all copies of approved contracts,
purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted within five (5) working days
from approval or issuance and within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries for review,
evaluation and inspection of items procured by the Agency.

We recommend that the Municipal Health Office coordinate with the barangay
health centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the
government’s goal of providing preventive care services to indigents will be fully
implemented, and thus, best results could be achieved in the most opportune time.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare monthly Report on
Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund
(LDRRMF)and the LDRRMO Designate shall submit the said report to COA Auditor within
the prescribed period as required in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012–002.

We recommend that management conduct semestral physical count of inventory and
submit its report not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semesters, respectively. Moreover, require the Property Officer to record all receipts and
issuances of supplies by using the Stock Card pursuant to Section 44 of NGAS Manual for
LGUs, Volume I.

We recommend that Management intensify efforts in the establishment and
maintenance of a GAD database containing all sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics,
in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and to ensure that the
GAD Focal Person should undergo trainings related to GAD to better equip her on the task
at hand.

We recommend the following courses of action:
A. Direct the Municipal General Services Officer to locate documents pertinent
to the LGU properties preparatory to the conduct of the complete physical
count / inventory of all properties in accordance with existing regulations and
in order to establish the actual existence;
B. Submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property (IIRUP) to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the latter's
ledger cards;
C. Require the Accounting and GSO to maintain ledger and property cards as
control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records; and
D. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Government adopt a system of payroll payment
through the Automated Teller Machines.

We recommend that:
a. Management must secure local sanggunian authority/approval for all the
contracts/projects entered into/implemented by the local chief executive that
were described in generic terms in the Appropriation Ordinance/AIP for CY
2016 to ratify the same and avoid disallowances pursuant to G.R. No. 175527
and COA Memorandum 2010-014.
b. Henceforth, the Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and
Development Council should properly plan, prioritize and identify the specific
programs/projects to be funded under the 20% EDF and incorporate the same
in the AIP.

We recommend that Management:
a. Ensure completeness of the supporting documents of all contracts/purchase
orders submitted for review pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 dated Feb.
12, 2009.
b. Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts/purchase
orders and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required
documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the
completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.

1.8 We recommended the following:
a. Provide depreciation for the PPEs acquired in the CY 2016;
b. Submit the schedule of the Construction in Progress accounts; and
c. Hasten the completion of the RPCPPE so that reconciliation
with accounting records can be attained, and so that reliability
of the value of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position can
be ascertained.

2.8 Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct review and validation on
each of the journal entries made under Advances and Other
Receivables accounts to determine the correctness and propriety of
the subject entry and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, when
necessary.

3.5 Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
properly manage/monitor the bank accounts of the LGU to avoid
payment of bank charges. Incurrence of surcharges/penalties is
considered as a personal liability of person responsible therefore
pursuant to Section 105 (2) of PD 1445.
3.6 Further, close the dormant accounts and/or transfer its balances to
the General Fund accounts of the LGU.
3.8 Request the Treasurer to follow up the reply from the bank on the request
for waiver of bank charges, otherwise, he is directed to pay for the bank
charges while waiting for the reply.

4.10 We recommended the following:
a. Refrain from granting additional cash advances if the prior
cash advances were not yet settled; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the
erring accountable officers, including the retired/transferred
employees to immediately settle their outstanding cash
advances.

5.7 Refrain from granting cash advances to elected officials except for
travel and confidential fund only, making sure that prior advances
were already settled before granting additional cash advances. More
so, refrain from granting cash advances to officials and employees
without specific purposes but salary deductions because the cash
advances lack legal basis aside from the fact that the officials and
employees concerned are not appointed or designated disbursing
officers of the LGU.
5.8 Likewise, instruct the Budget Officer and Accountant to review
thoroughly and meticulously the cash advances, making sure that
those granted did not violate any of the provisions of the rules and
regulations of cash advances. Require the attachments of the
pertinent documents before certifying the existence of
appropriations and the validity of expenses.

6.5 Ensure the timely submission of the related cash advance
disbursement vouchers and complete supporting documents, as
required under Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-Governance
Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular
No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

7.9 Refrain from granting honoraria without legal basis and follow the
provisions set in Budget Circular No. 2007-2. Moreover, the
Accountant is enjoined to meticulously check the legal basis and
supporting documents of the claims of all honoraria before certifying
the completeness, propriety and validity thereof.

8.5 Refrain from obligating and charging prior year’s unpaid
transactions to the current year’s appropriations which is contrary to
Section 350 of RA 7160. Unpaid expenses that are already due and
demandable during the year must be book up as Accounts Payable.
8.6 Likewise, the Budget Office and Accountant are advised to be strict
in enforcing the provision.

9.9 Direct the BAC Chairman, members and all concerned to:
a. Consolidate all office supplies requisition for the month and
submit to the Bids and Awards Committee for the procurement
process;
b. Ensure compliance to the latest provisions of the IRR of RA 9184
so that price advantageous to the LGU can be obtained;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to meticulously review the
completeness and validity of the required supporting documents
for all related disbursements in accordance with the applicable
laws, rules and regulations.

10.5 Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness and
propriety of the required supporting documents for all
infrastructure projects implemented by administration before
payments thereof are effected.
10.6 Require the Municipal Engineer to accomplish the necessary Status
of Work Accomplished for every infrastructure projects
implemented by administration, which will be validated by the
Municipality’s Inspectorate Committee and will be the basis for the
issuance of the related Certificates of Completion and Acceptance by
the head of agency.
10.7 Afterwards, submit the Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection
Report, and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance for said
infrastructure projects implemented by administration for review.

11.8 We recommended that Management:
a. Adhere to the requirement in the submission of financial
accounts and reports to COA;
b. Instruct the concerned Accountant and Treasurer to agree on
the dates of submission of the disbursement vouchers, Reports
of Collections and Deposits with its supporting documents so
that the Accountant can submit the financial reports on time;
and
c. Further, Section 122 (2) of P.D. 1445 provides that failure on the
part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and
reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the
suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have
complied with the requirements of the Commission.

12.7 Instruct the Local Waste Management Board to follow-up the
approval of the ESWM 10-year plan and hasten its implementation
to provide safe and sanitary management of solid waste.

13.6 Establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-
disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the
PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

1.7	We recommended that management require the concerned offices to perform the following:
Inventory Committee:
Complete the conduct of physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment.
Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE by type showing the balance of property and equipment per cards and per count and the shortage/overage, if any.
Accounting Office:
Reconcile the report of the inventory committee with the control accounts in the general ledger.
Maintain and update regularly the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC).
Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer:
Maintain and update regularly the Property/Equipment Cards for each class of fixed asset.

2.6	We recommended that management:
Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 2 P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) which is one of the documents supporting fund transfer; and
Require the leagues and/or organizations to submit their liquidation reports on the amounts transferred to them and to record all fund transfers as receivables to monitor the utilization of the funds.
3.6	We recommended that management submit the legal and corresponding guidelines for the granting of incentive. Otherwise, the granting of the incentive should be stopped immediately.
4.7	We recommended that management strictly comply with the minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dump in accordance with Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003.

5.8	We recommended that management strictly comply with the standards and requirements for collection and transport of solid waste pursuant to Section 24 of RA No. 9003 to avoid endangering the lives of its people through health hazards caused by uncovered solid wastes.
6.5	We recommended that management stop immediately the practice of spending government funds for religious or private purposes being violative of Section 335 of Republic Act No. 7160.

7.7	We recommended that management direct the MDRRM Officer to formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRM Plan which will be used as the basis in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the PPAs are in accordance with the national, regional and provincial frameworks.

8.5	We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of Section 7.2.1a of the COA Circular No. 2009-006 by submitting the disbursement vouchers and financial reports within the time frame to enable the Auditor to conduct timely audit and review of the transaction of the LGU.
9.6	We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the provisions set forth in Section 52 of RA No. 10717 or the General Appropriation Act of 2016.
We recommend management to return the unexpended balances of PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury and secure official receipt thereof.
