observations.recommendation: We recommend management that the liquidating officer immediately turnover all collections received by her to the Municipal Treasurer for review, validation of the official receipts to ascertain the accuracy and completeness of the amount received for deposit in compliance with Section 31 (c) of NGAS Manual Volume I.
We recommend management to avail of the remedies by administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action. Steps are enumerated in section 254 to 270 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Notices of Delinquency
observations.reason: Balances from previous years were collected. However, receivables for the current year are still not fully collected.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to implement within the year any plans/projects that can help improve the lives of the calamity victims; otherwise, return the money as instructed in COA Circular 2014-002.
observations.managementAction: The projects implemented.
observations.reason: Final management action: Petty management implemented. Refundable balances of ₱1,205 to bukidnon.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that to be able to initiate the development and maintenance of a GAD database, management may require it from the members of the GAD technical working group as one of the key performance indicators for their performance appraisal and incentives who will be closely monitored but be provided with necessary assistance.
We recommend management to execute a deed of consignment and collect the 20% commission fee retroactively starting on the day the sole souvenir shop started its lease and operation.
observations.managementAction: The bidding among the prospective lessors will be scheduled; a deed of consignment and ordinance are being formulated.
observations.reason: The bidding among the prospective lessors will be scheduled this 2017; the management implemented some actions but the deed/ordinance are still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly the balances of liabilities due to government agencies/corporations concerned in order not to compromise the benefits/due the intended beneficiaries and at the same time avoid penalties/surcharges arising from late payments. Henceforth, regularly remit all trust liabilities of like nature on the required due dates.
observations.managementAction: The management has reconciled.
observations.reason: There are still inherited balances from former accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that a room with a lock and key may be used as stock room for goods/supplies for sale separate from that of the office of the Stock Clerk who maintains the ledger/records of the supplies/goods for sale. Moreover, the Purchaser may only be the one who is tasked to access the goods/supplies for sale and to control the lock and key of the stockroom so that in instances of loss, accountability can be established. Further, require that the Purchaser, Stock Clerk and a representative from the Accounting Office to regularly conduct physical count of the goods/supplies for sale to reconcile their balances and report accurate figures in the financial statements. Finally, in the procurement of goods for sale, public bidding may be conducted and not on a reimbursement basis.
observations.managementAction: rehabilitation of the chocolate hills facilities is on-going; improvements and stockroom plans being implemented.
observations.reason: Improvements on the chocolate hills facilities are on-going; management has started to dispose some unserviceable property with proceeds of ₱280,000.00; remaining to be inspected by COA and auctioned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prospectively monitor every delivery of materials/items in each stage of project work item implementation so that the actual output thereof would be in accordance with the approved plans and specifications, thus, value for money is maximized and audit disallowances would be avoided in the future. Correspondingly, we recommend that the amount of ₱67,436.00 be deducted from the final payment yet to be made to the contractor, in order to effect the deficiencies noted in the conduct of inspection.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the recommendation. Final payment is yet to be made; the supplier has expressed final intention to appeal.
observations.reason: Final payment is yet to be made. The supplier has expressed intention to appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prospectively monitor each stage of the project work item implementation so that the actual output thereof would be in accordance with the approved plans and specifications, thus, value for money is maximized and audit disallowances would be avoided in the future. Correspondingly, we likewise recommend that the amount of ₱33,220.72 be deducted from the final payment yet to be made to the contractor, in order to effect the deficiencies noted in the conduct of inspection.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the recommendation. Final payment is yet to be made.
observations.reason: Final payment is yet to be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management deduct the liquidated damages of ₱89,765.24 from the final payment to the contractor in order to effect the deficiencies noted during the inspection. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management ensure the proper implementation and timely execution of the terms of the approved project contract so that the target completion date will be attained which leads to the opportune availment of the constituents of the benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the recommendation. Final payment is yet to be made; supplier may appeal.
observations.reason: Final payment is yet to be made. The supplier indicated intention to appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management retrace the entries made, perform reconciliation and make necessary adjusting entries to close the total amount of ₱1,605,496.22 to the appropriate account.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant traced newer entries (approx. ₱.3M) and was able to close these; found difficulty to trace older accumulations (approx. ₱1.5M). Suggested to close the account to 'prior years' to clean up.
observations.reason: The accountant closed newer entries but could not fully trace/close older accumulations; suggested reclassification to prior years to clean up.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management retrace the entries made and review the source documents which brought the Cash in Vault to a negative balance. Check if there were unrecorded cash receipts/inflows used to fund the overspending. Validate as well the related disbursements which caused the overdraft if these were valid. Finally, prepare adjusting entries when necessary.
observations.managementAction: The accountant expressed his difficulty in locating the missing disbursement vouchers. The amount was presented as evidence; they are hesitant to move it from one account to another because the related documents were submitted in court. The accountant will reclassify the amount as a negative item in the receivable from the former treasurer.
observations.reason: The amount is under litigation; documents submitted to court; management hesitant to offset until final decision.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of P100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007. Henceforth, fund transfers to NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts of the LGU in accordance with the same COA Circular in order to afford proper review and evaluation of the utilization of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: The management has studied the concern and found that Carmen Health District Inc is not an NGO/PO but a GOCC since it was incorporated by representatives of 10 LGUs and 1 Province. The memorandum of agreement among members exists; management cannot discontinue the contribution as it would breach the MOA.
observations.reason: The contribution is pursuant to MOA among member LGUs; treating CHD as GOCC changes disposition; management cannot unilaterally discontinue contributions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately reinstall the post that blocked the entrance of the Complex. Require that the Cashier’s Form be serially pre-numbered and issued at the entrance to visitors/tourists who opt to be issued an official receipt instead of the cash tickets. Also, require the Toll Gate Checker to see to it that before visitors/tourists exit the Complex, they can present the cash tickets or the Cashier’s Form duly signed by the Cashier as to OR number and amount paid indicating that the entrance fee has been duly paid for. Lastly, install safety measures in the booth where the Toll Gate Collectors are stationed such as providing for a cash vault, window grills and a security officer.
observations.managementAction: The management has deployed tourist policemen to improve security; phases of projects for improvement of the chocolate hills facilities are being implemented.
observations.reason: Phases of projects are ongoing; improvements and security measures being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately undertake a Deed of Consignment between the Municipality of Carmen and the sole souvenir shop so that collection of commission and other related fees may be effected retroactively.
observations.managementAction: The bidding among the prospective lessors will be scheduled; deed of consignment and ordinance are being formulated.
observations.reason: Bidding and deed/ordinance in process; actions partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management take proper action on government properties that are for disposal. The General Services Officer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445. In the meantime, the Municipal Accountant should reclassify other unserviceable properties to Other Assets account while awaiting the disposal of such assets amounting to ₱3,949,410.61.
observations.managementAction: Management has started to dispose properties and has disposed of some unserviceable properties with proceeds of ₱280,000.00. The remaining was already inspected by COA which is bound for auction.
observations.reason: Some unserviceable properties disposed; remainder to be auctioned after COA inspection.

observations.recommendation: 1. Verify the Memorandum of Understanding or Memorandum of Agreement entered into with the donor-agencies to ascertain the disposition/treatment of the balances of the financial assistance received. 2. Transfer the unexpended balances in accordance with Sections 98 and 99 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Finding traced to petty balances even before previous administrations; the Municipal Accountant traced accounts back as far as 1980s.
observations.reason: Accounts traced back to earlier years; MOAs not readily found; still being resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that proper sanctions as stipulated in the contracts, like liquidated damages, blacklisting of poor or non-performing contractors, and others, be strictly imposed upon defaulting contractors. We also recommend to the Municipal Engineer’s Office to validate the actual accomplishment of the Construction In Progress projects.
observations.managementAction: Some projects were implemented by the current administration; other projects were not implemented because they were impractical e.g. swimming pool. The 1st tranche requirements were not met for the second tranche to be released.
observations.reason: Some projects implemented; others not due to impracticality and unmet tranche requirements.

observations.recommendation: 1. Submit an explanation for the very delayed implementation of the projects resulting to the incurrence of interest expense of ₱2,396,553.52 and financial charges amounting to ₱305,225.90 to the disadvantage of the government. 2. Conduct a substantive study on how to maximize the loan usage and hasten the project implementation.
observations.managementAction: Some projects were implemented by current administration; other projects were not implemented because they were impractical e.g. swimming pool. Management suggests prioritizing income generating projects.
observations.reason: Some projects implemented; others not implemented due to impracticality and prioritization changes.

observations.recommendation: 1. Require the municipal engineer to make a study on how to rehabilitate and improve the waterworks system. 2. Appropriate funds for the rehabilitation/improvement of the quality of water distributed by the waterworks system and eventually implement new water rates equitable enough for the quality of potable water supply distributed.
observations.managementAction: Management purchased a lot which is a good source for potable water.
observations.reason: To be implemented in ensuing year; lot purchased as first step.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to demand immediate liquidation from the accountable officials and employees concerned and if not liquidated, to effect withholding of salary and other sanctions as provided under PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Final demand letters were sent; prior years outstanding cash advances of accountable officials and employees still active in service have been fully liquidated as of January 2013.
observations.reason: Demand letters were sent; some prior year outstanding cash advances were liquidated, but some older items remain to be resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer refrain from processing the payment for salaries and wages without the accomplishment reports prepared by the job order concerned to facilitate the review of the claims pursuant to Section 2 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management see to it that all disbursements for procurement are supported with complete, properly filled –up, duly signed and dated documentation as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We also recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee/BAC Secretariat see to it that all documents in any stage of the procurement process are properly filled-up and dated pursuant to the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that all concerned Accountable Officers be required to immediately liquidate their cash advances whose purpose had already been served. Strictly adhere to the aforementioned provisions of Sections 89 of Presidential Decree No. 14445 and provisions of Circular No. 97-002 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: To require those with existing cash advances to submit liquidation report to the accounting office.
observations.reason: Demand letters were sent to those with existing cash advances while the liquidations for the intelligence fund were sent to the COA Central Office, however no credit advices were received from COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the amount in excess of P5,000.00 of PEI received, otherwise all those who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable. Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of EO No. 80 as implemented by Budget circular No. 2013-3 and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: The excess of P5,000.00 was already booked-up and the installment deductions were made thru the payroll.
observations.reason: A letter was sent to management regarding payment, however no reply was received.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the noted deficiencies be corrected and the required documents submitted immediately for validation. Thereafter, all the officers involved in the processing of the claims should see to it that the supporting papers are complete and proper, before these are paid or else these would be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Will coordinate with the OIC Accountant for compliance of the deficiencies noted.
observations.reason: The OIC-Accountant failed to review the remaining balance of 22% because her transfer to the Budget Office was unexpected.

observations.recommendation: Concerned municipal official and employees are required to refund the amount of P22, 000 each or a total of P1,936,000.00 and management should limit the granting of additional benefits for personal services within the 45% level for a third class municipality as provided by law.
observations.managementAction: To request all officials/employees to refund the amount thru payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Continued the monthly deduction except those who retired/separated from the service

observations.recommendation: Concerned officials should be required to refund the excess payment of their RATA. Furthermore, the management should refrain from implementing the new RATA rates until such time that the PS cap/ limitation shall have been observed.
observations.managementAction: To request officials/employees to refund the amount thru payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Monthly deduction was made except those who retired/separated from the service.

observations.recommendation: Require all the municipal employees to refund P20, 625.00 each, or a total of P1,842, 270.91.
observations.managementAction: To request all officials/employees to refund the amount thru payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Continued the monthly deduction except those who retired/separated from the service.

observations.recommendation: Management should prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report at the end of each semester to be approved by the Local Chief Executive.  Thereafter, submit the duly approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester as prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management said that the Bids and Awards Committee thru its Secretariat has submitted the Project Report covering the 1st and 2nd Quarters of CY 2015 on November 29, 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned offices comply within the prescribed period the submission of the above-mentioned reports/records.
observations.managementAction: Management said that responsible offices were able to prepare all the required reports and records. Some of the above-mentioned reports like Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels and Pre-repair evaluation/inspection were complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit justifications regarding the discrepancies found in the preparation of the Program-of-Works.  Otherwise, the Contractor should refund the overpayment of P442,090.28.
observations.managementAction: Defects were already addressed and given remedy.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management’s strict compliance to the provisions set forth in the Memorandum of Agreement to realize its objective in alleviating poverty in the locality.
observations.managementAction:
Management admitted their failure to transfer their counterpart to Trust Fund – DOLE for the cited program.

They were able to grant its commitment to support the Balutan Project by providing a fund under General Fund which was indirectly considered as their counterpart.

observations.recommendation:
Submit legal basis for the granting of honoraria to the Bids and Awards Committee taken from the General Fund of the Municipality.

Proceeds from the sale of bid documents and other income earned from the conduct of public bidding should be adequately monitored and properly recorded as trust receipts in accordance with the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A. As a control measure, the Municipal Treasurer’s Office should devise a system to ensure that all collections are properly receipted as trust receipts and deposited to the Trust Fund account of the Municipality.

Payrolls for honoraria involving procurement projects be supported with the related BAC Resolution showing the mode of procurement used, list of completed procurement projects and contracts entered into for the period or month of claim that will support the propriety, validity and correctness of claims.

The Accounting Office should require the BAC Secretariat to submit the required documents to support the claims for honoraria and to come up with a computation of the correct amount of honoraria for CY 2015. Thereafter, management should require refund of overpayment of honoraria from the employees concerned, as the case may be.

The Accountant should deduct withholding taxes from payment of honoraria in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table and the same shall be remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period. Subsequent to the refund of overpayment of honoraria, the amount of withholding tax not deducted should be computed for eventual deduction from their salaries and other claims, toforestall disallowance in audit.

The local government should create an Internal Control Unit that is responsible for formulating rules and regulations in order to ensure that the government resources are effectively and efficiently used. This would also help the local government to present reliable and accurate financial statements that would be useful for an effective decision-making.

As part of the fiscal responsibility of the Local Chief Executive, install an effective monitoring control on a monthly basis, the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should install controls necessary for the deductions to be effected in the payroll to lessen the incidence of non-issuance of official receipts.

We recommended that the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised IRR be strictly observed including, but not limited to, the creation of Technical Working Group in consonance with the government’s commitment to promote good governance.

Management should request for another investigating body preferably from the Police Regional Office 11, Camp Catitipan, Davao City for further investigation of the incident and the watchmen who were reportedly on duty on the night of Sunday, June 7, 2015.  If warranted, erring watchmen including their immediate supervisor should be held responsible.

CCTVs should be installed in the Municipal Building particularly the hallway leading to the Office of the Treasurer.

Stop designating Job Orders as collecting officers otherwise, the responsible official doing so should be held liable for the loss of collections for whatever reasons as they are prohibited by laws, rules and regulations to function as collecting officers.

Management should observe the period of submission of disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents relative to the cash advance for confidential fund.  Also, limit the amount of cash advance for three (3) months requirements pursuant to 6.1.5 of COA/DBM/DILG/GCG/DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

The Municipal Accountant should draw adjusting and/or reclassification entries of the above noted deficiencies taking into consideration the description and/or definition of each account as well as its purpose and objectives.

Dep-Ed officials shall reconcile their records with the records of the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer. Once reconciled, said officials together with the representative of the Office of the Municipal Mayor shall conduct actual physical count to determine its existence. Once determined as to its existence, LGU-Carmen shall transfer the PPEs used by Dep-Ed in order that accountability of the items be established.  The Accountant shall prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to transfer of SEF-properties to Dep-Ed Province of Davao del Norte for the account of Dep-Ed - Carmen and to the respective national high schools with complete set of books of accounts as end-users pursuant to generally accepted accounting and auditing rules and regulations and pertinent provisions of P.D.1445.
observations.reason: For LSB Resolution – Transfer to DepEd.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant not to process the claims against the Municipality without complete documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: AOM
observations.reason: Moreover, the management adhere strictly to the provision of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A that payment of honoraria is based from maximum honorarium per successfully completed procurement projects and a procurement project shall be considered successfully completed once the contract has been awarded to the winning bidder.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal's Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: AOM
observations.reason: The BAC has not submitted the report in the GPPB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local chief executive resort to NFA as primary supplier or source of rice in all its procurements whenever it grants rice as a form of incentive or benefit to employees or use rice in connection with their functions. Forge with the NFA an appropriate Memorandum of Agreement to facilitate the rice procurement arrangement.
observations.managementAction: AOM

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall remit promptly and intact all taxes withheld by the Municipality to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in compliance with the BIR regulations.
observations.managementAction: AOM
observations.reason: Some taxes withheld are not remitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Local Waste Management Board to fast-track the completion of a Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan, in accordance with the Republic Act 9003 and such other applicable laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AOM

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management discontinue the practice of allowing deductions on monthly payroll other than those mandated by law. These personnel who contracted loans from these lending establishments must directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors.
observations.managementAction: AOM

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local chief executive secure copies of PNDF Volume 1, current edition, and to direct the Municipal Health Officer and other requisitioning officers to refer as basis for procurements of drugs the PNDF and thereby, supplement certification for each request the purchase thereof.
observations.managementAction: AOM

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD focal person of the Municipality be directed to submit the GAD Plan and reports to the DILG and to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to the above-cited regulations and ensure that at least five percent (5%) of its annual budget were indeed appropriated budget for GAD programs/projects and utilized for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: AOM

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial operation as of a given period of time.
observations.managementAction: AOM

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should review, analyze, validate and reconcile the dormant accounts with the related accounts in the financial statements. If the analysis/review of the accounts is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, the head of the agency should seek authority from the members of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan (SP) to write off the doubtful accounts. Once the legislative body approves, submit the listings of the doubtful accounts together with the SP Resolution to the COA and request authority for its writing-off supported by a list of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts stating the reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial statements/schedules and supporting vouchers/documents cannot be located.
observations.managementAction: AOM
observations.reason: The Loans-Receivable AOM account is still presented in financial statements.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to further impose stricter control in the grants and liquidations of cash advances to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002. Also, determine the status of employment of the accountable officers with long outstanding advances and if they are still employed in the Municipality, issue an order of payment, and the failure to liquidate the said cash advances shall be a valid ground for the withholding of his/her salary and other sanctions, as warranted. For those accountable officers who are no longer connected to the Municipality, issue a demand letter requiring them to liquidate all long outstanding advances.
observations.managementAction: Demand letter was issued.

observations.recommendation: Ensure appropriate compliance with COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the various activities that needs to be included in the Peace and Order Plan.
observations.managementAction: Authority to travel already submitted and received by the Provincial Government and IT was prepared.

PPELC already maintained by the Accounting Unit, respectively and failure to conduct the required annual physical count of PPE and render the required Report on the Physical Count of PPE, as provided under the Manual of the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units and COA Circular 92-386 to establish the existence of the said PPE items cast doubts on the reliability and validity of Property Plant and Equipment (PPE) Accounts as of December 31, 2015 amounting to ₱97,963,064.16.

observations.recommendation: Submit the lacking required documents on various claims of traveling expenses per COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, as enumerated in Part II, Finding No. 6, and the related Liquidation Reports and its supporting documents within the prescribed period. Also, ensure strict compliance on the laws, rules and regulations on the grant and liquidation of cash advances for traveling expenses to avoid possible misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Travel authority was submitted and IT was prepared.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Engineer to accomplish the necessary Status of Work Accomplished for every infrastructure projects implemented by administration, which will be validated by the Municipality’s Inspectorate Committee and will be the basis for the issuance of the related Certificates of Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency. Afterwards, submit to COA the Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance for said infrastructure projects implemented by administration.
observations.managementAction: SWA was already prepared for some projects.

observations.recommendation: Ensure timely submission of the related disbursement vouchers and complete supporting documents to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader, as required under Section 6.3.1 (f) of COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.
observations.managementAction: Some were submitted early but mostly delayed.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Supply Officer to properly accomplish the required Purchase Order in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996, and employ means to ensure timely submission to COA of the POs or any contract entered into by the Municipality, consistent with COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Some were submitted early but mostly delayed.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal DRRM Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to ensure timely preparation and submission of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, in compliance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and the Municipal Accountant to submit the related disbursement vouchers, together with the required Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for timely review and validation.
observations.managementAction: Reports were already submitted on a monthly basis to COA for the calendar year 2015.

observations.recommendation: Submit the Sanggunian Bayan Resolution approving the utilization of the MDRRMF – Quick Response Fund and the declaration of state of calamity by the appropriate government offices, as required under Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and ensure strict compliance with the subject provisions for future disbursements charged against the MDRRMF – QRF.
observations.managementAction: Account code used in recording was incorrect, however, adjustment was already made.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that the Special Education Fund shall be utilized solely for its purpose; i.e., programs, projects and activities enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: Require the GAD Focal Point of the Municipality to submit the agency’s GAD Plan to PCW and DBM for review and approval to ensure that the GAD programs/projects/activities therein address gender issues, in accordance with the existing regulations and guidelines on the preparation of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget were already prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: Immediately implement the said projects for an efficient, effective and economical utilization of government funds and timely attainment of project objectives to intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Fund was already utilized for its purpose.

observations.recommendation: Ensure prompt implementation of planned developmental programs/projects/activities of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
